races.race_name: Tally Ho Stud Irish EBF (C & G) Maiden

runners.name: Sergei Diaghilev
runners.spotlight: Wootton Bassett colt; second foal; brother to Group-placed 1m1f 2yo winner Endorsement (RPR 107), also Group-placed over 1m2f at 3 in 2026; dam unraced sister to winners Johannes Vermeer (7f 2yo Group 1; runner-up in Melbourne Cup) and Elizabeth Browning (1m1f Group 2); trip looks on the short side on his breeding but Ballydoyle first-choice on jockey bookings.
runners.comment: Brother to Group-placed 1m1f 2yo winner Endorsement; Moore's pick
runners.owner: Westerberg & Mrs Magnier & M Tabor & D Smith

runners.name: Winnie's Wish
runners.spotlight: Coulsty colt a 10,000euros foal and 35,000euros yearling; brother to 7f 2yo winner Madbadanddangerous (RPR 86), half-brother to winners Marvelosa (10.4f; 84) and Mulsanne (US 1m turf); dam unraced sister to 1m4f Listed winner, closely related to Gold Cup winner Courage Mon Ami; debutant respected from a yard with a decent recent 2yo winner.
runners.comment: Debutant respected from a yard with a decent recent 2yo winner

runners.name: Bold Commander
runners.spotlight: No Nay Never brother to 5f/6f 2yo winner First Approach (RPR 89), half-brother to winners Lisboa (1m2f; 84) and Sound It Out (Australian 7f-1m); dam Group-placed 6f winner (109), closely related to useful 7f/1m winner Aerion Power; seemingly Ballydoyle second-choice but don't rule out.
runners.comment: Brother to 5f/6f 2yo winner First Approach
runners.owner: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor

runners.name: South Dakota
runners.spotlight: St Mark's Basilica half-brother to winners High Definition (1m 2yo inc Group 2, RPR 120), Innisfree (1m Group 3/7f 2yo; 110) and Royal Aide (1m3f/11.7f Flat, 86; 3m/3m1f hurdle); dam 1m and 8.4f (Listed) winner (104); seemingly the Ballydoyle third-choice but worth a look for future reference.
runners.comment: Half-brother to 2yo Group 2 winner High Definition
runners.owner: M Tabor & D Smith & Mrs J Magnier & Westerberg

runners.name: Folsom Blues
runners.spotlight: Blue Point colt is closely related to 7f AW 2yo winner Greek Mythology (RPR 99), half-brother to 7f/1m winner Atlantic Coast (inc 2yo Group 3/Australia; 104); dam Group-placed 9.5f winner (95), half-sister to useful 1m2f winner Groundbreaking; yard in top form so newcomer has to be respected.
runners.comment: Blue Point colt related to some decent 2yos; yard in top form so has to be respected

runners.name: I Ready
runners.spotlight: Invincible Army gelding; 10,000 yearling; second foal; dam runner-up 6f 2yo (RPR 74), half-sister to winners Insignia Of Rank (1m Listed) and Mayleaf Shine (useful 5f inc 2yo); speedily bred sort but this likely a strong maiden.
runners.comment: Speedily bred sort but this likely a strong maiden

runners.name: Switching Sides
runners.spotlight: Kodiac colt bought back for 52,000euros as a yearling; dam placed 1m3f/1m4f (RPR 76), half-sister to 6f 2yo Group 2 winner Besharah; needs checking in the market on debut.
runners.comment: Kodiac colt, out of a Galileo half-sister to 6f 2yo Group 2 winner Besharah

runners.name: Dhikola
runners.spotlight: Ardad colt cost 45,000GBP as a yearling; third foal; closely related to Spanish 1m winner Running Hot, half-brother to 6f turf 2yo/1m AW winner Priapos (RPR 84); dam 5.7f winner (76), half-sister to winners Global Applause (5f Listed inc 2yo) and Auditor (useful 5f-7f inc 2yo); never recovered from bad start on recent Navan debut; best watched for now.
runners.comment: Never recovered from bad start on recent Navan debut; best watched for now
runners.owner: Walerc Limited/M J Wasylocha/Tony Cantwell/Paul J Nolan

races.race_name: Sherry FitzGerald Country Homes Orby Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: Emit
runners.spotlight: Consistent last season, going close in 1m2f Group 3 Gallinule Stakes on this weekend and later in year twice runner-up in Longchamp Group contests over 1m7f; both runs this term at 1m6f, latest when respectable third to Scandinavia in Navan Listed; possible fitness edge on some of these.
runners.comment: Possible fitness edge on some of these but ideally wants further at this stage

runners.name: Trustyourinstinct
runners.spotlight: Good form early last season, recording back-to-back wins here in today's event and Group 3 International Stakes over 1m2f; subsequent Leopardstown efforts were below best below best, as was last month's seasonal return here in 1m2f Group 3 Alleged Stakes dominated by two stablemates; entitled to come on for that.
runners.comment: Last year's winner well below best on recent return; entitled to improve for that

runners.name: Purview
runners.spotlight: Smart, lightly raced 4yo; never quite fulfilled Leopardstown's Derby Trial second this time last year although did end the campaign on a winning note in 1m2f Cork Listed in September in ready fashion; looks the type to make an even better 4yo upped to this trip; very interesting player.
runners.comment: Smart last year and promises to make an even better 4yo upped to this trip; big player

runners.name: Nyra
runners.spotlight: German import, having been bought for 875,000euros at Arqana in December; 1-9 for previous yard, winning Group 3 in Hamburg over 1m3f (good) and better form when beaten a neck in Group 2 at Baden-Baden and final start when third in 1m4f Group 1 Gran Premio del Jockey Club in Milan; McMonagle's choice of Joseph O'Brien's quartet so has to be respected.
runners.comment: Ex-German trained filly; Group 1-placed in Italy; choice of Joseph O'Brien's four

runners.name: Snapretend
runners.spotlight: Very useful filly has returned this term with 0.5l defeats by Moody at Listed level at both Cork (1m4f) and Gowran (1m6f), each time forcing it; this a much deeper race than those however so will do well to sneak a place.
runners.comment: Two narrow defeats at this level recently; this looks a much deeper race though

runners.name: Lady Lilac
runners.spotlight: Fairly useful handicapper over shorter trips; big jump in trip here and out of her depth.
runners.comment: Fairly useful handicapper over shorter trips; big jump in trip here and out of her depth

runners.name: Arouet
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced last year at 3, winning minor Roscommon contest and chased home a very smart type in Leopardstown Listed over this trip; recently landed odds in Cork 4yo maiden hurdle, an unusual prep for one from this yard; cheekpieces fitted for Flat return and bit to find on ratings.
runners.comment: Recent maiden hurdle winner; cheekpieces fitted for Flat return and bit to find on ratings

runners.name: Puppet Master
runners.spotlight: Ex Ballydoyle-trained; won Lingfield Derby Trial last year but well held in both Irish Derby and Irish St Leger; bought for 78,000gns and soundly beaten on yard debut in Grade 2 Leopardstown hurdle at Christmas; tried in a tongue-tie here and plenty on his plate in what looks a hot Listed race.
runners.comment: Last year's Lingfield Derby Trial winner didn't progress;  tried in tongue-tie

runners.name: Chally Chute
runners.spotlight: In the form of his life last year, winning 1m4f Roscommon Listed on seasonal debut and better again upped to 2m at this venue, unlucky narrow defeat in Irish Cesarewitch and ready winner of Group 3 Loughbrown Stakes in November on heavy; ideally wants it softer and this looks a tough enough task on seasonal return but has to be respected.
runners.comment: Better than ever last year; runs well fresh but ideally wants it softer

runners.name: Cristal Clere
runners.spotlight: Third career win came on final start last season in 1m Listowel Listed in testing conditions under a positive ride (just a day after being well beaten in handicap); balance of form is over shorter than this so others preferred on seasonal return.
runners.comment: Absent since 1m Listed win in September; this trip stretches him

races.race_name: Keadeen Hotel Handicap (Premier Handicap)

runners.name: Flame Of Forest
runners.spotlight: Fair form at 7f/1m in Britain; appeared not to stay this trip at Newcastle in January; tough task against that background.
runners.comment: Fair form at 7f/1m in Britain, appeared not to stay this trip at Newcastle in January

runners.name: Railwayview Lady
runners.spotlight: Sprang 125-1 shock in a 1m2f Navan maiden last September, proved that was no fluke later in the month when going down narrowly on handicap debut over 1m5f at Down Royal; progress not maintained in two subsequent outings; cheekpieces on now.
runners.comment: Struck form last autumn, progress not maintained in two subsequent outings, cheekpieces on

runners.name: Yulia
runners.spotlight: Two wins in France (on soft and AW); has won over 1m7f, and had never raced at under 1m4f until recent Irish debut at Sligo; unplaced favourite after starting slowly and meeting trouble in running; trip is a concern, but unwise to discount.
runners.comment: French import who stays considerably further than this, no luck in running on Irish debut

runners.name: Pillar Of Hope
runners.spotlight: Five turf wins include a 1m4f course success during a solid 2025 campaign; below his best in two outings this term; mid-field behind today's Perry Mason on the first occasion.
runners.comment: Course 1m4f winner during a solid 2025 campaign, below his best in two outings this term

runners.name: Fixation
runners.spotlight: Won over the extended version of this trip at Dundalk last November on stable debut; should be suited by this return to a longer trip after two seconds over 1m; good claimer aboard.
runners.comment: Should be suited by this return to a longer trip after two seconds over 1m, good claimer

runners.name: Dawn Rising
runners.spotlight: No wins last season but strong form in defeat in big staying handicaps; fourth in the amateur handicap at Galway, fifth in the Irish Cesarewitch and signed off with a fine second in the Cesarewitch; runs over an inadequate trip here on second start of the season.
runners.comment: Group 2 course winner at 2m, fine second in last year's Cesarewitch, inadequate trip here

runners.name: Perfect Your Craft
runners.spotlight: Winner of three of her six races for Ralph Beckett; clear chance on best form, though issue complicated by the fact that she is one of three runners for a stable in superb form.
runners.comment: Winner of three of her six races for Ralph Beckett, obvious chance with yard in super form

runners.name: In My Teens
runners.spotlight: Consistent last season; ended with C&D second behind today's rival Retracement on handicap debut; jumping let her down in juvenile hurdles; sharpened for return to the Flat by a handicap run at the Punchestown festival.
runners.comment: Consistent last season, ended with C&D second, sharpened by a hurdles run at Punchestown

runners.name: Iron Fist
runners.spotlight: Two soft ground wins last term; found 1m inadequate here on seasonal debut; much better when second at Cork, with Apercu back in fourth; could be in the mix.
runners.comment: Found 1m inadequate here on seasonal debut. much better at Cork, could be in the mix

runners.name: Perry Mason
runners.spotlight: Has shown a liking for this track, gained a second 1m success here in March; suited by soft ground; holding his form well on the evidence of Cork third; would benefit from any easing.
runners.comment: Has shown a liking for this track, suited by soft ground, would benefit from any easing

runners.name: Fiver Friday
runners.spotlight: Now 11lb higher than for C&D win in March, an emphatic success gained at the main expense of the Irish Lincoln winner Ribee; has maintained progress with a wide-margin win in a handicap hurdle at Bellewstown; strong contender.
runners.comment: Now 11lb higher than for C&D win in March, has maintained progress with a win over hurdles

runners.name: Playin Cool
runners.spotlight: Ended last season with a 1m1f maiden win on heavy ground at Leopardstown; may step up from his 1m run here in March; needs to reverse form with Perry Mason; finished in front of Calm The Jets and Iron Fist.
runners.comment: Ended last season with a 1m1f maiden win, may step up from his 1m run here in March

runners.name: Calm The Jets
runners.spotlight: Landed a 1m maiden at Dundalk on debut early last year; did not appear last turf season; fair run over 1m here on turf debut; needs to settle better than at Cork a month ago.
runners.comment: 1m AW winner, fair run over 1m here on turf debut, needs to settle better than at Cork

runners.name: War Rooms
runners.spotlight: Third in the 2024 Dante and generally kept good company on the Flat in Britain; started brightly in Ireland last spring; has become disappointing; plenty to prove at this stage.
runners.comment: Third place in 2024 Dante is becoming a distant memory, plenty to prove at this stage

runners.name: Dar Toungi
runners.spotlight: No hint so far of the form that brought him eight wins in France, including a Listed race over this trip two seasons ago; has beaten only one opponent in each of his two Irish starts; headgear tried now.
runners.comment: No hint so far of the form that brought him eight wins in France, headgear tried now

runners.name: Cloud Seeker
runners.spotlight: Five-time winner, beaten a short head by Apercu in this race last season; back from a spell in Bahrain; generally reliable performer at 1m2f/1m4f.
runners.comment: Back from a spell in Bahrain, five-time winner, generally reliable performer at 1m2f/1m4f

runners.name: Iowa City
runners.spotlight: Joined this yard for E210,000 after showing useful form in France; won a rated race over the extended mile at the Galway festival (soft); fair third of seven in a Listed race at Cork on final start last year; fourth at the same level at Naas on return; talented apprentice booked; may prove the pick of stable's three runners; leading chance.
runners.comment: Has shown enough in Listed races to suggest she could go close, talented apprentice booked

runners.name: Deressa
runners.spotlight: Ex-Dermot Weld, won her first two starts last season, both at Gowran over a slightly shorter trip than this; highly tried later in the season; interesting recruit to this yard.
runners.comment: Ex-Dermot Weld, won her first two starts last season, interesting recruit to this yard

runners.name: Apercu
runners.spotlight: Four turf wins, including this race last year, beating Cloud Seeker by a short head; not far behind Iron Fist on seasonal debut at Cork; out of her comfort zone in a Listed race at Gowran last time; respected now on the same mark as 12 months ago.
runners.comment: Not far behind Iron Fist at Cork, out of her comfort zone in a Listed race last time

runners.name: Dutch Gold
runners.spotlight: A long time since he showed his best form; the most recent of his two wins was in 2023; towards the rear in three outings this season; 6lb out of the handicap, can be ruled out.
runners.comment: Towards the rear in three outings this season, 6lb out of the handicap, can be ruled out
runners.owner: Albert Dravins & Exors of the Late Eamonn Scanlon

runners.name: Retracement
runners.spotlight: Fifth behind Apercu in this event 12 months ago; C&D winner on penultimate start last season, beating subsequent improver Fiver Friday; final outing over 1m4f was too bad to be true.
runners.comment: C&D winner on penultimate start last season, final outing over 1m4f was too bad to be true

races.race_name: Weatherbys Ireland Greenlands Stakes (Group 2)

runners.name: Comanche Brave
runners.spotlight: Useful form at 7m/1m last season, fifth in the Irish 2,000 Guineas, third in the Jersey Stakes, odds-on for 7f Naas win; kept on for third in 7f Group 2 in Saudi Arabia in February; ran as well as could have been expected when fifth of eight behind the spectacular Ka Ying Rising at Sha Tin last month; trip possibly a little sharp for him.
runners.comment: Useful form at 7m/1m last season, hard to be sure he has the speed to match some of these

runners.name: Bucanero Fuerte
runners.spotlight: Group 1-winning juvenile over C&D; returned from aborted stud career last year and won a 5f Listed race at Naas (good) and a C&D Group 3 (6f, good); fine third in the Group 1 Flying Five before failing to run to expectations at the Breeders' Cup; well below best on soft on seasonal debut at Cork; better over 5f at Naas, but needs more to make an impact here.
runners.comment: Group 1-winning juvenile over C&D, Group 1-placed here last season, fair chance if on song
runners.owner: AMO Racing Limited & Giselle De Aguiar

runners.name: Spycatcher
runners.spotlight: Won a 6f Listed at Doncaster early last season (good to soft); ended 2025 in top form with convincing wins in Group 3 at Chantilly (very soft) and a Listed race at Doncaster (Montassib fifth); failed to uphold form with Montassib at the same venue on seasonal debut.
runners.comment: Failed to uphold previous form with Montassib at Doncaster on seasonal debut, likes soft

runners.name: Powerful Glory
runners.spotlight: Looked smart when landing the Group 2 Mill Reef Stakes at Newbury (6f, heavy) on final juvenile start; beat only one rival in his first two races last term; produced massive shock (200-1) when beating the favourite Lazaat in 6f Group 1 at Ascot last October (good); wind-surgery evidently the key to that success; little mileage on the clock, fascinating prospect for this season.
runners.comment: Won Ascot Group 1 at 200-1, little mileage on the clock, fascinating prospect this term

runners.name: Montassib
runners.spotlight: Late-maturing type won the Group 1 Sprint Cup at Haydock (good) in 2024; did not appear last year until September and failed to recapture his best form in three runs, only fifth behind Spycatcher when favourite at Doncaster on last of them; reversed that form (and had James's Delight behind) when fourth in a 6f Listed race at the same venue eight weeks ago.
runners.comment: Late-maturing type won the Group 1 Sprint Cup at Haydock in 2024, not at best in 2025

runners.name: Big Gossey
runners.spotlight: Splendid veteran showed he retained enthusiasm when winning the 7f Gladness Stakes in March, his ninth Curragh win on his 50th visit; found 5f too sharp at Naas last time; runs in this event for the fourth consecutive year, second at 25-1 in 2023, ninth in 2024, third behind James Delight at 33-1 last season; asking a lot now in a strong edition.
runners.comment: Durable course specialist, twice placed in this race, asking a lot now in a strong edition

runners.name: James's Delight
runners.spotlight: Won this event on good ground last year, battling well to beat another British challenger, with Big Gossey a little unlucky in a close third; struggled in two Group 1s in the autumn, including the Ascot event won by Powerful Glory; out of the money, along with the shorter-priced Spycatcher, on seasonal debut at Doncaster in March; this is a deeper race than last year's.
runners.comment: Won this event on good ground last 12 months ago, this is a deeper race than last year's

runners.name: Fregada
runners.spotlight: C&D maiden winner at two; found wanting in two stakes races last year; same over 7f in a Group 3 at Leopardstown on seasonal debut.
runners.comment: Found wanting in two stakes races last year, same over 7f in a Group 3 on seasonal debut

runners.name: Valiant Force
runners.spotlight: Sole win on turf in the 2023 Norfolk Stakes; has won five conditions races at Dundalk since, the most recent over 5f in February; has struggled since then, most recently last Sunday at Naas; out of his depth here.
runners.comment: Norfolk Stakes winner in 2023, five AW wins, out of his depth here judged on recent form
runners.owner: AMO Racing Limited & Giselle De Aguiar

runners.name: Chicago Critic
runners.spotlight: All three wins have come at 1m; back after a spell in the Emirates; not far off best when touched off by Elnajmm in the 7f Group 2 Al Fahidi Fort in January; beaten 3l in fourth behind the same horse in a Group 3 last time; blinkered first time now; not appealing at this trip.
runners.comment: All three wins have come at 1m, back after a spell in the Emirates, trip looks inadequate

races.race_name: Tattersalls Irish 2,000 Guineas (Group 1)

runners.name: Gstaad
runners.spotlight: Beat stablemate True Love on debut before landing the Coventry Stakes; second in three consecutive Group 1 races, including the National Stakes and the Dewhurst; ended the campaign by overcoming a wide trip in the Breeders' Cup Juvenile Turf at Del Mar (1m, firm); has evidently trained on and can go one better than in the 2,000 Guineas by upholding form with Distant Storm.
runners.comment: Crowned last season with Breeders' Cup win, can uphold Newmarket form with Distant Storm
runners.owner: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor

runners.name: Distant Storm
runners.spotlight: E1,900,000 breeze-up purchase last spring; very smart juvenile form, aside from when pulling too hard in the Acomb; impressed in winning a 1m Group 3 event at Newmarket in September before a fine third placing (1 1/2l behind Gstaad) in the Dewhurst; now has an 8l deficit to overturn in another clash with Gstaad following his distant 2,000 Guineas third; first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Not far off Gstaad in the Dewhurst, 8l behind him in the 2,000 Guineas, likely second-best

runners.name: Alparslan
runners.spotlight: Won Leicester novice event by 5l on debut and landed a valuable sales event over an extended 6f at this venue last September; finished 4l behind runner-up Gstaad when a 50-1 shot in the Dewhurst; reversed the form of that race with fourth-placed Zavateri when landing the Greenham at Newbury (7f, good) on reappearance; missed the 2,000 Guineas.
runners.comment: Reversed Dewhurst form with Zavateri in the Greenham, needs to do likewise with Gstaad

runners.name: Thesecretadversary
runners.spotlight: Chesham Stakes runner-up and Listed winner at Tipperary before a respectable effort in a 1m Canadian Grade 1 on final 2yo start; won going away from Power Blue (gave 3lb) in Group 3 at Leopardstown (7f, good to yielding) on reappearance; pulled very hard in the 2,000 Guineas; fifth was a decent outcome in the circumstances; can make his presence felt if settling better.
runners.comment: Ran with credit the 2,000 Guineas despite pulling too hard, place chance if settling well
runners.owner: Cayton Park Stud & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Pacific Avenue
runners.spotlight: A useful 7f/1m winner last season; made the early running in the Dewhurst, runner-up in a 1m Listed event at Meydan in February; beaten favourite in a four-runner contest at Goodwood on return to Britain; supporting role now.
runners.comment: Beaten favourite at Goodwood since Listed second in Meydan, supporting role now.

runners.name: Power Blue
runners.spotlight: Productive juvenile campaign over sprint distances culminated in Group 1 win here from True Love in the 6f Phoenix Stakes (good); respectable second to Thesecretadversary (received 3lb), in 7f Group 3 at Leopardstown on reappearance; by no means a bad effort in the 2,000 Guineas on his first attempt at this trip, but probably more of a sprinting type.
runners.comment: Top-class 6f form at two, not disgraced in the 2,000 Guineas, more of a sprinting type

runners.name: Neolithic
runners.spotlight: Won a 6f conditions race at Naas last season; Listed-placed twice at this venue this season, on either side of a Gowran win (7f, soft); supporting role here.
runners.comment: Listed-placed twice at this venue this season, supporting role for Gstaad likely the plan
runners.owner: Westerberg & Mrs Magnier & M Tabor & D Smith

runners.name: Take Charge Star
runners.spotlight: Maiden winner over 7f at Gowran last September and a creditable sixth of 30 in the Goffs Million; had no luck in running in the Tetrarch Stakes over C&D earlier in the month; 97-rated colt faces an unenviable task; tongue-tie fitted.
runners.comment: Trouble in running last time, 97-rated colt faces an unenviable task, tongue-tie fitted

runners.name: Go Just Do It
runners.spotlight: Made the frame in three races at this venue last season, all won by Ballydoyle-trained colts; most meaningful form when third of five in a 7f Group 2 won by Constitution River; odds-on for a comfortable maiden win at Gowran on reappearance; massive rise in class for his first attempt at this trip.
runners.comment: Maiden winner at Gowran, takes a massive rise in class for his first attempt at this trip.

runners.name: Bamako Beach
runners.spotlight: Beaten a neck on both maiden starts over 7f; can win races; this is highly ambitious.
runners.comment: Beaten a neck on both maiden starts over 7f, can win races, this is highly ambitious.

races.race_name: Heider Family Stables Gallinule Stakes (Group 3)

runners.name: Causeway
runners.spotlight: Left Gowran debut effort behind to win a 7f maiden here last October on very soft ground; won the Madrid handicap at Naas (7f, soft) on seasonal return off 94; preferred by Ryan Moore to his higher-rated stablemate Neolithic in a 1m Listed race here 19 days ago; won comfortably and can continue on the upgrade; pedigree suggests this trip will be within his range.
runners.comment: Can maintain the progression of this season's two wins on his first attempt at this trip
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Shaihaan
runners.spotlight: Improved from his debut when chasing home Pierre Bonnard over 1m at Dundalk last August; found the competition too hot in the Group 2 Beresford Stakes; made a fine start to the season when beating two subsequent winners in a C&D maiden; got upset in the stalls when failing to build on that in the Derby Trial at Leopardstown.
runners.comment: Looked useful when winning a C&D maiden, got upset in the stalls in the Derby Trial

runners.name: Drop Dead Gorgeous
runners.spotlight: Regally-bred Dubawi filly; mentioned in positive terms by trainer before a smart debut win over 1m at Naas in March; combination of inexperience and trouble in running proved her undoing in a 1m fillies Group 3 here 19 days ago; remains a high-class prospect; this trip likely to suit.
runners.comment: Impressed on debut, recent Group 3 defeat can be excused, remains a high-class prospect
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: South Island
runners.spotlight: Course 1m1f  maiden winner on the fourth of five starts at two; limitations revealed in two Group 3 Leopardstown events won by the Aidan O'Brien-trained Dante third Christmas Day.
runners.comment: Limitations revealed in two Group 3 Leopardstown events won by Ballydoyle's Christmas Day

runners.name: Amadeus Mozart
runners.spotlight: Made a winning debut in a 7f maiden here last June; no match for Godolphin's Maho Bay in a novice event at Newmarket last month; the winner was a beaten favourite in the Lingfield Derby Trial.
runners.comment: Course maiden winner over 7f, well beaten at Newmarket on return, outsider of stable trio
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Shosholoza
runners.spotlight: Beat a subsequent winner by a wide margin on heavy ground at Tipperary on his debut last October; creditable fifth behind Pierre Bonnard in Group 1 at Saint-Cloud only three weeks later; plenty to prove now after failing to feature in the Ballysax at Leopardstown on reappearance.
runners.comment: Plenty to prove after failing to feature in the Ballysax at Leopardstown on reappearance
runners.owner: Rectory Road Hldgs Ltd/E Palmer/W Slattery/Mrs A M O'Brien

runners.name: Alphecca
runners.spotlight: Useful form on easy ground last autumn; Killarney maiden winner in early October and won a valuable restricted contest over 7f at Naas six days later; unplaced in a handicap on her seasonal debut; conditions race at Cork last month; well held by Drop Dead Gorgeous on running in a Group 3 fillies' event here last time
runners.comment: Well held by Drop Dead Gorgeous on running in a Group 3 fillies' event here last time

runners.name: Zia Zabel
runners.spotlight: Ran twice at two; has matured along the right lines, judged on his victory in a conditions race at Cork last month; however, this is likely to prove too taxing.
runners.comment: Good winner of a conditions race at Cork last month, this is likely to prove too taxing

runners.name: Mr Vettori
runners.spotlight: Won a heavy-ground maiden at Gowran last October after 50-1 second to subsequent Group 1 winner Hawk Mountain at this venue; made a satisfactory return when second of four in a conditions event at Cork; out of his depth in the Chester Vase.
runners.comment: Out of his depth in the Chester Vase and will need a clear career-best to make an impact

races.race_name: NorthStandard Handicap

runners.name: Zenford
runners.spotlight: One of the less exposed runners in the field; placed in three maidens, the first of which was in a Leopardstown contest won by Unauthorized last October; has the advantage of race fitness over that rival now.
runners.comment: One of the less exposed runners in the field, placed in three maidens, yard runs three

runners.name: Invincible Will
runners.spotlight: Solid nursery performer on easy ground, winning over C&D last September; good effort on return when second at Cork (1m, soft to heavy);  well beaten on good going at Naas; trainer would seem to have a better chance with the high-class Quiet Mutiny.
runners.comment: Best form has been with plenty of cut in the ground, well beaten on good going at Naas

runners.name: Morehampton
runners.spotlight: Finished behind stablemate Nakamura in last year's Birdcatcher Nursery; likely to show the benefit of a run in a conditions race at Gowran last month but others have stronger claims.
runners.comment: Finished behind stablemate Nakamura in last year's Birdcatcher Nursery, others preferred

runners.name: Coincidental Glory
runners.spotlight: Has shown his best form at Dundalk and needs to improve on this season's turf form; best placing on truf was in a 1m claimer at Gowran.
runners.comment: Has shown his best form at Dundalk and needs to improve on this season's turf form
runners.owner: Total Recall Racing Club & J Fairhurst & Cyril McMullan

runners.name: Quiet Mutiny
runners.spotlight: Beat dual Group 1 winner Precise on debut in a 7f maiden at Fairyhouse last June on her only juvenile start; looked in need of further when staying on into a distant second behind the impressive Royal Bay Cen in a Listed sprint at Cork early this month; potentially a cut above these rivals; Cork third Oh Cecilia now rated 101 after fine Group 3 second at Naas.
runners.comment: Beat dual Group 1 winner Precise on debut, again shaped well in a Listed sprint on return

runners.name: Nakamura
runners.spotlight: Did plenty of racing at two; winner of two 6f nurseries on easy ground and ran well to take fourth in the Birdcatcher at Naas; a bit high in the ratings since then; needs to step up on the form of a recent Cork run.
runners.comment: Winner of two 6f nurseries on easy ground, a bit high in the ratings since then

runners.name: Down The Glen
runners.spotlight: 7f Galway nursery winner on heavy ground last season; longer trip stretched his stamina at Sligo; fair sixth over 1m here on seasonal debut; longer trip stretched his stamina at Sligo.
runners.comment: Nursery winner on heavy ground last season, longer trip stretched his stamina at Sligo

runners.name: Seoul Force
runners.spotlight: Ran in good company in Ballydoyle-dominated maidens at this venue before 7f Dundalk maiden win last November; lacks a recent outing, but stable's current form is a positive.
runners.comment: Ran in good company in turf maidens before 7f Dundalk maiden win, lacks a recent outing

runners.name: Star Of Sapphire
runners.spotlight: Made a promising debut over 5f at Bellewstown last July; not seen again after winning a 6f maiden at Navan ten days later; hard to assess, and may need this.
runners.comment: Not seen again after winning a 6f maiden at Navan last July, hard to assess, may need this

runners.name: Substance
runners.spotlight: Similar level of form in trio of maidens at 6f-1m on varying ground; won with plenty to spare over slightly further than this at Roscommon on handicap debut; up 10lb, may remain competitive.
runners.comment: Won with plenty to spare at Roscommon on handicap debut, up 10lb, may remain competitive

runners.name: Gavoo
runners.spotlight: Two wins last season were over 6f; well beaten at Galway on his only attempt at 7f; kept on for fourth place over 6f at Naas on seasonal debut (Nakamura, seventh); worth another shot at the extra furlong on that evidence.
runners.comment: Two wins last season were over 6f, kept on over that trip at Naas on seasonal debut

runners.name: Glitter And Glory
runners.spotlight: Only poor run at two was when out of her depth in a valuable sales race at this venue; consistent performer at Dundalk, not without a chance if reproducing her AW winter form; trainer also runs another AW winner, Seoul Force.
runners.comment: Consistent performer at Dundalk, not without a chance if reproducing her AW winter form

runners.name: Golden Trigger
runners.spotlight: Maiden winner over this trip at Dundalk on third and final start at two; no turf experience, should be able to adjust.
runners.comment: Maiden winner over this trip at Dundalk, no turf experience, should be able to adjust

runners.name: Unauthorized
runners.spotlight: Ended last season with a maiden win at Leopardstown; opening handicap mark seems fair; however, he is not guaranteed to uphold Leopardstown form with Zenford who was making his debut then and has run twice this season.
runners.comment: Ended last season with a maiden win at Leopardstown, opening handicap mark seems fair

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Habitat Handicap (Premier Handicap)

runners.name: Indigo Dream
runners.spotlight: Sole win came on AW but four times runner-up on turf including last month over this trip at Naas (Go Athletico third) her first attempt at the distance; unchanged mark, could go well.
runners.comment: Staying-on Naas second on first attempt at 6f latest augurs well

runners.name: Back Down Under
runners.spotlight: Both wins have been at this track, including early last season over this trip (soft) and better form when third to Go Athletico in Scurry from out of handicap; reportedly coming into season when disappointing joint-favourite on C&D seasonal return in March, more expected of now.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner; excuses on seasonal return, more expected of now

runners.name: Sarahmae
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner over 5f on varying ground types but hasn't been at her best so far this season, first two over C&D; cheekpieces now tried.
runners.comment: Below best so far this season, first two over C&D; cheekpieces now tried
runners.owner: Mary B Hogan & Michael M O'Brien

runners.name: Headmaster
runners.spotlight: Two 7f wins in Britain last year for Willie Haggas included final start in Newbury handicap (soft) in first-time tongue-tie; bought for 48,000gns and okay comeback run (tired late on) behind a stablemate at Naas last month for which he is entitled to come on for but has a bit to find with several of these on that run.
runners.comment: Entitled to come on for recent comeback run and yard debut; best form at 7f though

runners.name: Tango Flare
runners.spotlight: Finished last season in excellent form with C&D win in the Bold Lad off 91 and good second in Navan Listed; probably needed last month's Naas comeback run, more expected of here and rider's claim a positive.
runners.comment: Last season's Bold Lad winner over C&D; all the better for recent Naas comeback run

runners.name: Dmaniac
runners.spotlight: Ended last season with C&D win and repeated the feat on seasonal return in March off 9lb higher; career-best when upped to 7f at Cork early this month when a clear second (beaten a neck) to an improver on favoured soft ground; still-improving 4yo would appreciate any softening in the ground.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner; still-improving 4yo would appreciate any softening in the ground

runners.name: Shadow Of The Moon
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo won Down Royal maiden (7f) in September; absent until no show after slow start on handicap debut here last month over 5f; inexperience remains a concern.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 4yo blew the start on last month's handicap bow; inexperience a negative

runners.name: Sir Les Patterson
runners.spotlight: Improved on the AW last winter with two wins at this trip (beat City House second occasion); turf return came at Newmarket early this month, showing plenty of pace until weakening final furlong in finishing creditable fourth in very valuable contest; remains on a handy mark compared to AW so has to be respected.
runners.comment: Newmarket fourth remains on a handy mark compared to AW so has to be respected

runners.name: Fort Vega
runners.spotlight: Better than ever last summer, winning at Cork and career-best when second in Scurry Handicap here over extended 6f; bit keen and not quite at same level in Listed at Galway festival when last seen; may just need this comeback run.
runners.comment: Scurry second has been absent since Galway festival; may just need this

runners.name: Aviatrice
runners.spotlight: Ended last year on a roll with successive AW wins at Dundalk over further, second occasion in fillies Listed over 1m; earlier in the campaign had finished third over C&D; runs off 7lb lower turf mark.
runners.comment: Dual AW winner when last seen in autumn; runs off 7lb lower turf mark

runners.name: Bishopton
runners.spotlight: Progressed well last year over 7f, winning twice; resumed progress on return in March when defying top-weight at Dundalk off 84; drops in trip with cheekpieces now fitted; wants decent ground.
runners.comment: Progressive AW performer at 7f; drops in trip with cheekpieces now fitted
runners.owner: William Jones/D W Sloan/Milena C Mocchetti/Kabin Racing Synd

runners.name: Gerrit's Gem
runners.spotlight: All three wins for Kieran Cotter came at Cork (6f-1m) and ended last term with solid C&D fourth behind Unique Journey; dropped out to finish last on seasonal return at Cork early this month, a run which should bring him on.
runners.comment: Best form at Cork but ran well over C&D last term; better for recent comeback run

runners.name: Unique Journey
runners.spotlight: Three wins include twice over C&D last October; promising C&D comeback second in March, stiffer task in Listed company last time; one to consider back in handicap company, especially given his record here.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner in October; struggled in Listed latest but considered back in a handicap
runners.owner: N W T Dalzell/James Morgan/Karl Kirwan/Mrs John Murphy

runners.name: Go Athletico
runners.spotlight: Rated 108 in his prime so has dropped a long way, but won Scurry Handicap (beating Fort Vega and Back Down Under) here last summer and last month's Naas third (three of today's rivals behind) was a good effort; can't rule out.
runners.comment: Scurry winner last summer and recent Naas third encouraging

runners.name: Erosandpsyche
runners.spotlight: Capable of smart form on his day but hasn't won since August 2022 and two runs so far this season leave him with a fair bit to find despite reduced mark; visor back on.
runners.comment: Two runs so far this season leave him with a fair bit to find despite reduced mark

runners.name: Moltophino
runners.spotlight: Runner-up six times before belatedly opening account in this race last year off 79 (4lb out of handicap); some good runs afterwards in defeat, although well below best final start behind Tango Flare in Bold Lad over C&D; lacks a recent run this time around but Hayes stays loyal.
runners.comment: Last year's winner off 6lb lower; lacks a recent run this time around

runners.name: Tina's Indian
runners.spotlight: Both wins have come here, notably minor conditions event (7f, heavy) last November; first run since when fourth of 5 in good-class Gowran conditions event last month; yard going well but 6f on decent ground may prove on the lively side.
runners.comment: Best form at 7f; yard going well but 6f on decent ground may prove on the lively side
runners.owner: C F Harrington & James John Hughes

runners.name: Genesis
runners.spotlight: Two-time AW winner gained a breakthrough turf win over C&D early this month (good) off 71; close Naas fourth was another step in the right direction, despite getting upset in stalls; 2lb wrong here.
runners.comment: Still-improving 5yo 2lb wrong here but not one to rule out

runners.name: Barbapapa
runners.spotlight: Three-time AW winner, whose sole turf win came over 7f at Leopardstown; absent since November but ran well on seasonal return when third in this race last year (to Moltophino) off today's mark; 7lb claim could prove useful.
runners.comment: Last year's third off same mark; 7lb rider's claim could prove useful on seasonal return

runners.name: I Bid You Ajou
runners.spotlight: All three wins at Fairyhouse last season came at this trip on decent ground; back to form on favoured ground at Leopardstown early this month when beaten a short-head in three-way photo; 3lb rise takes him back to a career-high mark but capable 7lb claimer booked.
runners.comment: Narrow Leopardstown loss takes him back to career-high mark but capable 7lb claimer booked

runners.name: City House
runners.spotlight: Smart turf form at up to 7f in Bahrain for George Scott early last year; standout runs for current yard this year when second to Sir Les Patterson on the AW and latest when close fourth of 13 at Chester early this month, extended 7f looking to stretch him; return to stiff 6f could suit.
runners.comment: Couple of good runs this term; return to stiff 6f could suit

runners.name: The Highway Rat
runners.spotlight: Five AW wins and two turf wins have been at Navan over 5f; last year's highlight was fine third in Rockingham here; absent since October and may just need this.
runners.comment: Best form is at 5f; absent since October and may just need this

runners.name: Bodhi Bear
runners.spotlight: Took his mark to a career-high when winning seasonal return here in March over 5f (soft-to-heavy) off 87; stiff task and ran as well as could be expected in good-class Naas Listed following month; has form at 6f/7f so respected back in handicap company.
runners.comment: Winner here in March and creditable run since in Listed; respected back in handicap class

races.race_name: O'Driscolls Irish Whiskey Irish EBF Maiden

runners.name: Lonely Island
runners.spotlight: Failed to make much impact on debut at Dundalk last December but more encouraging turf debut at Killarney recently when forcing it; bred to appreciate this longer trip so one to consider.
runners.comment: More encouraging turf debut at Killarney recently; bred to appreciate this longer trip

runners.name: Theorist
runners.spotlight: Teofilo gelding shaped with promise on both AW runs last winter but dropped away quickly on seasonal return over this trip at Cork last month, having forced it; bit to prove now.
runners.comment: Winter AW promise but Cork comeback run raises questions

runners.name: Count Bezukhov
runners.spotlight: Wootton Bassett colt; dam 1m-11.6f winner (inc 2yo/AW/Group 1 German Oaks; RPR 103), closely related to French 1m winner Mia Sunshine; eyecatching Leopardstown debut (1m4f) earlier this month when coming from some way back to finish third to odds-on stablemate Cannes under considerate handling; surprising he drops in trip here; cheekpieces tried.
runners.comment: Eyecatching recent Leopardstown debut; likely big player despite drop in trip

runners.name: Numantia
runners.spotlight: St Mark's Basilica colt cost 180,000euros as a yearling; half-brother to six winners inc Pablo Escobarr (1m2f-1m4f inc 2yo/AW/Group 3; RPR 113), Roberto Escobarr (1m2f-2m inc Group 3; 113) and Oddnadatta (Group-placed 7f 2yo; 103); dam 5f-7f winner (inc AW 2yo/Group 3; 113); nicely-bred debutant has to be considered.
runners.comment: Half-brother to a couple of Group winners; nicely-bred debutant has to be considered

runners.name: Vanir
runners.spotlight: Teofilo half-sister to winners Slick (US 1m turf inc 2yo; RPR 107) and Rukaana (7f/1m Flat, inc 2yo/AW, 80; 2m hurdle); dam maiden sister to 6.5f 2yo winner, half-sister to 7f Listed winner Mankib, out of 1,000 Guineas winner Natagora; seemingly stable second-choice, likely all the better for the experience.
runners.comment: Seemingly stable second-choice, likely all the better for the experience

runners.name: Walk In Provence
runners.spotlight: Frankel filly is half-sister to 5f winner Correspondence (RPR 73); dam 7f/7.4f 2yo winner (inc Listed; 99), sister to 7f winner Procrastinate, half-sister to 7f/1m winner Competizione; one of three newcomers for the Murtagh yard, Sakoku seemingly the preferred.
runners.comment: Frankel filly one of three newcomers for the Murtagh yard, Sakoku seemingly the preferred

runners.name: The Shandyman
runners.spotlight: Cracksman gelding a 90,000euros yearling; dam French 7f turf 2yo/1m2f AW winner, family of Arc winner Peintre Celebre; nicely improved on second start last autumn, only outstayed late over 1m in median auction maiden here (yielding); good chance he'll stay this trip and a definite player.
runners.comment: Second here last autumn; good chance he'll stay this trip and a definite player

runners.name: Sakoku
runners.spotlight: Sea The Stars filly is fifth foal of an unraced half-sister to 1m2f Listed winner Aghareed (dam of outstanding 1m/1m2f performer Baaeed and top-class 1m2f-1m6f performer Hukum); grandam won Breeders' Cup Filly & Mare Turf; yard going well and seemingly the pick of the yard's two debutantes so has to be wroth a market check.
runners.comment: Seemingly the pick of the yard's two debutantes so has to be wroth a market check

runners.name: Saxony Charms
runners.spotlight: Saxon Warrior filly cost 47,000euros as a yearling; half-sister to winners Fajjaj (Group-placed 7f 2yo/1m2f; RPR 109), Repartee (6f inc 2yo/Listed; 104) and Parlance (7f AW 2yo; 87); dam unraced half-sister to top miler Kingman; Billy Lee rides, newcomer worth a market check.
runners.comment: Billy Lee rides, newcomer worth a market check
runners.owner: Team Valor International LLC & A Oliver

runners.name: Royal Bones
runners.spotlight: Lope De Vega half-brother to winners Laganore (6f-1m2f inc Italian Group 1; RPR 111), Lady Anner (7f; 99), Dundory (1m-1m4f; 83) and Alfheim (1m4f/1m6f AW; 72); dam unraced half-sister to HK 1m2f/1m4f Group 1 winner Blazing Speed; well-bred newcomer but Foley rides Echo Inferno.
runners.comment: Well-bred newcomer but Foley rides Echo Inferno

runners.name: Stabannon
runners.spotlight: Circus Maximus gelding a 3,500euros foal and 4,000euros yearling; closely related to two winners inc Lady Of Luxury (1m4f AW; RPR 59), half-brother to three winners inc Ray's The Bar (7f-9.5f inc 2yo/US; 98) and Cosmic City (prolific 1m4f-2m1f inc AW; 89); dam 11.5f winner; unlikely source of a first-time out maiden winner.
runners.comment: Cheaply bought Circus Maximus gelding; unlikely source of a first-time out maiden winner

runners.name: Niobite
runners.spotlight: Zoustar brother to 7.3f 2yo winner Wholetthedogsout (RPR 61), half-brother to French 1m7f hurdle winner Crimson Velvet; dam unraced half-sister to Listed-placed winners Clear Impression (6f) and Al Fareej (5f); debutant likely best watched.
runners.comment: Brother to 7.3f 2yo winner Wholetthedogsout; debutant likely best watched

runners.name: Glen Echo
runners.spotlight: No show in recent maidens at Navan and Leopardstown; eligible for a mark after this.
runners.comment: No show in recent maidens at Navan and Leopardstown; eligible for a mark after this

runners.name: Matada
runners.spotlight: Beaten a long way in maidens at Navan and Gowran; upped in trip.
runners.comment: Beaten a long way in maidens at Navan and Gowran; upped in trip

runners.name: Will You Stop
runners.spotlight: Saxon Warrior half-brother to winners Gulya (French 7f; RPR 93) and Marroof (KSA 6f/1m); dam unraced sister to Irish 2,000 Guineas winner Romanised; promising recent debut in decent Gowran maiden when staying on fifth (second has won since) over 1m; shaped as though this longer trip will suit.
runners.comment: Promising Gowran debut; shaped as though this longer trip will suit

runners.name: Amethyst Stone
runners.spotlight: Hasn't counted in maidens at Navan and Leopardstown this term; more likely one for handicaps.
runners.comment: Hasn't counted in maidens at Navan and Leopardstown; more likely one for handicaps

runners.name: Echo Inferno
runners.spotlight: Too Darn Hot gelding cost 200,000gns as a yearling; half-brother to winners Sylphid (French 1m1f 2yo; RPR 102), Hosaamm (1m2f Flat, 99; 2m hurdle) and Advertorial (1m4f AW; 51); dam 1m1f winner (83), sister to Gold Cup second Nayef Road; soon beaten on sole 2yo outing in Tipperary maiden in August; gelded over the winter.
runners.comment: Soon beaten on sole 2yo outing in Tipperary maiden in August; gelded over the winter

races.race_name: William Hill Epic Boost British EBF Hedge Of Oak Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: Friendly Soul
runners.spotlight: She's won five of her six starts, the latest victory coming in the Group 1 Prix de l'Opera at Longchamp (1m2f, very soft) in October 2024; absent since and she needs to prove she retains her ability, but she probably won't need to be at her very best to come out on top in this line-up.
runners.comment: Absent since 2024 but this Group 1 winner won't need to be at her very best to win today

runners.name: Wemightakedlongway
runners.spotlight: Last season's best performances came in Group 1s when runner-up in the Irish Oaks at the Curragh (1m4f, good) and very close fourth in the Prix de l'Opera at Longchamp (1m2f, very soft); flopped on last month's reappearance at Cork, which is disconcerting, but she's a leading player if leaving that performance behind in first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Disconcerting flop on last month's reappearance but leading player judged on 3yo form

runners.name: Cheshire Dancer
runners.spotlight: 22-1 winner of the Group 3 Valiant Stakes at Ascot (1m, good) last July and good fourth in US Grade 3 (1m, firm) next time; of interest on that form but tailed off in first-time cheekpieces (retained) on reappearance at Newmarket (1m1f, good to firm) three weeks ago; unraced on slow ground.
runners.comment: Group 3 winner at Ascot last July but tailed off on reappearance at Newmarket 3 weeks ago

runners.name: Noche Clasica
runners.spotlight: Made it 3-4 in handicaps when winning on last month's reappearance at Musselburgh (1m1f, good); up in trip/grade today but she powered ahead once in the clear last time and it's hard to identify where the ceiling of her ability lies; has won on good to soft (unraced on softer); dam was two-time 1m4f Listed winner; interesting contender.
runners.comment: Made it 3-4 in handicaps with impressive win at Musselburgh; up in grade but of interest

runners.name: Dreamasar
runners.spotlight: Made it 2-4 when scoring easily on this month's handicap and seasonal debut at Salisbury (1m2f, good to firm; unraced on slow ground); had just three rivals to beat and takes a steep rise in grade today, but this half-sister to her stable's Group 1 winner Dreamloper could have plenty more to offer.
runners.comment: This is much tougher than Salisbury handicap but she could have plenty more to offer
runners.owner: Hot To Trot Racing 2 & J Fill

runners.name: Sand Gazelle
runners.spotlight: Listed winner at York (1m2f, good) last July and runner-up in Group 3 at the Curragh (1m1f, good to yielding) in August; down the field on the Rowley Mile at Newmarket on both starts since, most recently her reappearance but that may not be her track; could bounce back with a bold show in first-time cheekpieces; unraced on ground slower than good to soft.
runners.comment: Listed winner last July; perhaps unsuited by Newmarket's Rowley Mile on last two starts

runners.name: Sparks Fly
runners.spotlight: Four-time Listed winner who made Group 3 breakthrough at Saint-Cloud (1m, very soft) last October; ran well when 2l fourth in Group 2 Huxley Stakes at Chester (10.3f, good) recently; may be vulnerable to stronger stayers at this trip and she carries a 5lb penalty, but might not be far away.
runners.comment: Group 3 winner in France last October and might not be far away despite a 5lb penalty

runners.name: Consecrated
runners.spotlight: Impressive win on debut at Newbury (7f, good) last April; 0-4 since (including in two Listed races) and has something to find in today's company, but this lightly raced half-sister to connections' Group 2 winner Sacred is in top hands and may be able to take a sizeable step forward at some point.
runners.comment: 0-4 since easy debut win but possible she'll take sizeable step forward at some point

runners.name: Revoir
runners.spotlight: 0-7 since winning on 2yo debut in October 2024 but she finished close-up in a few Listed/Group 3 races last season (1m2f-1m4f, various ground) and she might be the type to improve with age; not ruled out on reappearance.
runners.comment: Went close in Listed/Group 3 races last season; might be the type to improve with age

races.race_name: William Hill Silver Bowl Handicap (Heritage Handicap)

runners.name: Princling
runners.spotlight: Runner-up last October on both 2yo starts (7f, AW/good), on the second occasion in a novice contest at Newmarket that has worked out very well; reappeared with ready win at 1-4 at Wetherby (1m, good to firm) and there's every chance this unexposed colt will be superior to this opening mark.
runners.comment: Considerable promise on all three starts; may well be superior to this opening mark

runners.name: Penny Time
runners.spotlight: All three starts over 1m on AW; first past the post on debut at Kempton in March (disqualified; rider weighed in light) then won easily at Southwell before 4l second of 12 in another novice there; improvement is needed if he's to make a winning turf/handicap debut but that can't be ruled out.
runners.comment: Promising start to career (all three runs over 1m on AW); improvement needed but possible

runners.name: Blue Courvoisier
runners.spotlight: Reappeared with close second on handicap debut at Newmarket (1m, good) last month and 3l fourth of 11 at Ascot (1m, good) since; remains lightly raced but he needs to take a step forward if he's to win today.
runners.comment: Solid efforts on both starts this spring; remains lightly raced but needs a step forward

runners.name: Langstone
runners.spotlight: Reappeared having been gelded and left sole 2yo run (6f, soft) well behind to win at Doncaster (7f, good to soft); improved again to follow up in Kempton novice (7f, AW), doing well to score having been carried left; could be on an advantageous mark for this handicap debut, especially now at 1m.
runners.comment: Novice wins on last two starts; could be on an advantageous mark for this handicap debut

runners.name: Crest Of Fire
runners.spotlight: Having made the running he was beaten just a neck on nursery debut at Doncaster (1m, good to soft) last September, which was the second leg of a four-timer for the winner; reappeared with 10l win at 2-5 in maiden at Southwell (1m, AW) in March and he could be well treated off an unaltered mark.
runners.comment: The form of his sole nursery run reads well and he easily won an AW maiden on his return

runners.name: Astrazar
runners.spotlight: Won Ffos Las maiden (7.3f, heavy) last September on seventh start when going beyond 6f for first time, and he followed up in a valuable EBF final at York (7f, good); denied a clear run when mid-division in handicap at Newbury (7f, good) on reappearance and the step up to 1m looks a good move; chance.
runners.comment: Won final two 2yo starts and denied clear run on reappearance; 1m can suit; chance

runners.name: Laureate Crown
runners.spotlight: Reappeared at Wolverhampton (8.6f, AW) in March having been gelded and won a small-field handicap, and he improved again to follow up at Sandown (1m, good); this hold-up horse travelled well last time and could have more left in the tank, provided the ground suits (unproven on slow surface).
runners.comment: Travelled well when winning at Sandown and he could have more left in the tank

runners.name: Sir Albert
runners.spotlight: 2-2 in nurseries last summer (7f, good/heavy) and returned with close Listed fifth at Lingfield (7f, AW) in February; has top weight in this hot handicap but the step up to 1m may eke out something extra; each-way chance.
runners.comment: Close Listed 5th (7f, AW) on sole run this year; step up to 1m may eke out something extra

runners.name: Jamestown
runners.spotlight: Promising fourth on debut at Dundalk (7f, AW) in March and runner-up on next two starts (7f/1m, soft/good), most recently short-headed at Naas on handicap debut in a race that is working out well; this Irish challenger is respected up 4lb for last time.
runners.comment: Short-headed on handicap debut at Naas in race that is working out well; respected up 4lb

runners.name: Vincenzo Peruggia
runners.spotlight: Won at Beverley (7.4f, good to firm) on the second of his three 2yo starts and reappeared with close second of six in handicap at Musselburgh (1m, good to soft; unraced on softer); however, soundly beaten at Newmarket (1m, good to firm) since and needs to bounce back.
runners.comment: Reappeared with close second at Musselburgh but soundly beaten at Newmarket since

runners.name: Suspended Sentence
runners.spotlight: Won Ripon novice (1m, good to firm) last August on third start then followed up in four-runner nursery at Sandown (1m, soft), before creditable Listed fourth in France (1m, soft); he may have needed last month's comeback run at Sandown (1m, good to firm) but he's not obviously well treated.
runners.comment: Good form on soft last September; may have needed comeback but not obviously well treated
runners.owner: G C Myddelton, C F E Hill & Partner

runners.name: Special Dividend
runners.spotlight: Won last summer on first two starts (6f, AW) then kept on for third of 18 in nursery at York (7f, good to firm) that worked out nicely; well beaten in Newmarket Group 3 (7f, good) on final 2yo start (gelded since) but retains potential and he's worth a second look in a first-time tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Worth a second look in view of his good third of 18 in a nursery at York last August

runners.name: Synners Kid
runners.spotlight: Won for Joseph O'Brien on nursery debut at Naas (1m, yielding to soft) last September and easily followed up at Bellewstown (1m, soft), both in a tongue-tie; having shot up the weights he reappeared with a respectable sixth of ten on stable debut at Ascot (7f, good) recently and could build on that back on slow ground with the tongue-tie reapplied; not discounted.
runners.comment: 2-2 in slow-ground nurseries last autumn; could build on his respectable comeback run

runners.name: Sovereign Ocean
runners.spotlight: Won for Iain Jardine on debut at Musselburgh (7f, good) last July then 11l sixth of seven in the Acomb at York (7f, good to firm); won in a small field on his stable/handicap debut at Wolverhampton (7f, AW) in December and this likeable type is open to further improvement; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Did it nicely in small field on stable debut; 2-3 and open to further improvement

races.race_name: William Hill Sandy Lane Stakes (Group 2)

runners.name: Venetian Sun
runners.spotlight: Top 6f 2yo, completing a notable hat-trick with wins in Group 3 at Royal Ascot, Group 2 at Newmarket (July) and Group 1 at Deauville (all good/good to firm); seemed fine at 7f (ran on after meeting trouble) when third in the Moyglare at the Curragh (yielding) on her final start but shaped like a non-stayer when stepped up to 1m for 1,000 Guineas at Newmarket on reappearance; remains open to progress back at 6f; leading claims.
runners.comment: 3-3 at 6f, including Group 1; failed to stay 1m on return; leading claims back at 6f

runners.name: Brussels
runners.spotlight: Runner-up twice in Group 1 company as a juvenile, including in the Middle Park at Newmarket with Coppull narrowly behind in third; however, he made an underwhelming start to his 3yo career when only sixth to Coppull (Division third) in 6f Ascot Group 3 on reappearance 22 days ago; possible he simply needed the run but arrives with a point to prove.
runners.comment: Second in Middle Park last year but over 4l behind Coppull and Division on Ascot return

runners.name: Coppull
runners.spotlight: Won the Group 2 Richmond Stakes at Glorious Goodwood (6f, good) last July and rounded off his juvenile campaign with a close third in the Group 1 Middle Park at Newmarket (6f, good to firm); showed he's trained on well with a win in 6f Ascot Group 3 (good to firm) on reappearance, with reopposing Division 0.5l behind in third (Brussels sixth); likely to go well for yard which won this with Harry Angel in 2017.
runners.comment: Landed 6f Ascot Group 3 on return, with two of today's rivals behind; should go well again

runners.name: Division
runners.spotlight: Raced at 6f; won three times last year, notably a York Listed race (good; also won on good to soft); improved again when coming with a strong late run (started slowly) for 0.5l third to Coppull in Ascot Group 3 (good to firm) on reappearance three weeks ago; probably capable of better again.
runners.comment: Listed win at 2; improved again promising third to Coppull on Ascot return; more to come

runners.name: Five Ways
runners.spotlight: AW Group 3 winner last September and bettered that form when 2.75l fourth to Wise Approach (Brussels second, Coppull third) in Group 1 Middle Park at Newmarket (6f, good to firm) later that month; late headway for seventh of 14 in 5f Group 3 back at Newmarket on reappearance; likely to benefit from the return to 6f but whether he's quite good enough remains to be seen.
runners.comment: Won Group 3 and 4th in Group 1 as 2yo; late headway on 5f reappearance; return to 6f helps

runners.name: Spicy Marg
runners.spotlight: Proved a useful sprinter last season, notably winning over 5f at Glorious Goodwood in the summer and over 6f at Newmarket (good-ground Listed contest) final outing; finished in front of Venetian Sun when creditable eighth in the 1,000 Guineas on her reappearance; while she shouldn't be inconvenienced by the drop back to 6f it remains to be seen whether she can confirm that superiority over Karl Burke's filly at the shorter distance.
runners.comment: Finished in front of Venetian Sun in 1,000 Guineas but far from sure to back sprinting

runners.name: Aqpan
runners.spotlight: AW maiden winner but limitations seemingly exposed at Group 3 and Listed level at Newbury since, including dropped to 6f last weekend.
runners.comment: Useful AW winner but limitations seemingly exposed at Group 3/Listed level last twice

runners.name: Rock On Thunder
runners.spotlight: Raced at 6f; smart 2yo, notably second in the Gimcrack at York; below par tackling going softer than good for the first when sixth in the Mill Reef at Newbury on final start, which is a bit of a concern should the ground here remain on the soft side; on a positive note, his yard has won this twice since 2019, including with Inisherin in these silks two years ago.
runners.comment: Gimrack second; yard no stranger to success in this but going softer than good a worry
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

races.race_name: William Hill Temple Stakes (Group 2)

runners.name: American Affair
runners.spotlight: Highly progressive in 2025, winning the Group 1 King Charles III Stakes at Royal Ascot (5f, good to firm); also won on good to soft last year; it was his first run since when down the field at York recently and he may have needed it, but a dramatic transformation is required just ten days on.
runners.comment: 5f Group 1 winner at Royal Ascot last June; well beaten ten days ago on sole outing since

runners.name: Frost At Dawn
runners.spotlight: Made all in Listed race here (6f, good to firm) last May and placed in two 5f Group 1s subsequently; in the mix on best form and she's a lot better than she showed at Newmarket on her return, but has to leave that heavy defeat well behind; cheekpieces go on; slight doubt about slow ground.
runners.comment: Placed in two Group 1s last season but has to leave heavy reappearance defeat well behind

runners.name: Night Raider
runners.spotlight: Not beaten far in two 5f Group 1s last August/September (good to firm/yielding) and he finally got off the mark on turf when making all in the Group 3 Palace House Stakes at Newmarket (5f, good to firm) three weeks ago; that was his first run since being gelded and he could be tough to reel in.
runners.comment: Made all in Palace House on first run since being gelded and he could be tough to reel in

runners.name: First Instinct
runners.spotlight: Close third in the Group 2 Sandy Lane on this card 12 months ago (6f, good) and she won a Group 3 at Newbury (5f, good to soft) last September; drawn badly when down the field in the Abbaye at Longchamp last October and she may have needed her comeback run at Newmarket; she's not discounted.
runners.comment: Group 3 winner at Newbury last September and she may have needed comeback run at Newmarket

runners.name: Getreadytorumble
runners.spotlight: 4yo who made his debut last May and progressed very nicely, his campaign culminating in Listed third at Ascot (5f, soft) in October; returned with Listed third at Naas (5f, good) where he might have won with a clear run; others have better form but he's unexposed and could have more to offer.
runners.comment: Others have better form but he was unlucky when Listed third at Naas on reappearance

runners.name: Asfoora
runners.spotlight: Carries a 5lb penalty today and she was down the field on her comeback at Newmarket three weeks ago; however, she may have needed that run and is entitled to respect having won two 5f Group 1s last season, namely the Nunthorpe at York (good to firm) and the Prix de l'Abbaye at Longchamp (very soft).
runners.comment: Two Group 1 wins last season and could leave her recent reappearance run well behind

runners.name: Shagraan
runners.spotlight: His form took a surge forward towards the end of last season with 5f Listed wins for Mick Appleby at Beverley (good to firm) and Ascot (soft); reappeared with 3l third of 14 to Night Raider on stable debut in the Group 3 Palace House at Newmarket (5f, good to firm) and he's open to improvement on this second start of the season; one to consider.
runners.comment: Two Listed wins last August/October and could build on this month's Palace House third

runners.name: Jm Jungle
runners.spotlight: 6yo who rose through the ranks last season and won the Group 2 King George Stakes at Goodwood (5f, good to soft) in August; reappeared with respectable fourth of 14 to Night Raider in the Palace House at Newmarket (5f, good to firm) three weeks ago and he could show the benefit of that outing today.
runners.comment: Group 2 winner last August and he could improve for his comeback fourth at Newmarket
runners.owner: MPS Racing Ltd & The Ayrshire Tradesmen

runners.name: Mgheera
runners.spotlight: Made it 2-2 for the yard (good/good to soft) when winning this last year and some good runs in defeat subsequently, including in Group 1s; could improve for her recent reappearance sixth at Longchamp; might be thereabouts.
runners.comment: Won this last year and some good runs in defeat subsequently; might be thereabouts
runners.owner: Lord Lloyd Webber And Arthur Mitchell

runners.name: Argentine Tango
runners.spotlight: 2yo Listed winner at Newmarket last June and has run well when fourth in Group 3/Listed races on her two starts this spring, but she would be a shock winner today.
runners.comment: 2yo Listed winner last June and in good heart this spring, but she would be a shock winner

runners.name: Beautiful Diamond
runners.spotlight: Three Listed wins, latest at Ayr (5.5f, soft) last September; returned with Listed third at Bath (5f, good) but well beaten in Longchamp Group 3 (5f, soft) since; fifth in this race in 2024; may again be vulnerable.
runners.comment: Three-time Listed winner but below par in France last time; may be vulnerable

runners.name: Jakajaro
runners.spotlight: He's got back on track in a major way in 5f handicaps this spring, winning at Newmarket then impressively making all in a big field at York last week (both on good ground; effective on soft early in career); back up in grade this afternoon but this thriving 5yo could be capable of another bold bid.
runners.comment: Impressively made all in big-field handicap at York last week; could make another bold bid

runners.name: Khaadem
runners.spotlight: Won the 6f Group 1 at Royal Ascot in 2023 and 2024 and added 5.5f Grade 2 win at Keeneland last October; however, this 10yo wasn't at his best at Meydan in March on his sole run this year, and the drop back to 5f and the forecast slow ground are likely negatives.
runners.comment: Two-time Group 1 winner but this 10yo was below his best on sole run this year

races.race_name: William Hill Best Odds Guaranteed Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Almuhit
runners.spotlight: Rediscovered the knack for new stable last year, including two front-running wins over 2m in four-runner Flat races and two places when the action got more competitive; returned to some hurdles form on last two outings in that sphere; a lesser show on Musselburgh Flat in between was over 1m6f but this race may also prove too tough.
runners.comment: Stepping back up from 1m6f should suit but this race may also prove too tough

runners.name: Hermetic
runners.spotlight: Dual soft-ground winner (1m4f/2m) in France last autumn, from just four races there; picked up for 105,000euros in November and put up a bold show when fourth of 17 at York (1m4f, good) ten days ago; stuck to his task at 20-1 that day and looks a strong candidate if that run has brought him on.
runners.comment: Two soft-ground wins (1m4f/2m) in France; bold show at York (1m4f) on recent British debut

runners.name: Blazeon Five
runners.spotlight: Ended last year in storming form for 1m6f/2m hat-trick and came a respectable second at Newbury (2m, good) five weeks ago after a three-month break; peak efforts on softer than good, so she needs a close look.
runners.comment: Respectable second last time; peak efforts on softer than good, so she needs a close look

runners.name: Pole Star
runners.spotlight: Won two 1m6f handicaps last term, one in a valuable race here; back-to-form third with a first-time visor at Newmarket (1m6f) three weeks ago, so re-enters calculations despite earlier lesser efforts over 2m2f and 2m.
runners.comment: Back-to-form third with first visor at Newmarket (1m6f) three weeks ago; stamina unproven
runners.owner: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum

runners.name: Prydwen
runners.spotlight: What a fantastic horse he's been, hitting his peak in 2024 but coming down the weights in handicaps and already making his presence felt over 2m and 1m4f this season; his fifth in Germany 13 days ago was in a Group 2, so another good run; effective at this track and remains in serious calculations on good or good to soft, but unproven on soft.
runners.comment: Making his presence felt again; player on good or good to soft, but unproven on soft

runners.name: Venezuelan
runners.spotlight: Small-field Lingfield maiden last August was his only win but he was on the premises in nearly all his handicaps; tried various headgear on last five starts and switches it again on this first run since leaving Ralph Beckett for 90,000gns in October; unraced beyond 1m4f or on softer than good, but staying trips are worth exploring and he has a competitive mark.
runners.comment: Left R Beckett 90,000gns October; just a maiden win but staying trips are worth exploring
runners.owner: P and Mrs G A Clarke

runners.name: Humble Spark
runners.spotlight: Held up; 6yo who is 6-27 on AW surfaces but 1-14 on turf; minor honours back on turf for last four outings and he was second of six in this race last year, but a supporting role again seems likely.
runners.comment: 1-14 on turf; second of six in this last year but a supporting role again seems likely

runners.name: Ouro Preto
runners.spotlight: 3-15 when trained in France, the latest win in a claimer at Longchamp (1m4f, very soft) last September; well handicapped judged on peak French efforts but he was a well-beaten last of seven on British debut at Hamilton (1m3f, good; 14-1) eight days ago.
runners.comment: Ex-French; well-beaten last of seven at Hamilton (1m3f, good; 14-1) eight days ago

runners.name: Zinc White
runners.spotlight: All three wins have been on soft going, the most recent in impressive style in the Chester Plate (2m2f, soft) in May 2023 off 1lb higher than today; absent 678 days before an encouraging run last June, followed by two backward steps and he was tailed off on his favoured soft ground in August last time; has left Ian Williams and comes with clear risks.
runners.comment: Tailed off on his favoured soft ground in August last time; left Ian Williams; clear risks

runners.name: Premiere Ligne
runners.spotlight: Finally off the mark in Britain in May 2024 and runner-up on next four starts, including when coming from well back over 2m4f at Glorious Goodwood; however, he flopped when just 9-1 for the Cesarewitch (soft) that October and not seen since; that was his only run in Britain on softer than good; well handicapped were he to return at his best after 588 days off; market may guide.
runners.comment: Well handicapped were he to return at his best after 588 days off; market may guide

runners.name: Yashin
runners.spotlight: Started 2025 with a bang when winning a slowly run, four-runner Group 3 at Ascot (2m, good to firm) in April; remote with ambitious targets on next three starts, followed by an upturn (sixth of 11) in 2m handicap at Chester in September but well beaten on Newmarket reappearance (1m6f) three weeks ago; hood and blinkers (he has tried other headgear) are now given a go.
runners.comment: Has not lived up to April 2025 win; hood/blinkers (other headgear has been tried) go on

runners.name: Tashkhan
runners.spotlight: 2-2 here on the Flat, albeit in 2021; very smart on Flat at his best but he's not scored in this sphere since 2023 and had a disappointing 2025; had a mixed hurdles campaign last season, last seen 11 weeks ago; all wins have been on soft or heavy ground.
runners.comment: Had a disappointing 2025 and a mixed hurdles campaign; all wins have been on soft or heavy

races.race_name: William Hill Price Boosts Every Day Handicap

runners.name: Noble Vow
runners.spotlight: Can be headstrong but comfortably got off the mark at the seventh attempt in a recent maiden at Bath (5.7f, firm); ran well on good to soft last year (unraced on softer); could be on a handy mark if things have now clicked.
runners.comment: Comfortably won recent Bath maiden; could be on a handy mark if things have now clicked

runners.name: Bintaryana
runners.spotlight: Improved upon sole 2yo run to win at Nottingham (6f, good) last month, and runner-up in Southwell novice (6f, AW) since; she could do with settling better but this well-bred filly might appreciate the drop to 5f on handicap debut, and may well have a part to play.
runners.comment: Well-bred filly who might appreciate drop to 5f on handicap debut; could have part to play

runners.name: Yellow Diamonds
runners.spotlight: Built upon previous promise to win four-runner novice at Musselburgh (5f, soft) last October for Kevin Philippart De Foy; well beaten on recent stable/seasonal debut at Beverley but unseated rider on the way to post, had a bad draw and failed to settle; she's not written off.
runners.comment: Won on final 2yo start and not much went right on reappearance; she's not written off

runners.name: Justmyluck
runners.spotlight: Made all on handicap debut at Newcastle (5f, AW) in January but two lesser runs have followed (5f/6f, AW) and a return to form is needed.
runners.comment: Made all on h'cap debut at Newcastle (5f, AW) in January but two lesser runs have followed

runners.name: Burdett Estate
runners.spotlight: All three starts over 5f on AW; won at Wolverhampton in December on second outing and not beaten far when third of three in novice there in March; open to further improvement and not ruled out on turf/handicap debut.
runners.comment: 5f AW novice winner; open to further improvement and not ruled out on turf/handicap debut

runners.name: Hanney Girl
runners.spotlight: Two wins from her 11 starts, both last summer; took a step back in the right direction with 2l fifth of 17 at York (5f, good) last Friday and she's back down in grade today; also effective on soft ground; one to consider.
runners.comment: Step back in right direction when fifth of 17 in a higher grade at York last Friday

runners.name: Sands Of Josepi
runners.spotlight: Made all at Southwell (5f, AW) last November on third start then disappointing on nursery debut at Newcastle (5f, AW); however, he could settle better in a first-time hood on this reappearance and is not ruled out.
runners.comment: Disappointing in sole nursery but could settle better in first-time hood on reappearance

runners.name: Under The Radar
runners.spotlight: Won at Thirsk (5f, good) last May on his second start but failed to progress subsequently; he needs to raise his game on this reappearance and first outing since being gelded.
runners.comment: Won at Thirsk (5f, good) last May on his second start but failed to progress subsequently

races.race_name: Fitzdares Harroways Handicap

runners.name: Startled
runners.spotlight: Overcame trouble to win a shade cosily under Saffie Osborne at York (7f, good) last week, taking his record in turf handicaps to 3-3 and record since upped to 7f to 2-2; may well show further progress and defy another rise in the weights; respected.
runners.comment: Won a shade cosily at York last week, taking record in turf handicaps to 3-3; respected

runners.name: Strength Of Spirit
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner at 7f-1m and steadily progressive until a below-par effort at Chester (7.5f) most recently; may bounce back but this is no easy task off current mark in such a competitive affair.
runners.comment: Steadily progressive until below par last time; others are preferable
runners.owner: Hambleton Racing Ltd XXXV & E Burke

runners.name: Cool Molly
runners.spotlight: Has made excellent progress on AW this year and transferred that ability to turf with creditable third at Chester (7.5f, good) last time; needs additional improvement to add to her tally, however.
runners.comment: Transferred her good AW progress to turf with a creditable effort at Chester

runners.name: Pathein
runners.spotlight: Thrice-raced colt who has useful 1m figures, most recently 2l third of four in conditions race at Goodwood (good); visual impression and pedigree both suggest this drop to 7f is worth exploring; still open to further progress and looks interesting despite conceding weight all round.
runners.comment: Still open to further progress; interesting despite conceding weight all round

runners.name: Catullus
