runners.spotlight: Unraced on ground softer than good; has consistent RPRs over 7f and looks reasonably solid in that respect; chance of showing progress depends on the effects of his physical tweaks (gelding/wind operations) since a disappointing effort over 6f at Meydan in February.
runners.comment: Chance of showing progress depends on the effects of two physical tweaks

runners.name: Goodwood Galaxy
runners.spotlight: A local runner in all respects and aptly registered his peak RPR in sole Goodwood attempt, namely fourth in last year's Vintage Stakes (7f, good); not exactly solid on his form since but the return to this venue may have a positive effect.
runners.comment: Returns to the scene of peak effort, namely fourth in the Vintage Stakes

runners.name: Hasbro Market
runners.spotlight: Creditable sixth to Startled at York last week but finished out of the first two for the first time and failed to show further progress; that defeat suggests he's not the percentage call in another competitive field.
runners.comment: Creditable sixth to Startled at York but failed to show further progress

runners.name: Ironwill
runners.spotlight: Unexposed colt whose sprinting form includes two wins last term and a respectable fifth at Newmarket (good) on reappearance; a possible danger with that outing under his belt, provided he stays this new trip.
runners.comment: Unexposed colt who looks a possible danger, provided he stays this new trip

runners.name: New Monarch
runners.spotlight: Eased once beaten upped to 1m on Good Friday when bidding for an AW double; consistent sort otherwise, including on turf last year, and may rebound; however, balance of form suggests he's no certainty to defy his mark on handicap debut.
runners.comment: May rebound but balance of form suggests his mark may be difficult

runners.name: Aqua Bear
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish runner whose form for new yard comprises a success in AW maiden (subsequently gelded) and creditable fourth in Ascot handicap, both over 7f; needs to do better still in this field, however.
runners.comment: Ran creditably on reappearance but needs to do better still

runners.name: Reciprocated
runners.spotlight: Gelded prior to finishing 2l third at Pontefract (6f, good; wore cheekpieces) on reappearance, posting best effort since starting his career with two wins; ideally needs to settle better back up in distance but otherwise has possibilities off the same mark.
runners.comment: Ran encouragingly at Pontefract on reappearance and has possibilities
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Rogue Messiah
runners.spotlight: Prevailed in Lingfield handicap (AW) in latest 6f start, adding to debut win (turf) and taking record to 2-4; bred to stay this extra furlong and may be capable of further improvement; one to consider.
runners.comment: Prevailed in latest 6f start; bred to stay 7f and open to further improvement

runners.name: Bone Marra
runners.spotlight: Ran well behind Rogue Messiah at Lingfield on seasonal debut, taking AW record to 112; however, far from certain to back up that effort, with his turf form much less convincing.
runners.comment: Ran well on reappearance but has something to prove back on turf

runners.name: Shaman Champion
runners.spotlight: Record of 0-6 since debut success but he has a notable piece of course form, namely close second in nursery at last year's Glorious Goodwood; could go well returned to this venue with Newbury reappearance (stable debut) under his belt.
runners.comment: Form includes close second at last year's Glorious Goodwood; could go well

runners.name: Richie's Rocket
runners.spotlight: Far from disgraced in the Listed event won by Hilitany when bidding for a Lingfield AW double; disappointing at Newmarket, the scene of his promising debut, returned to turf last time (gelded since) and consequently has something to prove off his mark.
runners.comment: Disappointing back on turf last time; something to prove off his mark

runners.name: Wechaad
runners.spotlight: Ended his steadily progressive 2yo campaign by scoring readily at Doncaster (sole handicap attempt) then running well in the Horris Hill at Newbury, both over 7f (soft/good to soft); may well have more to offer this term and enters calculations on reappearance.
runners.comment: Steadily progressive last term; ran well in the Horris Hill; enters calculations

runners.name: Hilitany
runners.spotlight: Absent and gelded since bagging Listed honours at Lingfield in February when upped to 7f and making AW debut; needs to prove he can transfer that improved form to handicap level and back to turf; otherwise unexposed over this distance and hard to dismiss.
runners.comment: Needs to prove he can transfer the form of AW Listed success back to turf

runners.name: Siren Suit
runners.spotlight: Absent and gelded since finishing last in valuable event at Ascot on Champions Day; scored readily at Kempton (AW; sole 7f attempt) earlier in October when back from a layoff, and that performance bodes well for him being fresh; may do better still.
runners.comment: Seasonal debutant who gained his 2yo win when fresh and may do better still

races.race_name: British Stallion Studs EBF Cocked Hat Stakes (Listed Race) (Colts & Geldings)

runners.name: Del Maro
runners.spotlight: Record is only 1-6 but he's consistent on bare figures and has two pieces of Group 3 form, last time fourth of five (not disgraced) in a Chester Vase won by the the colt who is now Derby favourite; possibilities, provided he takes well to first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Consistent; not disgraced in the Chester Vase; possibilities if taking well to cheekpieces

runners.name: Saxon Street
runners.spotlight: The only Derby entry in this field; promising colt who is two from two and already a trial winner, having comfortably beat his rivals in the Blue Riband Trial at Epsom (1m2f, good; Listed) on seasonal debut; should take well to Goodwood and commands respect.
runners.comment: Promising colt who is two from two, latest success in trial at Epsom; commands respect

runners.name: Golden Story
runners.spotlight: Twice-raced colt who is open to additional improvement upped further in trip; won at Doncaster (1m, soft) in the autumn then rallied for third place in the Dee Stakes at Chester (1m2f, good; Listed) on reappearance; one of the main contenders.
runners.comment: Rallying third in the Dee Stakes; open to additional improvement upped further in trip

runners.name: Oratorical
runners.spotlight: Progressive start to his career culminated in heavy-ground novice win at Nottingham; much less solid on his Listed/handicap form since and this step back up in class presents a stiff task at the weights.
runners.comment: Far from solid on his form since heavy-ground win and this is a stiff task

runners.name: Command The Stars
runners.spotlight: Did too much up front over 1m1f on penultimate start but never landed a blow when ridden with restraint in first-time hood (retained) over 7.5f latest; progress has stopped since French Listed win and he still has something to prove beyond 1m.
runners.comment: Progress has stopped since Listed win and he still has something to prove beyond 1m
runners.owner: James Paul Services To Industry Ltd

races.race_name: Elston Consulting EBF Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Knock Three Times
runners.spotlight: Ran above market expectations (22-1 shot) in 6f event on good ground at Nottingham, finishing half-length second of seven; the form pick, assuming that promise is confirmed.
runners.comment: Close second of seven at Nottingham, running above market expectations; the form pick

runners.name: Dark Issue
runners.spotlight: 62,000gns yearling; by Dark Angel and first foal of French 9.5f/1m2f winner (RPR 96); stable has landed this race two times in the last three years; newcomer to consider.
runners.comment: 62,000gns yearling; by Dark Angel; stable has landed this race twice in last three years

runners.name: Wondervision
runners.spotlight: Rallying third of 12, faring best of the newcomers despite seeming green, in 5f contest on good ground at Ascot; likely to be suited by this extra furlong and looks open to progress; possibilities.
runners.comment: Rallying third of 12, faring best of the newcomers, in 5f contest at Ascot

runners.name: Coat Of Red
runners.spotlight: 220,000gns yearling; Blue Point half-sister to two winners, notably Chindit (7f/1m including 2yo/Group 2; RPR 119); dam well-related 7f AW scorer (67); yard's first 2yo runner of the season; has good credentials and market support would heighten interest.
runners.comment: 220,000gns yearling; Blue Point half-sister to Group 2 winner Chindit; interesting

runners.name: Beautiful Effort
runners.spotlight: The progeny of two winners (sire 5f-7f, dam 8.6f AW) for her connections; both parents took a little time to get off the mark; may need this debut outing.
runners.comment: The progeny of two winners for her connections; both parents took time to get off the mark

runners.name: Forever Faithfull
runners.spotlight: 17,000gns foal, 30,000gns yearling; by Caturra and first foal of 7f winner (including 2yo/Spain; RPR 79); yard's record with 2yos thus far in 2026 doesn't augur well.
runners.comment: 30,000gns yearling; yard's record with 2yos thus far in 2026 doesn't augur well

runners.name: Virtue Charity
runners.spotlight: 75,000gns yearling; Havana Grey half-sister to winners Forever Penywern (1m2f AW/1m3f turf; RPR 87) and Waiting For You (French 1m1f 2yo); stable's first 2yo runner of the campaign; market informative.
runners.comment: 75,000gns yearling; by Havana Grey; stable's first 2yo runner of the season

runners.name: Vesper Honey
runners.spotlight: 55,000euros yearling, £85,000 (breeze-up) 2yo; St Mark's Basilica half-sister to two French winners, notably Tiger Touch (1m2f; RPR 79); dam 6f-1m scorer (including 2yo/Group 3; 113); check the betting.
runners.comment: £85,000 (breeze-up) 2yo; by St Mark's Basilica; check the betting

runners.name: Yumuri
runners.spotlight: 75,000gns yearling; by Havana Grey and first foal of 5f/6f-winning (including 2yo; RPR 100) daughter of 5f 2yo scorer; suitably bred and her yard's two individual 2yo runners this term include a winner.
runners.comment: 75,000gns yearling; yard's two individual 2yo runners this term include a winner
runners.owner: Glentree Pastoral Pty Ltd And Partners

races.race_name: Fitzdares Height Of Fashion Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies)

runners.name: Earth Shot
runners.spotlight: Twice-raced filly who is open to any amount of further progress and looks a Listed/Group performer in the making; eye-catching second at Newbury (1m, soft) in the autumn then went one better by stretching nicely clear to score by 5l at Newmarket (1m2f, good) on reappearance; respected.
runners.comment: Stretched nicely clear at Newmarket to build on her 2yo effort; looks a smart prospect

runners.name: Inis Mor
runners.spotlight: Campaigned only over 7f/1m but she's bred to be suited by this new trip; solid form as a 2yo then ran well in the Nell Gwyn on reappearance; came up short in the 1,000 Guineas since but brings a respectable rating to this drop back in class and there's still a chance she can do better still.
runners.comment: Came up short in 1,000 Guineas but brings a respectable rating to this drop back in class

runners.name: Brilliant Star
runners.spotlight: The progeny of Group 1 winners for her notable connections; has duly shown plenty of promise at novice level and is 2-2 this year, gaining those wins at Yarmouth (good to firm) and Kempton (AW) by an aggregate of nearly 20l, both at about 1m3f; brings major potential to this next step.
runners.comment: Two from two this year, gaining those wins by an aggregate of nearly 20l; brings potential

runners.name: Just Call Me Angel
runners.spotlight: Prevailed by half a length in the Italian 1,000 Guineas on seasonal debut to complete a San Siro double (1m, good) and take overall record to 4-7; progressing well, albeit incurs 5lb penalty and needs to prove she can transfer that Group form back to domestic soil; dam stayed 1m2f.
runners.comment: Prevailed in the Italian 1,000 Guineas on reappearance, taking record to 4-7; progressive

runners.name: On Message
runners.spotlight: Proved well treated off a mark of 80 at Epsom (8.5f, good) on handicap/seasonal debut, following up her autumn win (7f, AW); goes into deeper waters but is improving and her pedigree bodes well, with one of her siblings a Listed-placed filly (stayed 1m2f) for her connections.
runners.comment: Improving sort and one of her siblings include a Listed-placed filly for her connections

runners.name: Winged One
runners.spotlight: Won well at Newcastle (1m, AW) in the second of her two autumn starts but was the rank outsider and ran accordingly (last of five) in the Cheshire Oaks on reappearance; again looks up against it.
runners.comment: Last of five in the Cheshire Oaks; again looks up against it

runners.name: Della Pace
runners.spotlight: Registered her 2yo win on AW and ran well in Wolverhampton handicap (9.5f) returned to that sphere on reappearance; has steadily progressive RPRs, including on turf, but this is a much stiffer task; bottom on ratings.
runners.comment: Ran well in AW handicap on reappearance; this is a much stiffer task

runners.name: Classic Cuvee
runners.spotlight: Gained her Doncaster maiden win (7f) at the chief expense of the current Oaks favourite but needs to rebound, having suffered a couple of heavy defeats at Newmarket subsequently; goes without usual hood in this first middle-distance attempt.
runners.comment: Needs to rebound from a couple of heavy defeats; goes without usual hood

races.race_name: Darley British EBF Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: St Hilda
runners.spotlight: Both starts on Lingfield AW, finishing fourth over 7f then taking a step forward with neck second over 1m2f; leading player on the figures and may improve further to boot.
runners.comment: Close second in latest Lingfield AW start; leading player on the figures

runners.name: Guesstimate
runners.spotlight: Disappointing on reappearance but has consistent form otherwise, creditable third at Wolverhampton (8.5f, AW) most recently; bred to be suited by this new trip; reasonably solid.
runners.comment: Largely consistent efforts over shorter; bred to be suited by this new trip

runners.name: Lady Magu
runners.spotlight: Showed promise with fifth of 12, only about 2l behind St Hilda who had the benefit of experience, in 1m2f AW maiden at Lingfield; set for an interesting rematch with that rival.
runners.comment: Only about 2l behind St Hilda at Lingfiled; set for an interesting rematch

runners.name: Evening Fades
runners.spotlight: Front-running fourth of 12 in December maiden at Lingfield (1m, AW) that has thrown up a few winners since; represents major yard and has possibilities with progress plausible.
runners.comment: Fourth in Lingfield AW maiden that has thrown up a few winners since

runners.name: Cixi
runners.spotlight: Has proved reasonably consistent over 7f-1m on artificial surfaces, good third at Kempton most recently; however, pedigree raises doubts regarding this new trip and switch to turf.
runners.comment: Pedigree raises doubts regarding this new trip and switch to turf

runners.name: Triumph
runners.spotlight: Similar type to Kenkelly, having finished third of five on debut; ran to an ordinary RPR in that Ripon maiden (1m2f, good) but looks open to improvement.
runners.comment: Similar type to Kenkelly and may build on her Ripon effort

runners.name: Kenkelly
runners.spotlight: Only a modest third behind the form fillies in small-field contest at Yarmouth (11.5f, good to firm) but may do better with that outing/experience under her belt.
runners.comment: Modest third at Yarmouth but may do better with the outing under her belt

runners.name: Mother Dear
runners.spotlight: Green and always behind in 7f newcomers' event at Newbury, having been friendless in the betting; by Masar and may fare better over this longer distance.
runners.comment: Green and always behind in 7f newcomers' event at Newbury

races.race_name: Avastra Funds Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Finalise
runners.spotlight: Won an AW novice and runner-up on turf in her other three starts last term; made the running in C&D handicap (good to soft) on final outing but was mown down late on by Dancingwithmyself; interesting raised just 2lb and she could be a useful staying prospect.
runners.comment: C&D second to Dancingwithmyself on final outing; could be a useful staying prospect

runners.name: Dancingwithmyself
runners.spotlight: Her second win last year came over C&D (good to soft); career-best form when second at Musselburgh (1m6f, soft) on reappearance, meeting trouble in running, but she could not lay a glove on the principals when returned to 1m4f at Ripon (good) three weeks later; return to 1m6f will presumably help and might offset good ground if it dries out; one to consider.
runners.comment: C&D winner; return to 1m6f should help and might offset good ground if it dries out

runners.name: Peaky Blinder
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at Redcar (1m6f, good to soft) in October and won well again when reappearing at Southwell (slowly run 2m, AW; gelded) this April; raised substantially in class for the Chester Cup (2m2f, good; 7-1) 15 days ago, which saw him chase leaders to 4f out before dropping right out, but he was going places before that and could rebound.
runners.comment: Tailed off when 7-1 for the Chester Cup (2m2f) but he was going places before that

runners.name: Valedictory
runners.spotlight: All the rage in the betting for both starts this term; the line came a touch too soon for his strong finish at Newbury (1m2f) on handicap/seasonal debut, while trying to come from further off the pace did not work out at Ascot on the step up to 1m4f but he did muster some late headway (despite hanging right); that sees him upped further in trip today with first-time headgear; it will be interesting to see whether his backers dare go in again.
runners.comment: Expensive red-hot favourite this season, narrowly over 1m2f and some late headway at 1m4f

runners.name: Align The Stars
runners.spotlight: 2024 C&D winner who broke his losing run in clearcut style when he reappeared with restored cheekpieces at Kempton (2m, AW) in March; second in valuable race at Newmarket (1m6f) confirmed that he's turned a corner and must remain in serious calculations.
runners.comment: Won in March; 2nd in valuable race at Newmarket (1m6f) confirmed that he's turned a corner

runners.name: Too Soon
runners.spotlight: No win since 2yo, having come up short in Group races, Listed races and four handicaps; fourth of 14 off this mark at Ascot (1m4f) latest was his closest finish in that winless period and he kept on well, so some hope is revived.
runners.comment: Fourth at Ascot (1m4f) latest was his closest finish in winless period and he kept on well

runners.name: Moon Over Miami
runners.spotlight: Third turf win was a narrow one at Newbury (2m, good; rallying) in April on his final start last year; may have needed his 2026 reappearance and the unusual test of the Chester Cup might excuse his disappointing show next time, but a revival is needed.
runners.comment: Absent last term after April 2m win; possible excuses this season, the Chester Cup latest

runners.name: Spinning Wheel
runners.spotlight: Queen's Vase at Royal Ascot (good to firm; 25-1) was the only blemish in his five-race 3yo career, which he ended with a narrow but game front-running win in handicap at Leicester (1m4f, good to soft); raised 6lb but worth another go at this trip.
runners.comment: Game front-running win (1m4f, good to soft) final 3yo start; worth another go at this trip
runners.owner: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum

runners.name: Hipop De Loire
runners.spotlight: Sixth off 1lb higher in last year's 1m6f Ebor at York and third in the 2m2f Doncaster Cup; however, ended last season on a low note and pulled up in hot races for all three starts over hurdles this spring; left Willie Mullins for 15,000gns 16 days ago and blinkers (last worn in Germany in 2023) replace recent cheekpieces; which side he shows today remains to be seen.
runners.comment: Classy on Flat; pulled up in hot hurdle races this spring; left W Mullins for 15,000gns

runners.name: Oneforthegutter
runners.spotlight: Scored at the Newmarket July meeting (1m6f) last year but hasn't accomplished a lot since; he's back 2lb below that last winning mark, so will pop up in front again at some stage, but it's hard to say when.
runners.comment: Well handicapped and will pop up in front again at some stage but it's hard to say when

races.race_name: Track Radio Handicap

runners.name: London Is Blue
runners.spotlight: Record of 2-2 at Brighton bodes well for this first visit to Goodwood, which is quite a similar track; may resume progress with Bath reappearance under his belt and looks interesting.
runners.comment: Record of 2-2 at Brighton bodes well for this first visit to Goodwood; interesting

runners.name: One And Gone
runners.spotlight: Gained sole success on AW but has posted two sound placed efforts since returned to turf and dropped to 5f, latest at Chester; again has each-way possibilities.
runners.comment: Has posted two sound placed efforts since returned to turf and dropped to 5f

runners.name: Tie Fighter
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden who is making belated handicap debut; chance of opening his account largely depends on the effects of first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Chance largely depends on the effects of first-time headgear

runners.name: Wedonttelllies
runners.spotlight: Gained his 2yo win over 7f on AW but ran respectably over 6f at Ascot (good) most recently, while shaping as if this drop to 5f is worth exploring; possibilities if taking well to first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Worth a crack at this new trip and has possibilities if taking well to headgear

runners.name: Eightthreeone
runners.spotlight: Record of 133 (all over 6f) since switched to handicaps, the success on AW; close third at Windsor (good to firm) most recently; may remain competitive, provided he's as effective back at 5f.
runners.comment: Record of 133 (all over 6f) since handicapping; close third last time

runners.name: Campenaerts
runners.spotlight: Had a record of 2-3 in handicaps (AW/soft) last term for previous yard; below par at Bath on stable/seasonal debut but may do better with the outing under his belt.
runners.comment: Record of 2-3 in handicaps last term; below par on stable/seasonal debut

runners.name: Arlo's Girl
runners.spotlight: Thrice-raced filly who has failed to back up the form of her debut success (AW); never landed a blow at Chester switched to turf most recently; something to prove.
runners.comment: Has failed to back up the form of her debut success

runners.name: Coyy
runners.spotlight: Overcame traffic issues to get up near the finish in Windsor handicap (5f, good to firm) last time, opening his account; may build on that success and show further improvement; enters calculations.
runners.comment: Overcame traffic issues to win at Bath last time; may show further improvement

runners.name: Angel Numbers
runners.spotlight: Record of 0-9 since debut success but she is somewhat threatening to double her tally, having finished second in two of her three outings this term, latest at Bath (5f, firm); not ruled out.
runners.comment: Runner-up in two of her three starts this year; not ruled out

runners.name: Dark Alley
runners.spotlight: Successful in Bath maiden (5.7f) last summer and ran respectably in 5f AW handicap on final 2yo start; however, progress is likely required in this field on reappearance.
runners.comment: Progress is likely required in this field on reappearance

runners.name: Riley Rocks
runners.spotlight: Thrice-raced colt who has progressive AW form and completed a 6f double with handicap success at Southwell most recently; likely danger provided he's as effective on turf and returned to 5f.
runners.comment: Thrice-raced colt who has progressive AW form; completed a 6f double last time

races.race_name: William Hill Keep Your Raceday Positive Handicap

runners.name: Lord Roxby
runners.spotlight: Generally performed with great credit last season, including a 6f Hamilton win in May off the same mark as today; never involved in either start this year but his stable's good recent form provides some hope.
runners.comment: Hasn't fired in two runs this year but yard's good recent form provides hope
runners.owner: N Skinner D Jenkins J Glover

runners.name: Kats Bob
runners.spotlight: Enthusiastic front-runner who shone for Ruth Carr in 2025, winning five 6f handicaps (including a defeat of Jonny Concrete here on good to firm); creditable in-frame efforts on two of three starts this year without necessarily suggesting he's ready to strike again.
runners.comment: Five wins for Ruth Carr last year, including C&D; has run with credit this spring

runners.name: Fortamour
runners.spotlight: Back on form when only narrowly denied a seventh Ripon win 15 days ago but the fact he's placed just once in his eight visits to York is less encouraging
runners.comment: Nearly gained seventh Ripon win latest but his York record isn't anywhere near as strong

runners.name: Jonny Concrete
runners.spotlight: Second to Kats Bob over C&D (good to firm) last year off 3lb higher; has resumed in good form this spring, following Chester near miss with a close second of 13 to reopposing Mae Amor at Hamilton (6f, good) last Sunday; obvious chance off the same mark; second-time blinkers replace regular cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Returned in form, including close second to Mae Amor at Hamilton last Sunday; good shout

runners.name: Tiriac
runners.spotlight: Slowly away and never a threat on all three starts this year, the latest last weekend; being dropped another 2lb can only help but he isn't obviously in good enough form to add to his 2024 success in this.
runners.comment: Won this in 2024 but isn't obviously in good enough form to stage a repeat

runners.name: Blind Beggar
runners.spotlight: Record of 240500 for current yard reflects that he hasn't been able to hold his form; others are much more obvious.
runners.comment: Well held both starts this year and others are much more obvious
runners.owner: New Vision Bloodstock & Steel A Win

runners.name: An Outlaw's Grace
runners.spotlight: Down in weights and step back in the right direction when second of nine at Windsor (6f, good to firm) 12 days ago; has handicapping scope if able to build on that.
runners.comment: Back-to-form second at Windsor latest; has handicapping scope if able to build on that

runners.name: Orne
runners.spotlight: Very useful on his day for previous yard; nowhere near the same force for his current one but his mark reflects that and he looked to be coming to hand when fourth of 22 over 7f at here at last week's Dante meeting, travelling comfortably in the lead for a long way; not discounted back at 6f.
runners.comment: Better signs from reduced mark when fourth over 7f here last week, leading long way

runners.name: Al Hussar
runners.spotlight: Raced around 5f; creditable in-frame efforts on all four outings this year, notably when neck second of 17 at Thirsk (good) last Saturday; nudged up another 3lb but it shouldn't prevent a prominent showing if proving as effective at the new trip.
runners.comment: In good form over 5f, including close second last weekend; player if as effective at 6f

runners.name: Kiniro
runners.spotlight: Won twice for Harry Charlton, both over 6f on Lingfield's AW track; didn't get the chance to show what he could do (repeatedly denied a clear run) when eighth of 12 on recent Chester return for new yard; left the impression he'd have been involved in the finish with more luck and enters calculations.
runners.comment: Two AW wins for previous trainer; met trouble on recent return for new yard

runners.name: Papa Cocktail
runners.spotlight: Consistent spell on Tapeta over the winter and continued the good work back on turf when close second of six at Doncaster (6f, good to firm) three weeks ago; he's never won above a mark of 75, though.
runners.comment: In good form, including 2nd back on turf latest, but he's 5lb above highest winning mark

runners.name: Yes I'm Mali
runners.spotlight: Prevailed in big-field Class 5 event at Thirsk (6f, good) last month on second start for new yard; found it harder in a Newbury Class 2 last weekend but could fare better with his sights lowered.
runners.comment: Thirsk win for new yard last month and this easier than Newbury Class 2 last weekend

runners.name: Mae Amor
runners.spotlight: Struggled on first three starts for this yard last autumn but back to form with a bang when accounting for the reopposing Jonny Concrete and 11 others at Hamilton (6f, good) last Sunday; only scraped home by a short head so she may struggle to confirm that narrow superiority over Iain Jardine's charge on the revised terms.
runners.comment: Edged out Jonny Concrete at Hamilton but Iain Jardine's charge is weighted to turn tables

runners.name: Almarada Prince
runners.spotlight: Split James's Delight and Elmonjed in top 3yo handicap over C&D (good to soft) in 2024; failed to win in 2025 but he has edged down the weights and his three runs for Paul Midgley have been quite encouraging, faring best of those in the far-side group when sixth of 16 back on turf at Ripon (6f, good to soft) 37 days ago; considered.
runners.comment: While since he won but his three efforts for Paul Midgley have been encouraging; claims

runners.name: Beattie Is Back
runners.spotlight: Landed a 7f turf win at Doncaster (good to firm) last summer and close second at Wolverhampton (7f) on latest start in February; back from a 12-week break with his stable among the winners but he's done all his winning at 7f.
runners.comment: Scored on turf last summer and went close on AW latest but done all his winning over 7f
runners.owner: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd

runners.name: Miraculous
runners.spotlight: Met trouble when fifth at Chester (6f, good) a fortnight ago but there were no apparent excuses when filling the same position at Newmarket last Saturday; the type to click for this yard before long but others are stronger today.
runners.comment: Met trouble at Chester a fortnight ago but no obvious excuses at Newmarket last weekend

runners.name: Jenever
runners.spotlight: Still to find top form this term but he is well handicapped if a change of headgear (first-time visor for cheekpieces) gives him a lift.
runners.comment: Yet to find best form this year but well treated if a first-time visor gives him a lift

runners.name: Mister Sox
runners.spotlight: Started 2025 with three wins; wasn't as forward for this year's reappearance but quickly back on form when second (Kats Bob fourth) of nine at Thirsk (6f, good to firm) three weeks ago.
runners.comment: All the sharper for reappearance when second at Thirsk (6f) three weeks ago

runners.name: Dakota Gold
runners.spotlight: Tremendous stable servant over the years with 17 wins; understandably not the force of old but won on 2025 reappearance (off 6lb higher) and has a strong York record (6-18).
runners.comment: Tremendous stable servant who won on 2025 reappearance and has a strong York record

runners.name: Intervention
runners.spotlight: Won last season's Great St Wilfrid at Ripon off a 7lb higher mark; didn't run badly at Nottingham (5f, good) on penultimate start and a wide draw offers an excuse at Chester since.
runners.comment: Well handicapped without obviously shaping as if ready to take advantage

runners.name: Trilby
runners.spotlight: Reacted well to first-time cheekpieces when picking up nicely to collar a back-to-form rival (pair clear) at Haydock (5f, soft) on Thursday; 5lb penalty in a deeper race but a bigger factor could be that the ground is unlikely to be as testing this time (has won on good but seems most potent when the mud is flying).
runners.comment: Added to good record in mud at Haydock on Thursday but ground likely to be quicker here

races.race_name: Tomahawk EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands A, B, C And D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Lake Muritz
runners.spotlight: Beat four rivals at Beverley (5f, good to soft) earlier this month but came up short in Listed event here last week; needs to get back on track; should stay this extra furlong.
runners.comment: Beverley winner; came up short in Listed event here last week; should stay this far

runners.name: Stardom Glory
runners.spotlight: 90,000gns yearling; Starspangledbanner half-brother to winners Rebel Tale (Group-placed 5.7f 2yo; RPR 106), Rebel Step (1m 2yo, 95) and La Vale (US 6f-1m); dam US 1m1f turf winner, half-sister to 1m-1m2f Listed winner Arch Rebel; interesting newcomer for yard whose 2yos have been in top form.
runners.comment: 90,000gns yearling; interesting newcomer for yard whose 2yos have been in top form

runners.name: Cuban Heels
runners.spotlight: 100,000gns yearling by Havana Grey, out of 1m 2yo winner; promising third of ten at Ascot (6f, good; 2-1 favourite) two weeks ago, briefly looking a threat; open to improvement and looks the one to beat.
runners.comment: Promising third at Ascot two weeks ago, briefly looking a threat; looks the one to beat

runners.name: Turnstile
runners.spotlight: Has had three goes already; faded into fourth of 13 at Newmarket last Friday, not convincing with his stamina on first attempt at 6f; one of three runners for Tim Easterby.
runners.comment: 0-3; did not convince with stamina for 6f when fourth at Newmarket last Friday

runners.name: Mussab
runners.spotlight: 52,000gns purchase at the breeze-ups; by Ten Sovereigns; dam 6f winner (RPR 78), half-sister to 6f winner Rhossili Down, out of 6f 2yo winner; bred to be pretty sharp and worth a market check.
runners.comment: 52,000gns purchase at the breeze-ups; bred to be pretty sharp and worth a market check

runners.name: Possessive
runners.spotlight: By Lucky Vega; dam 7.5f/1m winner (including 2yo/Group 2 German 1,000 Guineas; RPR 110), sister to three winners at up to 1m, out of Listed-placed 6f winning half-sister to 6f 2yo Group 2 winner Zinziberine; bred to be useful and represents an excellent stable; needs serious consideration.
runners.comment: Bred to be useful and represents an excellent stable; needs serious consideration

runners.name: Ponte Carlo
runners.spotlight: Signs of ability over 5f at Musselburgh (third of four) and Beverley (met trouble when eighth of 11); retains potential but needs a big leap forward over this extra furlong.
runners.comment: Signs of ability in two runs over 5f; retains potential but needs a big leap forward

runners.name: Ubetawatchout
runners.spotlight: 20,000gns foal, 45,000gns yearling; brother to 5.5f/6f 2yo winner Tricky Tel (RPR 82), half-brother to three winners; dam maiden (61) sister to useful 7f/10.6f winner Jackaroo; may need a run or two before becoming of interest.
runners.comment: Brother to a 5.5f/6f 2yo winner but may need a run or two before becoming of interest

races.race_name: William Hill Epic Boost Handicap

runners.name: Vantheman
runners.spotlight: None too consistent but he had an excuse on his penultimate start and surged back to form to win at Hamilton (5f, good; made all) last Sunday; that was only his second go in blinkers and he's again of interest under a 5lb penalty.
runners.comment: Only second go in blinkers when making all at Hamilton; possibilities under 5lb penalty

runners.name: Alvin
runners.spotlight: 3yo who arrives on the up; easy winner at Bath (5f, firm) last month and ran well considering he was drawn wide and slowly away when fourth at Chester next time; still unexposed and commands major respect.
runners.comment: Upwardly mobile 3yo; better than result when fourth at Chester; commands major respect

runners.name: Sports Coach
runners.spotlight: Hasn't built on his success at Southwell (5f, AW) in March; not disgraced when sixth over C&D nine days ago but he'll need more if he's to return to winning ways.
runners.comment: Not disgraced when sixth over C&D but he'll need more if he's to return to winning ways

runners.name: Fortification
runners.spotlight: Sprang a 40-1 C&D surprise last week on first start since leaving Archie Watson, finishing fast to lead late on; now 4lb higher but he hasn't had much racing and there may be better still to come.
runners.comment: Sprang 40-1 C&D surprise last week on stable debut; may still be unexposed

runners.name: Elegant Erin
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 in top form, winning twice at Beverley (5f, soft), but she has looked rusty on both her starts this year; absent another seven weeks since her last run and probably best watched.
runners.comment: Ended 2025 in top form, winning twice, but has looked rusty on both starts this year

runners.name: We Never Stop
runners.spotlight: Seems best on the AW these days and has run poorly on his last two turf outings, including here; others make more appeal.
runners.comment: Seems better on AW these days; poor efforts last two turf outings; others appeal more

runners.name: Temple Of Athena
runners.spotlight: All three wins at 5f; below par since beating eight rivals at Wolverhampton in March, although she faced a stiff task and met some trouble in Listed race here last week; this is a more realistic assignment but others still have more obvious claims.
runners.comment: Stiff task and met trouble in Listed race here last week but others are still more obvious

runners.name: Reigning Profit
runners.spotlight: 5lb lower than last winning mark and came close to taking advantage when finding only Vantheman too strong at Hamilton (5f, good) last week; meets that rival on 5lb better terms; should be involved in the shake-up once more.
runners.comment: Found only Vantheman too strong at Hamilton and 5lb better off here; respected

runners.name: Stratusnine
runners.spotlight: Slipped and was pulled up at Chester 16 days ago but he'd previously run well when second over 6f at Newcastle; creditable efforts on both his runs at York (7f); yet to prove his effectiveness at 5f but he's not short of speed; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Last effort best ignored and he'd previously run well at Newcastle; not ruled out

runners.name: Ziggy's Triton
runners.spotlight: Returned to form with a win at Musselburgh (5f, good) in April but followed that with a below-par run over C&D last week; Danny Tudhope jumps ship; others are more enticing.
runners.comment: Followed Musselburgh win with below-par run over C&D; others are more enticing

runners.name: Air Force One
runners.spotlight: Has claims on his narrow Beverley win in April (5f, good to soft; only 2lb higher here), but he was never the slightest threat over C&D next time after a sluggish start; needs to bounce back.
runners.comment: Has claims on narrow Beverley win but never the slightest threat over C&D next time

runners.name: Master Of My Fate
runners.spotlight: Successful twice over 5f at Southwell this year and ran respectably at Nottingham in April, but he's 3lb above his highest winning mark and will need a career best if he's to go in again.
runners.comment: Two Southwell wins this year; fair run last time but will need career best to go in again
runners.owner: Ms Janelle Cao, Ross Harmon & Partner

runners.name: Pilgrim
runners.spotlight: In-form 5yo; overcame trouble to win at Musselburgh (5f, soft) in April and was a creditable fourth in a big field here (6f, good) ten days ago; should be thereabouts again off an unchanged mark.
runners.comment: Musselburgh winner; fourth in big field over C&D last time; in the mix off unchanged mark

runners.name: King Of Light
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 better than ever and he made an encouraging return to action when second at Goodwood (5f, good) at the start of this month; has run well over C&D in the past; solid credentials.
runners.comment: Encouraging return to action when second at Goodwood; solid credentials

runners.name: Vintage Clarets
runners.spotlight: Prolific winner over the years, including over C&D; no impact yet this season but he wasn't disgraced from a wide draw at Chester last time and he's tumbling in the weights; solid each-way claims.
runners.comment: Not disgraced from wide draw at Chester; falling in weights; solid each-way claims

runners.name: Lethal Nymph
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in this race in 2024, and he warmed up nicely for another bid with a good fourth in a big field over C&D at the Dante meeting; should be in the thick of things once more.
runners.comment: Runner-up in 2024 and warmed up nicely at the Dante meeting; should be thereabouts

runners.name: Partisan Hero
runners.spotlight: All wins at 7f/1m but he was placed twice over 5f in Bahrain during the winter; met trouble when second favourite at Chester (extended 7f) on his return to Britain 17 days ago; harder to pin down than some of these and certainly not discounted.
runners.comment: Met trouble when second favourite at Chester on return to Britain; not discounted

runners.name: Francisco's Piece
runners.spotlight: Smart 2yo; struggled in good handicaps last year but he has slipped down the weights and might well have needed the run when seventh of nine at Catterick on return; has possibilities if building on that.
runners.comment: Might have needed the run when seventh at Catterick; has possibilities if building on that

runners.name: Squealer
runners.spotlight: Only sixth and ninth this year, but he caught the eye on his reappearance and blew the start next time; now 7lb lower than when winning over C&D (good ground) last summer; likely to be doing his best work late on and a leading contender if getting the breaks.
runners.comment: Excuses this year and now 7lb lower than when winning over C&D last summer; interesting

runners.name: Toca Madera
runners.spotlight: Useful on his day for Brian Meehan, third to subsequent Group 1 winner American Affair over C&D (good to firm) last spring off an 11lb higher mark; might have needed both his runs for new connections, midfield over C&D at the Dante meeting last time; very dangerous if staging a revival.
runners.comment: Third in warm C&D event this time last year; very dangerous if staging a revival
runners.owner: L Bond & Sheikh Aymen Jawad Al Hamandi

runners.name: Spoof
runners.spotlight: Old-timer who notched win number 12 at Doncaster's Lincoln meeting (5f, good to soft; 40-1); can't be discounted off 4lb higher but others are still higher on the list.
runners.comment: Old-timer who notched win number 12 at Doncaster's Lincoln meeting; 4lb higher here

races.race_name: William Hill Bronte Cup Fillies' Stakes (Group 3)

runners.name: Danielle
runners.spotlight: Likeable 5yo who signed off for last year with a demolition job on 17 rivals in Listed event at Doncaster (1m2f, heavy) in November; possibly ideally suited by the mud but drying ground should not inconvenience her unduly and she has made the frame in two Group 2 races over 1m6f; the one to beat if giving her running after a winter break.
runners.comment: Runaway winner in big field on final 4yo start; the one to beat after a winter break

runners.name: Desert Spring
runners.spotlight: Stablemate of Danielle last season, winning 1m4f novice at Southwell; creditable fourth in French Listed races (10.5f/1m6f, good to soft/very soft) for new yard, rallying in latter; fair bit to find with the favourite but she's still relatively lightly raced and her fitness is guaranteed.
runners.comment: Fourth in French Listed races; something to find on form but her fitness is guaranteed

runners.name: Miss Alpilles
runners.spotlight: Upwardly mobile 5yo who seemed suited by the step up to 2m when winning Listed race at Newmarket (good) last autumn; ran creditably against male rivals when 8l third of eight in Group 3 Sagaro at Ascot on return; today's drop back in distance isn't an obvious positive but she may have more in the tank and should give a good account of herself.
runners.comment: Upwardly mobile 5yo; third in Group 3 at Ascot; should go well despite this shorter trip

runners.name: Miss Wong
runners.spotlight: From a useful middle-distance family and she won two handicaps at around 1m3f in the second half of 2025; however, she has come up well short in two attempts at Listed/Group 3 level, and she has a mountain to climb on form.
runners.comment: Has come up well short at Listed/Group 3 level and has a mountain to climb on form

runners.name: Lava Stream
runners.spotlight: Went close in the Group 2 Ribblesdale (1m4f, good to firm) during a 3yo campaign that ended in the Irish Oaks and Yorkshire Oaks; missed 2025 and shaped as if needing the run when beating only one home in Listed race at Nottingham (25-1) four weeks ago; usual hood is left off today; not ruled out but she'll have to make a major leap forward from her reappearance.
runners.comment: Went close in Group 2 as 3yo; missed 2025 and seemed rusty on return; needs a leap forward

runners.name: Rock N Roll Pinkie
runners.spotlight: Six wins (all handicaps) from 32 runs; not disgraced after losing ground at the start when fourth of six at Nottingham four weeks ago; faces a significantly stiffer assignment on her first attempt at a Group race.
runners.comment: All six wins in handicaps; faces much stiffer assignment on first attempt at a Group race

runners.name: Tamam Desert
runners.spotlight: Often highly tried and unplaced all seven starts since winning a Galway maiden in September 2024; seemed to stay 1m6f in Gowran Listed race last time but still finished only seventh; flying even higher today.
runners.comment: Only seventh in Gowran Listed race last time and flying even higher today

races.race_name: Reg Bond 'Always And Forever' Handicap

runners.name: Reliable Ricki
runners.spotlight: Has progressed with every run and his form has a solid look; came from well back when leading late on at Doncaster (1m, good) at the start of this month; drops back 1f in trip but he's not short of speed; open to further improvement and high on the list.
runners.comment: Progressive; came to rear to win at Doncaster; retains potential and high on the list
runners.owner: James Paul Services To Industry Ltd

runners.name: The Resdev Scholar
runners.spotlight: Reliable type who again gave his running when third of 14 over C&D at the Dante meeting (good); nudged up 1lb but that should not prevent another bold bid.
runners.comment: Reliable type; third over C&D at Dante meeting; another bold bid anticipated
runners.owner: N Bradley 29, Resdev & South Bank Racing

runners.name: Moscow Power
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced and unexposed; landed odds at Chelmsford (6f, AW) in March and the runner-up has boosted the form; beat all bar two of his 18 rivals in warm handicap at Ascot (6f, good) two weeks ago, doing his best work late on; should be suited by today's step up in distance; leading contender.
runners.comment: Good third of 19 on handicap at Ascot; unexposed over this longer trip; leading contender

runners.name: Toastmaster
runners.spotlight: 0-7 for the Crisfords, only fifth of seven on turf debut at Doncaster (1m, good to firm) last month; looks exposed unless a change of scenery gives him a lift.
runners.comment: 0-7 for the Crisfords; looks exposed unless a change of scenery gives him a lift

runners.name: Stellar Vision
runners.spotlight: Free-goer who followed a good effort at Newmarket's Craven meeting (7f, good) with a poor one at the Guineas meeting; beginning to look a difficult ride and on a retrieval mission here.
runners.comment: Followed good effort at Craven meeting with poor one at Guineas meeting; others preferred

runners.name: French Affair
runners.spotlight: Usually runs some sort of race but he has not finished with much purpose at Southwell (AW) or Lingfield (turf) on his last two starts, and this is more competitive; needs a revival.
runners.comment: Has not finished with much purpose on last two starts; needs a revival

runners.name: Dacres Cross
runners.spotlight: Rather fizzled out as a 2yo and he was tailed off on reappearance at Wetherby; difficult to recommend after that.
runners.comment: Rather fizzled out as 2yo and tailed off on return at Wetherby; difficult to recommend

runners.name: Harswell Angel
runners.spotlight: Progressive first three starts but he has not cut much ice in handicaps, fading into eighth at Wetherby on latest; at least the handicapper is cutting him some slack and the good recent form of his stable is a plus.
runners.comment: No real impact in handicaps but at least he's now falling in the weights

runners.name: Sandy Craic
runners.spotlight: 0-6 but she's proving consistent and she was beaten off by the reopposing Zarvali only late on at Catterick (7f, good) 12 days ago; good-value claimer takes 7lb off; not ruled out each-way.
runners.comment: Consistent filly; good-value claimer takes 7lb off; not ruled out each-way

runners.name: Lucky Hero
runners.spotlight: 0-7 as 2yo but improved a bit for gelding operation when making winning reappearance in Redcar novice (7f, good to firm; 11-4) three weeks ago; may find a bit more and he's more persuasive than many.
runners.comment: Gelded prior to making winning return in novice at Redcar; more persuasive than many

runners.name: Cotai Eye Joe
runners.spotlight: Looked green first two starts; improved a bit under positive ride when winning 11-runner maiden at Wolverhampton (7f, AW) last month; hardly looks thrown in for handicap/turf debut but he may not have reached his ceiling just yet.
runners.comment: Wolverhampton maiden winner; this is tougher but he may not have reached his ceiling
runners.owner: Ursa Major Racing & Alfa Site Services

runners.name: Illy's Roo
runners.spotlight: All three wins have come over this trip but he finished two places behind Goldwork when only seventh of ten at Ascot two weeks ago; needs a positive reaction to the first-time hood.
runners.comment: Two places behind Goldwork when seventh at Ascot; needs positive reaction to the hood

runners.name: Pints In Peace
runners.spotlight: Has been busy on the AW, winning over this trip at Wolverhampton in March and May; most recent success came in a weakish race and he's now up 6lb and back on turf (unplaced all five starts); enough doubts.
runners.comment: Two recent AW wins but mark has risen and he's 0-5 on turf

runners.name: Ubetterseethis
runners.spotlight: 1-13; showed he retains all his ability when third of ten at Beverley (7.4f, good to soft) on reappearance, in the firing line throughout; this is warmer but he could make the frame again.
runners.comment: Showed he retains ability with Beverley third; this is warmer but could make frame again

runners.name: Sporting Light
runners.spotlight: Made it 3-3 on AW with convincing success over this trip at Southwell two weeks ago; however, he's gone up 10lb and his turf form is at a much lower level.
runners.comment: Made it 3-3 on AW at Southwell last time but up 10lb and turf form is at much lower level
runners.owner: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd

runners.name: Try Storm Cat
runners.spotlight: Hasn't built on a 6f win at Pontefract last summer, and two runs for his current yard have been some way below par; falling in the weights but needs to turn over a new leaf.
runners.comment: Two runs for current yard have been some way below par; needs to turn over a new leaf

runners.name: Bye Law
runners.spotlight: Won Ayr maiden and Redcar novice (6f, good/good to firm) last summer but hasn't repeated the form in sprint handicaps; hard to make a case for but perhaps this step up in distance will suit.
runners.comment: Treading water in sprint handicaps; hard to make a case for but this longer trip may suit

runners.name: Record Day
runners.spotlight: Unplaced in three 7f novices this spring, racing freely in rear on latest; needs a chunk of improvement on handicap debut, but market support would be significant.
runners.comment: Unplaced in three 7f novices this spring; needs a chunk of improvement on handicap debut

runners.name: Goldwork
runners.spotlight: Proved his effectiveness over 7f when fifth of ten at Ascot (good ground) two weeks ago; others have more obvious potential but first-time cheekpieces could give him a boost.
runners.comment: Creditable fifth at Ascot last time and first-time cheekpieces could give him a boost

runners.name: Zarvali
runners.spotlight: Trained last year in Ireland by Johnny Murtagh; made an immediate impact for new yard when winning Catterick novice (7f, good) 12 days ago, seeming suited by the step up from sprint distances; may improve further but this is a fair bit warmer.
runners.comment: Seemed suited by step up in trip when winning on stable debut at Catterick; this is warmer

races.race_name: Royal Yorkshire Regiment Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Stoneacre Donny
runners.spotlight: 33-1 winner of a 7f novice at Thirsk last September; improved form from off the pace again in all three handicaps since, this season's over 1m4f at Doncaster and York; the latter was a warm race in which he shaped as if a return to 1m2f would be fine; up 4lb but on the shortlist.
runners.comment: Second in a warm 1m4f race here last week and shaped as if a return to 1m2f would be fine

runners.name: Magic Trip
runners.spotlight: Won at Bath (1m, good to soft) last September on the first of his four starts; prominent long way and took a bump in the closing stages when fifth of six in a hot race for handicap debut at Sandown (1m2f, good) last time, which was his highest rating and he can probably do better still.
runners.comment: Fifth of six in hot race for handicap debut at Sandown (1m2f, good) was his highest rating

runners.name: Bridge Of Eagles
runners.spotlight: 11-2 for handicap/seasonal debut at Goodwood (1m2f, good) three weeks ago and showed improved form to come from off the pace and win narrowly; the runner-up went one better on Monday; open to further improvement and the 3lb rise will probably underestimate him.
runners.comment: Open to further improvement after Goodwood 1m2f win; 3lb rise probably underestimates him
runners.owner: C & L Washbourn And Mrs S Woodward

runners.name: Pendella
runners.spotlight: Won one of four AW races to start career; 14-1 for turf debut in warm race here (1m4f, good) nine days ago, leading 3f out to 2f out before cracking and shaping like a non-stayer behind Stoneacre Donny; 5lb claimer is enlisted and this first go at 1m2f should suit.
runners.comment: Led 3f out to 2f out before cracking behind Stoneacre Donny in 1m4f race here last week

runners.name: Alba Gu Brath
runners.spotlight: Two seconds and a win (hung left) to start his career this winter on AW, all over 1m; many from this stable bounce back but, notwithstanding the 106 days off, last month's handicap/turf debut at Doncaster (1m4f, good to firm) looked severely disappointing.
runners.comment: 106 days off when severely disappointing on handicap/turf debut (1m4f, good to firm)

runners.name: Mr Seagull
runners.spotlight: Made bright start as 2yo, before well beaten at big prices in two sales races in September; gelded and wore cheekpieces on handicap/seasonal debut at Haydock (1m, good to firm; 18-1) four weeks ago, when keeping on into fourth as if this trip is worth a go, but he needs to raise his game markedly.
runners.comment: This trip is worth a go judged on 1m reappearance but he needs to raise his game markedly
runners.owner: Two Plus Three Two Plus Four

runners.name: Pearl River
runners.spotlight: All seven races on good or softer; 1m2f suited him well on final two runs as 2yo, culminating in a Nottingham handicap win; not discredited when up against it on his two starts this term and this is much more realistic, but he still needs better.
runners.comment: Not discredited when up against it both starts this term and this is much more realistic

runners.name: Moment Of Light
runners.spotlight: Gelded and tongue tied when justifying favouritism in five-runner handicaps over 1m3f on Kempton AW this February and April; raced a bit freely and never got into it when back to turf at Chester (1m4f, good; second favourite) 16 days ago but not at all discredited and it's possible he may resume his progress; cheekpieces go on.
runners.comment: Not at all discredited at Chester latest and may resume his progress; cheekpieces go on

runners.name: Northern Brave
runners.spotlight: Ran twice over 1m at Ayr last autumn, on second occasion stretching 5l clear when 2-7 in a four-runner novice (heavy); made his reappearance in a warm handicap here last week, weakening markedly behind Stoneacre Donny but perhaps both in need of the race (28-1) and stretched by the 1m4f; better may well be forthcoming this time.
runners.comment: Weakening here last week when perhaps both in need of the race and stretched by 1m4f

runners.name: Plan C
runners.spotlight: Fair form at Kempton (7f, AW) and Sandown (1m, soft; three ran) on his last two starts as 2yo and he shaped as if he'd prove capable of better, including at this trip; one to note on handicap/seasonal debut.
runners.comment: Shaped as 2yo as if he'd prove capable of better, including at this trip; one to note

races.race_name: William Hill Boosts Every Day Handicap

runners.name: Pulsar Star
runners.spotlight: Just five races, the first two for James Tate; runner-up three times, including on handicap debut in nine-runner race at Nottingham (1m, good) five weeks ago; return to 7f may well be fine and he's relatively unexposed, but taking on so many rivals from almost the widest draw asks a major question.
runners.comment: Just five races; 2nd three times, including on handicap debut at Nottingham (1m, good)

runners.name: Signcastle City
runners.spotlight: Drew a blank last year but came down the weights, won over 7f on AW in February and competitive over 1m in two recent outings; case can be made but others have a bit less to prove in this big-field scenario.
runners.comment: A case can be made but others have a bit less to prove in this big-field scenario

runners.name: Commander Of Life
runners.spotlight: Not far away in one of these big-field C&D races last July; showed at Doncaster that month that he can win on turf and he's not been far away in any of his races this year, so he has place claims.
runners.comment: He has not been far away in any of his races this year, so has place claims

runners.name: Mereside Diva
runners.spotlight: Second when favourite for this race in 2024, with mixed fortunes in her five subsequent C&D appearances but she was a creditable third last June; seen in regular action this year, including when second at Wetherby last month but not so good here eight days ago; her last two wins (2024 and this February) were in small fields, so others make more appeal today.
runners.comment: 2nd in this in 2024 but below form last week and her last two wins were in small fields

runners.name: Jersey Maverick
runners.spotlight: 15 races for one win, at Epsom (7f, good to firm; from 9lb lower) last July; faced 7f on AW for last four starts, with career-best form in two close calls, the latest under Mikkel Mortensen at Southwell late last month but today's stronger race for return to turf surely demands better still.
runners.comment: Career-best form in two close calls on AW but this turf race surely demands better still
runners.owner: Tim Cottier, Clark, Spence And Fellowes

runners.name: Modern Times
runners.spotlight: Won a 6f Ripon maiden last June and stays 7f; 0-13 in handicaps, however, and others present stronger cases despite his career-low mark.
runners.comment: Stays 7f; 0-13 in handicaps and others present stronger cases despite his career-low mark

runners.name: Lady Mariko
runners.spotlight: Dual 7f turf winner last summer who overcame the drop back to 6f at Kempton (AW) in April; disappointing at Yarmouth three weeks later but she's from a yard which is in fine form.
runners.comment: Disappointing at Yarmouth last time but she's from a yard which is in fine form

runners.name: Beale Street
runners.spotlight: Last of 20 over 6f last May on his only race here, before finishing on the heels of the leaders twice over 7f at Doncaster; 0-11 on turf and not the most consistent but he won at Newcastle on two of his last three AW starts and the latest was over 7f; 6lb lower today and needs a close look.
runners.comment: 0-11 on turf but has been close up; another AW win last time and he's 6lb lower today

runners.name: Inspired
runners.spotlight: All five starts have been on AW, with some pretty useful form over 1m at Newcastle this winter before a flop in 7f handicap at Chelmsford; gelded and absent in the 11 weeks since; drawn wide; well bred (albeit for much longer trips) and lightly raced for his leading yard but plenty to prove today.
runners.comment: All five starts have been on AW; flop in 7f handicap last time; gelded and absent 11 weeks
runners.owner: Fine Claret Racing,Mrs Burke & C Wood

runners.name: Documenting
runners.spotlight: 13yo who has won on 12 occasions, the latest in December 2024 and his C&D win came before most of today's rivals were born; not disgraced on reappearance and he's now 13lb lower than this time last year, but his last turf win was in 2022; not the percentage call.
runners.comment: 13yo who is 13lb lower than this time last year, but his last turf win was in 2022
runners.owner: Kevin Frost Racing Club & Mike Humphries

runners.name: Golden Strike
runners.spotlight: Record of 122 over 7f on AW once fitted with a visor; however, despite turf achievements early in career, his return to turf at Doncaster (7f, good to soft; second favourite) in March bore no comparison.
runners.comment: Did well at 7f on AW once fitted with a visor; return to turf in March bore no comparison

runners.name: Rascal Recknell
runners.spotlight: Two wins (one on turf at Doncaster) and a second from his four starts for new stable this season, all in 7f handicaps; failed to settle last time, yet still challenged strongly in the final 1f and left the impression he remains capable of better.
runners.comment: Two wins and a 2nd from his four starts for new stable; could still be capable of better

runners.name: Quest For Fun
runners.spotlight: Plenty of C&D form in big fields, notably wins on good to soft in 2023 and July 2025; while not so striking as Leadman, he didn't have the best of runs when fifth of 22 over C&D eight days ago, so might step up a bit today.
runners.comment: Plenty of C&D form in big fields, including his fifth of 22 eight days ago

runners.name: Highfield Viking
runners.spotlight: Gained last year's two wins over 7f on good ground in August and came second in a 15-runner race next time; made a respectable return to action one month ago and enters each-way calculations.
runners.comment: Made a respectable return to action one month ago and enters each-way calculations

runners.name: Leadman
runners.spotlight: Has an underwhelming strike-rate, given his ability, but he was second of 22 off a good mark over C&D eight days ago and would surely have gone very close had he not met trouble in running from well off the pace; the problems today are that he's back up 4lb, drawn even wider and has another big field to negotiate his way through; huge player but plenty needs to go right.
runners.comment: Huge player but plenty needs to go right; it didn't when 2nd of 22 over C&D eight days ago

runners.name: Paladin
runners.spotlight: Free-going sort who was 4-18 for Charlie Johnston, including two 1m wins last summer, and made a winning start for new connections when 33-1 for a nine-runner race at Wolverhampton (8.6f, AW) in January; absent since and has more to prove back at 7f for the first time since 2024.
runners.comment: Off since January win (yard debut); more to prove back at 7f for the first time since 2024

runners.name: Havana Prince
runners.spotlight: Fourth and fifth in his two big-field C&D races last term; Newcastle win in March ensures a career-high mark today but it's hard to judge him harshly on last week's C&D result as he didn't see daylight at various stages.
runners.comment: Fourth and fifth in his two big-field C&D races last term; no daylight here last week

runners.name: Silver Trumpet
runners.spotlight: All his wins have been over 7f but there's been only one of those (Epsom last August) in very nearly two years now; still paying for the ease of that win in a seven-runner race, so others are preferred.
runners.comment: Still paying for the ease of his Epsom win last August, so others are preferred

runners.name: Feel The Need
runners.spotlight: Most of his 14 races at York have been over 7f, including wins in this race in 2024 and another last June; absent since second on AW in January and that's the longest break he's had for two years, which asks a question, but he has to be considered off his last winning mark.
runners.comment: Back from longest break in two years; has to be considered, though, off last winning mark

runners.name: Follow Your Heart
runners.spotlight: Behind on his only run here (2023); Doncaster last June saw his only turf win since 2021, amidst plenty of AW success admittedly but he was out of form in February when last seen; has a competitive mark but other cases look a bit more solid, particularly as he's drawn wide.
runners.comment: Competitive mark but other cases look a bit more solid, particularly as he's drawn wide

runners.name: Bloodsweatandtyres
runners.spotlight: Both races were 7f novices on Newcastle AW, showing promise in September and winning in clearcut style in November; has a useful pedigree and brings clear potential but 22-runner race is something of a baptism of fire for this handicap and turf debut.
runners.comment: Both races were 7f novices on Newcastle AW, clearcut winner on second occasion; potential

runners.name: It Just Takes Time
runners.spotlight: C&D winner in 2022, with no joy from nine subsequent visits but most of those were over shorter, whereas his last four wins (elsewhere) have been at about 7f; he's now 1lb lower than when making all at Beverley last September and a case can be made.
runners.comment: C&D winner; 1lb lower than when making all at Beverley last September; case can be made

races.race_name: Bangor-On-Dee Ladies Day Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Ladies Day
runners.spotlight: Finished second in three of her last four bumpers including a Listed mares' event at Huntingdon in December; runner-up again on recent hurdling debut at Fakenham (2m4f, good) and that was a promising effort behind an 11-10 favourite who made all; sets a fair standard on that form and she's a big player back in trip.
runners.comment: Useful bumper performer who was runner-up on hurdling debut at Fakenham; key player

runners.name: Mega Etoile
runners.spotlight: Has improved with each of her three hurdle runs and her latest third of 14 at Fontwell (2m1f, good) can be marked up because she had traffic issues in the closing stages and got hit by a rival's whip; could continue to progress with experience and she's an interesting contender.
runners.comment: Has been progressive over hurdles and her latest third at Fontwell can be marked up

runners.name: Earnest Belief
runners.spotlight: 0-6 on Flat for the Gosdens; encouraging third on hurdling/stable debut at Carlisle (2m1f, good to soft) in March but he failed to build on that when a remote fourth at Hexham (2m, good); has something to prove after that and this looks tough on the figures; equipment returns including a first-time tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Disappointing at Hexham (3-1) three weeks ago and this looks tough on the figures

runners.name: Winter Life
runners.spotlight: 0-9 on the Flat and was tailed off at 150-1 in both runs (1m4f/1m6f) for current yard in October 2024; makes no appeal now switched to hurdling after 576 days off.
runners.comment: 0-9 on the Flat and she makes no appeal now switched to hurdling after a long absence

runners.name: Double Indemnity
runners.spotlight: Bought for £30,000 after winning an Irish point; runner-up on hurdle debut at Carlisle (2m1f, good to soft) in February but he was pulled up here (2m3f, good) the following month and was reported to have an irregular heartbeat; has bit to prove after that but it's still early days and he's in good hands; stablemate of Earnest Belief.
runners.comment: Irish point winner; flopped here in March but was reported to have an irregular heartbeat

runners.name: Go Try
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to the useful Perfect Myth but he's been tailed off at big prices in two hurdle runs (1m7f/2m, good) this spring.
runners.comment: Tailed off at big prices in two hurdle runs (1m7f/2m, good) this spring

runners.name: Happy Harrison
runners.spotlight: Failed to beat a rival in two good-ground bumpers six months apart; now goes hurdling after 375 days off and he can only be watched.
runners.comment: Failed to beat a rival in two bumpers and now goes hurdling after a year off; best watched

runners.name: Chambers
runners.spotlight: Eight-race maiden on the Flat and was a well-held sixth of 14 on hurdling debut at Fontwell (2m1f, good) two weeks ago; passed over.
runners.comment: 0-8 on the Flat and was well held on hurdling debut at Fontwell two weeks ago

races.race_name: Wrexham Lager Novices' Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Big Boy Barney
runners.spotlight: Point winner who got off the mark over fences on his second attempt, at Huntingdon (2m4f, good) in March; third on both starts since and first-time cheekpieces are now employed along with regular tongue-tie; should be thereabouts.
runners.comment: Point winner who got off the mark over fences at Huntingdon in March.

runners.name: Mary
runners.spotlight: Both wins over hurdles came when making all; yet to convince as a chaser, falling in two of her three starts in this sphere; a clear round would be a priority.
runners.comment: Dual winner over hurdles; has fallen in two of her three runs over fences.

runners.name: Kadjar Des Mottes
runners.spotlight: Irish point winner whose best effort over fences was when second of four finishers at Doncaster (3m, soft) in February; unseated early on the following month and well beaten over hurdles since; first-time cheekpieces are now fitted; may need softer ground to be seen to advantage.
runners.comment: Irish point winner whose best effort over fences was when second at Doncaster in February.

runners.name: Le Gentleman
runners.spotlight: Formerly trained in France, he finished well when third of four finishers at Chepstow (2m, good to soft) last month on his second run in Britain; should be suited by this step up in trip but has never run on ground without soft in the description.
runners.comment: Finished well when third at Chepstow last month.
runners.owner: The North West National Hunt Racing Club

runners.name: Herja
runners.spotlight: Won a 2m4f Sedgefield maiden hurdle last September, but has failed to make an impact in her two starts over fences; has another go on her handicap debut but will need to improve.
runners.comment: Won a maiden hurdle, but has failed to make an impact in her two starts over fences.

runners.name: Bobbarelli
runners.spotlight: A dual winning pointer, his only outing over fences came when pulling up in a hunter chase at Cheltenham in May 2025; a runner-up over hurdles at Warwick and Taunton this year, he should run well.
runners.comment: A dual winning pointer, he was a runner-up over hurdles at Warwick and Taunton this year.

runners.name: Wudya
runners.spotlight: Has a mixed hurdling record but landed her second win in that sphere at this track (2m3f, good) last July; catching her fresh may be the answer and she's interesting on chasing debut.
runners.comment: Has a mixed hurdling record but got her second win in that sphere at this track last July.

races.race_name: Cheshire Oaks Designer Outlet Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Orange Diamond
runners.spotlight: Has record of 11142132 for his current yard, with those first three wins over hurdles at Worcester (2m4f) and his last success over fences at Market Rasen (2m7f); up another 3lb after his second over hurdles at Warwick (2m3f, good) last month, but he went down fighting after making a mistake at the last; could still have more to offer and he's a big player.
runners.comment: Record of 11142132 for current yard and latest second was back over hurdles; key player

runners.name: Play Pretend
runners.spotlight: Won at Worcester (2m, good) last July and he ended last year with two close calls over 2m4f; well-held fifth on reappearance at Market Rasen last month but he may have needed that run and has claims if he can get back near best.
runners.comment: May have needed his reappearance run at Worcester and has claims if back near best

runners.name: Copper Beach
runners.spotlight: Record of 1-18 over hurdles but he returned after 510 days off with a second at Southwell (2m4f, good) two weeks ago; that was a good effort behind an improver and he's a player if he can build on that.
runners.comment: Returned from long absence with a second at Southwell; dangerous if he can build on that

runners.name: The Secret Pearl
runners.spotlight: Won a mares' maiden at Market Rasen (2m, good) last April; runner-up in first two handicaps during the winter but she's been disappointing in last two runs; bit to prove but mark is sliding and she's had wind surgery since her latest effort at Fontwell last month; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Bit to prove but has possibilities if she can get back on track after wind surgery

runners.name: Back In The Bay
runners.spotlight: Made it 2-5 over hurdles when landing a mares' handicap at Stratford (2m3f, good) last July but she flopped in a three-runner race in final run last year; has bit to prove after 218 days but she has potential in this sphere and needs a close look in market.
runners.comment: Flopped in final run last year but she's still lightly raced; needs a close look on return
runners.owner: Value Racing - Back In The Bay

runners.name: Crystal Glance
runners.spotlight: Record of 4-12 over hurdles but she was vulnerable in her final three runs last year including over C&D (good); now only 1lb higher than for her last success but she needs to rediscover her spark after six months off.
runners.comment: Only 1lb higher than for last win but needs to rediscover her spark after six months off

runners.name: Rock Hurley
runners.spotlight: Won a C&D handicap (good to soft) last April but he's not come close to that form in four subsequent runs and latest was back here in March; has some questions to answer and he's still 3lb higher than for his last win.
runners.comment: Won over C&D last April but he's not come close to that form in four subsequent runs

runners.name: Magic Wave
runners.spotlight: Won off 2lb lower at Kelso (2m5f, good) last April but he's not seen much action since and has been beaten 39l in both runs this year; has some questions to answer and he needs a big turnaround.
runners.comment: Dual hurdle winner but he's lost his way in last two starts and needs a big turnaround

runners.name: Torrent
runners.spotlight: Got off to a flyer when winning two of his first three starts over hurdles in 2024; has drawn a blank since but he's on a reduced mark and wasn't beaten far when fourth at Uttoxeter (2m4f, good) three weeks; has possibilities if he can build on that and he's in the mix.
runners.comment: On reduced mark and he wasn't beaten far at Uttoxeter (2m4f) last time; in the mix

runners.name: Sauce Of Life
runners.spotlight: Unbeaten in bumpers (2-2) and very lightly raced for his age; won a hurdle handicap at Doncaster (2m4f, good to soft) in December 2024 but he's had only two runs since and has struggled in both of them; has something to prove after another absence and he needs first-time cheekpieces to work.
runners.comment: Very lightly raced 11yo who has something to prove after another absence; cheekpieces on
runners.owner: Mrs I C Sellars And Miss D C Sellars

races.race_name: F Jones Initiative Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Gris Majeur
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark over fences at Ludlow (2m4f, good to firm) last October and has been placed on all five starts since; drops back in trip which may not be in his favour.
runners.comment: Won over fences at Ludlow last October and has been placed on all five starts since.
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Brian Davies

runners.name: Dexter
runners.spotlight: Won five times when trained in France; first win in Britain came at Cartmel (2m1f, good) last August; has run well when in the frame since but was beaten 30l back over hurdles when last seen in November; may be better for the run.
runners.comment: Won five times when trained in France; first win in Britain came at Cartmel last August.

runners.name: Yes Indeed
runners.spotlight: Won twice over fences at around this trip last spring; not so good since but is 1lb lower than his last winning mark so cannot be ruled out.
runners.comment: Won twice over fences at around this trip last spring; not so good since.

runners.name: Jet Of Dreams
runners.spotlight: Has five wins over fences, the latest at Sedgefield last September off 5lb lower; should strip fitter for a recent fourth over hurdles but may need to come down a few pounds.
runners.comment: Has five wins over fences, the latest at Sedgefield last September off 5lb lower.

runners.name: Supreme George
runners.spotlight: Has taken well to fences and completed a three-timer at Catterick in January; resumed winning ways at Chepstow (2m, good to soft) last month and this front-runner may prove hard to peg back.
runners.comment: Resumed winning ways at Chepstow last month.

runners.name: Start Again
runners.spotlight: Exeter maiden hurdle winner in October; pulled up on his chase debut at Uttoxeter three weeks ago; best watched.
runners.comment: Pulled up on his chase debut at Uttoxeter three weeks ago.

runners.name: Nibras Gold
runners.spotlight: Dual 1m4f Flat winner (made all) and Summer Hurdle third last year, and jumped perfectly well when second on two of three chase starts in summer 2024; record of 62121 when returning from breaks of over 200 days.
runners.comment: Dual 1m4f Flat winner (made all) and Summer Hurdle third last year; goes well fresh.

races.race_name: Bangor-On-Dee Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Walking On Air
runners.spotlight: Back over hurdles with respectable fifth of 15 in Class 2 event at Haydock (3m, good) two weeks ago; 5lb lower back over fences but he's 0-12 in this sphere and he fell at Ayr on his penultimate run; down the list.
runners.comment: Fair effort over hurdles latest but he's 0-12 over fences and fell on his penultimate run

runners.name: Cruz Control
runners.spotlight: Took big step back in right direction when fifth at Aintree (3m1f, good to soft) last month, in race he won the previous two seasons; still on a workable mark and has a fighting chance here if first-time cheekpieces have any positive effect.
runners.comment: Back to form with creditable fifth at Aintree last month; dangerous with cheekpieces added

runners.name: Somespring Special
runners.spotlight: 8yo who made it 5-16 over fences when bouncing back with a clearcut win at Newton Abbot (2m5f, good) two weeks ago; up 6lb but she hit a personal best last time and is a big player again if her stamina holds out on this step up 3m.
