runners.comment: Made it 5-16 over fences when scoring at Newton Abbot (2m5f); respected back up in trip

runners.name: Alien Storm
runners.spotlight: Ran respectably when third of five over hurdles at regular haunt Plumpton (2m4f, good) last month; better known as a chaser these days but he looks weighted near best off this mark and is untried beyond 2m5f; has some work to do back in this sphere.
runners.comment: Four-time chaser winner but he looks weighted near best and is untried beyond 2m5f

runners.name: Twig
runners.spotlight: Second at the 2024 Cheltenham Festival and he started last season with two wins, including a gritty success in the Becher at Aintree (3m2f); struggled in the Grand National last month but he's now only 2lb higher than for his last win and looks interesting on this drop back in trip and class; last success was on soft but most of his wins have been on good ground.
runners.comment: Struggled in the Grand National but he's interesting back in calmer waters at this trip

runners.name: Mars Harper
runners.spotlight: Left Gordon Elliott after disappointing hurdle run last autumn; well beaten again on stable debut in March but, after wind surgery, he returned to chasing with a good second at Fakenham (3m, good) 19 days ago; has possibilities if he can build on that but his last win was in 2023.
runners.comment: Back over fences after wind surgery with a second at Fakenham last month; possibilities

runners.name: Lounge Lizard
runners.spotlight: Four-time chase winner at up to 3m1f; well handicapped on his Huntingdon win for Henry Daly last March but he's struggled since including in two runs for new yard this spring; ran well to a point at Ludlow last time but he was on the retreat when he fell and others preferred.
runners.comment: Well handicapped on his Huntingdon win last March but he's struggled since; down the list

races.race_name: Tom Jones Live At Chester Racecourse Mares' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Jetty May
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the fifth attempt over hurdles when keeping on well for a 16-1 win at Newton Abbot (2m6f, good) last month; up 5lb but she responded well to a tongue-tie last time and is open to more progress at this new trip; key player.
runners.comment: Improved form with tongue-tie added when scoring at Newton Abbot; big player again up 5lb

runners.name: Pooley's Promise
runners.spotlight: Won a Fontwell handicap (3m, good) for Ryan Potter last May but she's been disappointing since including in two runs for new yard this spring; mark continues to fall but she was pulled up at Taunton last time and has a lot to prove; hood is added and tongue-tie is removed.
runners.comment: Won at Fontwell last spring but she's been disappointing since and has a lot to prove

runners.name: Camshaft
runners.spotlight: Big improvement with cheekpieces added on handicap debut when beating 11 rivals at Ffos Las (2m4f, good to soft) last month; up 6lb but she knuckled down well last time and this unexposed mare shaped as though this new trip should be within range; strongly respected.
runners.comment: Won in cheekpieces on handicap debut at Ffos Las; up 6lb but she's open to more progress

runners.name: Newmill Getaway
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner who is 0-12 over hurdles but she has a solid record in handicaps for current yard and was runner-up at Taunton (3m, good) in March; should go well again off an unchanged mark and she's in the mix.
runners.comment: 0-12 over hurdles but she's consistent and was runner-up at Taunton latest; in the mix

runners.name: La Cantate
runners.spotlight: Record of 0-7 but she returned after 276 days off with a promising second in a Ludlow handicap (2m5f, good) last month; nudged up 2lb but she stayed on well last time and has possibilities on this step up to 2m7f.
runners.comment: Reappeared with a good second at Ludlow (2m5f) and has claims if she can build on that

runners.name: Porridge
runners.spotlight: 0-13 over hurdles; ran a big race at a massive price when fourth in a Fontwell maiden (2m3f, good to soft) last month but her mark shot up 41lb after that and she struggled back in a handicap at Fontwell (2m5f) last week; opposable.
runners.comment: 0-13 over hurdles and she struggled off a tough mark back in a handicap latest; opposable

runners.name: After Midnight
runners.spotlight: Ten-race maiden but she rallied on her Fontwell reappearance (3m2f, soft; cheekpieces) in March and was not beaten far in fourth; this is a deeper race but she's unexposed as a stayer and is not ruled out off 1lb lower than last time.
runners.comment: Fair effort when fourth on Fontwell reappearance (3m2f) in March and she's not ruled out
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Donald Smith

runners.name: After The Ball
runners.spotlight: 0-8 in Irish points and she hasn't shown a great deal in three hurdle runs (2m, good) this spring; now goes handicapping but this is competitive and she needs a transformation on this big step up in trip
runners.comment: Handicap newcomer but she needs a transformation on this big step up in trip

runners.name: Victoria Milano
runners.spotlight: Won two novice hurdles (2m3f/2m) for Alistair Ralph in 2023-24; struggled in first three runs for current yard but she showed signs of a revival when third of 12 at Uttoxeter (2m4f, good) last Saturday; was no match for the first two in that race but her mark continues to fall and she looks worth another try at this trip; could be dangerous.
runners.comment: Showed signs of a revival when third at Uttoxeter (2m4f) last Saturday; could be dangerous

runners.name: Faerie Cutlass
runners.spotlight: Won at Ludlow (3m, good to soft) in November 2024 but she struggled last year and was beaten 20l at Sedgefield when last seen in November; now 4lb lower than for her last success but she needs a major revival after a break.
runners.comment: 8yo who struggled last year and needs a major revival after a break

races.race_name: Wrexham Evening On Friday 26th June Mares' Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 1 Elim') (GBB)

runners.name: Bourbon Girl
runners.spotlight: 11th foal; half-sister to useful hurdler Master Malt and two bumper winners; 10-1 for a four-runner bumper at Sedgefield (2m1f, good) in March but she ran well to finish a close second; that was a promising debut and she sets the standard on that form.
runners.comment: Close second on Sedgefield debut in March and she sets the standard on that form

runners.name: Magnifix Du Moulin
runners.spotlight: Close third in a 1m4f French bumper on soft ground in October when tongue tied; weak 9-1 shot on recent stable debut at Fontwell (2m1f, good) but she overcame some traffic issues before scoring with something in hand; has a penalty but she has a useful pedigree and looks a good prospect.
runners.comment: Ex-French 4yo who won on recent stable debut at Fontwell; respected under a penalty

runners.name: Lady Coco
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to winners Choccabloc (bumper/2m5f hurdle) and My Bobby's Lass (2m3f/2m4f hurdle); dam bumper/2m1f hurdle winner, closely related to 2m7f/3m1f hurdle winner As I See It, out of bumper/smart 2m-2m5f hurdle winner; yard has been firing in winners recently and she's an interesting newcomer.
runners.comment: Has several bumper winners in her pedigree and she's an interesting newcomer

runners.name: Maybellino
runners.spotlight: Eighth foal; half-sister to 2m3f hurdle winner Samtara; dam unraced sister to bumper and 2m6f-3m2f hurdle/chase winner Georgian King, half-sister to bumper/3m1f hurdle winner Madame Jasmine; yard 0-13 in bumpers last season and she could be one for later on.
runners.comment: Yard 0-13 in bumpers last season and she could be one for later on

runners.name: Molly Hastings
runners.spotlight: 30,000euros 3yo; fifth foal; half-sister to 2m/2m1f hurdle winner Telecon; dam placed 2m-2m2f hurdles/chase in France, sister to bumper/useful 2m-2m7f hurdle/chase winner The Polomoche, half-sister to useful 2m3f/2m6f hurdle winner Lessons In Milan; yard 3-47 in bumpers in recent years and others look more likely.
runners.comment: Yard 3-47 in bumpers in recent years and others look more likely

runners.name: Phantom Of Delight
runners.spotlight: Bought for £30,000 after winning an Irish point (soft/heavy) in January; sister to a smart bumper winner and she's also a half-sister to a useful 2m hurdler; plenty to like on pedigree and she needs a close in market on rules debut.
runners.comment: Point winner and she's a sister to smart bumper winner; needs close look on rules debut

runners.name: Anniespatient
runners.spotlight: 6,000euros foal; first foal; dam 2m hurdle winner, out of bumper/2m-2m5f hurdle winning sister to useful bumper and 2m3f-2m5f hurdle/chase winner Twelve Roses; yard not known for bumper winners and she could be a longer-term prospect.
runners.comment: 6,000euros foal; yard not known for bumper winners and she could be a longer-term prospect

runners.name: Bonnie Lazarevic
runners.spotlight: Third foal; dam useful and prolific bumper/2m-3m1f hurdle/chase winner, closely related to winners Letterpress (2m/2m1f hurdle) and Belle Empress (2m6f hurdle/3m6f chase), half-sister to useful 2m3f-2m5f chase winner Fistral Beach; has quite a bit of stamina in pedigree and market should guide on debut.
runners.comment: Has quite a bit of stamina in pedigree but she needs checking in market on debut

runners.name: Liberty Leader
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam 2m4f-2m5f hurdle/chase winner, half-sister to smart bumper and 2m hurdle/chase winner Libberty Hunter; has fair target to aim at on debut and is best watched unless market suggests otherwise.
runners.comment: Has fair target to aim at on debut and is best watched unless market suggests otherwise

runners.name: Pearl Knot
runners.spotlight: Third foal; half-sister to 3m chase winner Pearl Island; dam 2m6f-3m hurdle winner, sister to 3m-3m5f chase winner Bretney, closely related to useful 2m4f-4m hurdle/chase winner Captain Cattistock, family of top 2m chaser Pearlyman; yard 21% in bumpers in recent years and she needs watching in market on debut.
runners.comment: Yard 21% in bumpers in recent years and she needs watching in market on debut

runners.name: Do Wah Diddy
runners.spotlight: Second of five on Huntingdon debut (2m, good) in November 2024 but she was tailed off at Doncaster 18 days later; on the sidelines since and has something to prove back after a long absence.
runners.comment: Tailed off at Doncaster in her last run in 2024 and has something to prove on comeback

races.race_name: Cartmel Sticky Toffee Pudding Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Loch Cuan
runners.spotlight: Has made a positive start over hurdles, runner-up in maidens at Navan (2m) in February and Downpatrick (2m6f) 15 days ago; likely to progress further so in the mix.
runners.comment: Runner-up in maidens at Navan and Downpatrick; can progress further; in the mix

runners.name: Getaway Vic
runners.spotlight: Irish point/bumper scorer who made a promising start in this sphere when fourth of five in novice at Ffos Las (2m4f, good) 18 days ago, leading long way; cheekpieces added; has more to offer and rates a player.
runners.comment: Promising hurdling debut fourth at Ffos Las; has more to offer and rates a player

runners.name: Hawk's Rock
runners.spotlight: A fairly useful bumper performer; back from six months off when shaping well on his first go over hurdles to be third of seven in a novice at Downpatrick (2m6f, good) 15 days ago; can take a step forward in retained cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Shaped well on first go over hurdles when third at Downpatrick; can take a step forward

runners.name: Woodythewoodpecker
runners.spotlight: Remains winless but comes here in decent nick, having finished third of seven in 25.5f handicap at Hereford last month; place prospects once more back in maiden company.
runners.comment: Remains winless but comes here in decent nick; place prospects back in maiden company

runners.name: Fortunes Dawn
runners.spotlight: Maiden pointer who came in last of four in April; much more required on her hurdling debut.
runners.comment: Maiden pointer who came in last of four in April; much more required on her hurdling debut

runners.name: Lipstick Traces
runners.spotlight: Fair bumper performer who has shown similar form in this sphere, finishing third of nine in 2m Hexham novice three weeks ago; she's no forlorn hope in a refitted hood.
runners.comment: Solid third in 2m Hexham novice three weeks ago; she's no forlorn hope in a refitted hood

runners.name: Howsmenoftomorro
runners.spotlight: Maiden pointer who came in last of three finishers in April; goes hurdling now but others appeal more.
runners.comment: Maiden pointer; last of three finishers in April; goes hurdling but others appeal more

runners.name: Presentandcorrect
runners.spotlight: Fair form shown when third in Kelso bumper in February but came in last of eight in similar company there the following month; more needed now sent hurdling.
runners.comment: Last of eight in Kelso bumper latest; more is needed now sent hurdling

runners.name: Stavanger Rose
runners.spotlight: Failed to complete all four runs over hurdles for Daisy Hitchins, although she was still in third when falling at the last in 2m6f novice at Newton Abbot last month; changed hands for 2,000gns since and more required for her new handler.
runners.comment: Failed to complete in four hurdles runs for Daisy Hitchins; more required for new yard

runners.name: Raincloud
runners.spotlight: 0-19 and she was last to finish in a 2m5f handicap at Market Rasen last month; others more persuasive.
runners.comment: 0-19 and was last to finish in a 2m5f handicap at Market Rasen last month

runners.name: Road To Rosley
runners.spotlight: 0-9 and tried in cheekpieces when pulled up in 2m Wetherby handicap hurdle in February; it's easy to look elsewhere.
runners.comment: 0-9 and pulled up in 2m Wetherby h'cap hurdle in February; it's easy to look elsewhere

races.race_name: Hadwins EBF Mares' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Tour Ovalie
runners.spotlight: Resumed winning ways at Ludlow (2m) in December and in good form until finishing last of ten in 2m4f Sandown handicap four weeks ago; the sort who might bounce back though.
runners.comment: In good form until last of ten at Sandown four weeks ago; might bounce back though

runners.name: My Chiquita
runners.spotlight: A fairly useful 2m4f hurdles winner for Ben Brookhouse; made a promising start for her new yard after eight months off when third of eight over 2m4f at Aintree eight days ago; she should strip sharper here and rates a player off the same mark.
runners.comment: Promising start for new yard with Aintree third (2m4f) last week; player off the same mark

runners.name: Demoiselle Kap
runners.spotlight: Bagged a four-timer (around 2m) over hurdles/fences last winter; not at her best of late though and finished a remote second in 2m Hexham seller three weeks ago on her final run for Jennie Candlish; more needed in refitted cheekpieces for her new handler.
runners.comment: Not at her best of late, remote Hexham second final run for Jennie Candlish; more needed

runners.name: Belle Le Grand
runners.spotlight: Back on the up (in first-time cheekpieces) when easily landing a gamble in 2m1f handicap at Newton Abbot 17 days ago; up 7lb but runner-up has gone in since so this C&D winner merits serious consideration in retained headgear.
runners.comment: Easily landed gamble at Newton Abbot latest; this C&D winner merits serious consideration

runners.name: Magnolia
runners.spotlight: A dual 2m hurdles winner at Kelso last winter and back in good nick of late, finishing third of six at Perth (2m) nine days ago; not out of things off a 2lb lower mark.
runners.comment: Back in good nick of late, third at Perth nine days ago; not out of things eased 2lb

runners.name: Dunsy Rock
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at just the second attempt in 2m Fairyhouse maiden in February and not disgraced when fifth in 2m1f Listed event at Killarney two weeks ago; remains with potential now going handicapping.
runners.comment: Respectable fifth at Killarney (Listed) latest; remains with potential on handicap debut

runners.name: Winds Of Winter
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D scorer in first half of 2025; lightly raced since but she took a step back in the right direction when fourth at Hexham (2m) three weeks ago; possibilities off a slipping mark.
runners.comment: Dual C&D scorer and step back in right direction with Hexham fourth latest; possibilities

runners.name: Kientzheim
runners.spotlight: Has made a positive start for her current yard, scoring at Market Rasen (2m4f) in November and backing it up with a good second of 11 at Carlisle (2m1f) in February; well in the mix after a break with few miles on the clock.
runners.comment: Low-mileage mare; good second at Carlisle in February; well in the mix after a break

runners.name: Melinda
runners.spotlight: Made a winning start over hurdles in 2m Musselburgh juvenile in November 2024; had been off for 17 months before coming in last of five on Newcastle AW (1m4f) 22 days ago; needs to take a big step forward.
runners.comment: Off for 17 months before finishing last on Newcastle AW recently; needs a big step forward

runners.name: Zahra Dodville
runners.spotlight: Ex-French filly; scored easily on her debut for James Owen in 2m Fakenham juvenile hurdle in October and backed it up with a good third of 12 in Listed event at Newbury (2m) the following month; off since but yard continues to go very well so she must enter calculations.
runners.comment: Easy winner at Fakenham; good third at Newbury (Listed) so enters calculations after break

races.race_name: PSR Marquees Mares' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Starlyte
runners.spotlight: Few secrets left after 41 hurdles starts but back below her last winning mark again, and every outing so far this year has suggested that an extended run of races beyond 3m looks in order now; not without interest.
runners.comment: Back below her last winning mark, and step up back beyond 3m looks the right move.

runners.name: Tread Softly Now
runners.spotlight: Triple winner at up to 2m4f (soft and good); inconsistency tempers confidence in her backing up last month's Perth near-miss (2m4f, good; first-time visor retained), but has staying potential on breeding.
runners.comment: No certainty to back up Perth near-miss; encouragement for stayers' trips on breeding.

runners.name: Penny Perfect
runners.spotlight: Looked like one for stayers' trips when taking a Downpatrick 2m6f maiden last summer (good), but unable to confirm that impression on her one run after; much the longest absent of these, and her mark is no gift.
runners.comment: 2m6f maiden winner who didn't improve for 3m latest as expected; off since October.

runners.name: Keep On Cobbling
runners.spotlight: Well served by sharp left-handed tracks and good ground, winning at Stratford (2m maiden) and Sedgefield (2m5f handicap) last year; impression given when second at Warwick latest (2m5f) was that she'll stay further and that is partly backed up by pedigree.
runners.comment: Sharp left-handed track and good ground are positives; may stay this far.

runners.name: Musique De Fee
runners.spotlight: 2l third in this race (good) last year for Georgina Nicholls when undone by two late mistakes, only to land a C&D Class 2 two days later (good to soft) off the same mark as today's; easily her best effort for new connections at Hereford latest (3m2f, good), and her turn is possibly nearing.
runners.comment: 2l third in 2025 renewal before winning a C&D Class 2 two days later; turn nearing again.

runners.name: Bittalemon
runners.spotlight: Dual 2m1f novice winner here last summer (good to soft and good); well below that level twice after, albeit once in Listed company; effectiveness beyond 2m3f has yet to be tested.
runners.comment: Dual 2m1f novice winner here last summer; effectiveness beyond 2m3f has yet to be tested.

runners.name: Pinot Rouge
runners.spotlight: Below par since last June's Hexham win off 2lb higher (3m, good to soft) until reviving, up to a point, with last month's Ayr Class 2 fourth; has to show that a sharp track is what's wanted, though.
runners.comment: Bit better again at Ayr last time; wouldn't be convinced that Cartmel is her track.

races.race_name: William Hill Novices' Limited Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Tellherthename
runners.spotlight: Both wins came at Huntingdon when trained by Ben Pauling; made his stable debut in the County Hurdle at the Cheltenham Festival, when not beaten far, and he wasn't disgraced when hooded and held up out the back in the Swinton at Haydock (favourite) two weeks ago; this will feel like a drop in the ocean in comparison and, although turned over at a short price last October in his only chase, he should make light work of these two rivals on his return to fences.
runners.comment: Pretty useful in two hurdle runs (County/Swinton) for new yard; big chance back chasing

runners.name: Half A Chance
runners.spotlight: Sole success came in a Limerick maiden hurdle in May 2024; placed several times since and made a promising debut over fences when fourth of 12 at Wexford (2m, good to yielding) 16 days ago; should win races but up against a serious rival here.
runners.comment: Made a promising debut over fences when fourth at Wexford 16 days ago.

runners.name: Tamarind Bay
runners.spotlight: Still a maiden despite being placed in eight of his 12 starts over hurdles; may do better now that he tackles fences.
runners.comment: Still a maiden despite being placed in eight of his 12 starts over hurdles.

races.race_name: RF Miller & Co Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Stratagem
runners.spotlight: Useful chaser at his best for Paul Nicholls; in good form this spring for his current yard since undergoing wind surgery, scoring at Sedgefield (3m2f) before finishing a good fourth over 3m6f at Perth when set a lot to do; can go well eased 1lb here.
runners.comment: Won at Sedgefield before good Perth fourth when set a lot to do; down 1lb; can go well

runners.name: Loup De Maulde
runners.spotlight: Scored twice over fences (around 3m) in March 2024; back from two years off in good nick, runner-up over 3m2f at Chepstow last month; can race off the same mark here so he's a player with Harry Cobden taking over.
runners.comment: Back from two years off in good nick, Chepstow second latest; player with Harry Cobden up

runners.name: Puddlesinthepark
runners.spotlight: A three-time scorer (including over C&D) last season; hinted at a revival when a never-nearer fifth over 3m4f at Taunton last month so needs considering off a slipping mark.
runners.comment: Hinted at revival when fifth at Taunton last month; considered off a slipping mark

runners.name: Loughderg Rocco
runners.spotlight: Very lightly raced but fairly useful hurdles/chase winner for Laura Morgan in 2021/22; just two runs since back from huge absence for this yard and not totally disgraced when seventh over 3m at Ffos Las (soft) in March, prominent to three out; had wind surgery since; tongue-tie now added.
runners.comment: Not totally disgraced when seventh at Ffos Las in March; tongue-tie is added now

runners.name: You Some Girl
runners.spotlight: Made a winning start over fences at Newcastle (2m7f) in October; has failed to build on it though and she unseated her rider at Hexham (3m) two weeks ago; has a bit to prove.
runners.comment: Made a winning start over fences at Newcastle in October but she has failed to build on it

runners.name: My Gift To You
runners.spotlight: Resumed winning ways after six months off in 4m1f hunter chase at Warwick (good) 22 days ago; effective over this much shorter trip and this likeable front-runner is not out of things.
runners.comment: Won 4m1f hunter chase at Warwick latest; this likeable front-runner is not out of things

runners.name: Ki Woo
runners.spotlight: Gained a first win over fences at Haydock (3m4f, good to soft) in March and posted a solid fifth off a 6lb higher mark there (good) last month; possibilities.
runners.comment: Won 3m4f Haydock chase in March and solid fifth of 11  there last month; possibilities

runners.name: Caughtinyourtrance
runners.spotlight: A two-time hurdles winner here last summer; started well over fences this spring when placed at Newcastle (2m7f) and Uttoxeter (2m4f) but pulled up at Ayr last month; has since had wind surgery and no surprise to see him bounce back here.
runners.comment: Started well over fences but pulled up at Ayr latest; had a wind op and could bounce back

races.race_name: Molson Coors Handicap Chase

runners.name: Defence Witness
runners.spotlight: Justified favouritism over 2m5f on this card last year (good to soft), and added a Catterick stayers' event in the winter, both off 4lb lower; all wins on good to soft and soft but last month's Sedgefield second on good was the equal of his peak soft-ground form; chance.
runners.comment: Won the 2m5f chase on this card last year; winless on good but handles it if necessary.

runners.name: Halfway House Lad
runners.spotlight: 5lb out of the weights when romping home on recent stable debut at Hexham (3m, good); now 8lb higher and all wins have been gained at Newcastle or Hexham so still has some convincing to do around a sharp track.
runners.comment: All wins have come at Newcastle or Hexham, including on stable debut latest (3m, good).

runners.name: Isle Of Sark
runners.spotlight: Posted best effort for these connections when third at Sedgefield in April (2m5f, good), before appearing to find it harder work last time; mixed record over stayers' trips, and would appreciate the ground drying out.
runners.comment: Didn't build on previous good Sedgefield third latest; mixed record over stayers' trips.

runners.name: Thehairyfella
runners.spotlight: Had a below-par Defence Witness behind when a C&D third off today's mark last June, and belatedly off the mark next time out back in Ireland; can go well after a break without necessarily being able to threaten a win.
runners.comment: C&D third last June was followed by a belated first career win; can give a fair account.

runners.name: Kaviar Wood
runners.spotlight: Rarely a strong finisher in points or sub-3m hurdles previously but still full of running in the lead when falling at the last on February's Hereford chase debut (3m1f, heavy); up 2lb without winning but given time to recover, and despite the bigger field size this is race is no better.
runners.comment: Last-fence faller when still leading on February's chase debut (3m1f, heavy); up 2lb.

runners.name: Mistymal
runners.spotlight: Form of October 2024's Hereford chase debut win (3m1f, good) has been consolidated rather than improved upon; fair record after long absences but realistically needs the wind surgery since last seen to elicit improvement.
runners.comment: Hasn't kicked on from 2024's chase debut win (3m1f, good); has received wind surgery.

runners.name: Jakana
runners.spotlight: Back on track to a certain extent when fourth switched to fences at Perth latest (3m, good), shaping as if further still is required; longer trip all to the good and is now 20lb lower than this time last year.
runners.comment: 12l fourth on last month's Perth chasing debut, shaping as if needing further than 3m.

runners.name: Jet Light
runners.spotlight: Still early days in handicaps, but fences and cheekpieces (retained nonetheless) elicited no improvement on recent return from a year out.
runners.comment: Still early days, but no improvement latest for fences and application of cheekpieces.

runners.name: Croagh Patrick
runners.spotlight: Back on the lowly mark off which he snapped a long losing run in December 2024 at Carlisle (3m2f, soft); remains to be seen what ability remains on this stable debut following 14 months out.
runners.comment: Off for 14 months; back on the mark he defied at Carlisle in December 2024 (3m2f, soft).

runners.name: Fast Deal
runners.spotlight: Approaching two years since he last showed any form to speak of, and has a lot to prove from 9lb out of the weights this time.
runners.comment: Approaching two years since he last showed any form to speak of; 9lb out of the weights.

runners.name: Lights Are Green
runners.spotlight: C&D record of 2-7 includes victory in this race in 2023 off 5lb higher (good); pulled up in last year's renewal, however, and other results last term offer scant optimism on this return from seven months off.
runners.comment: 2023 race winner off 5lb higher (good); struggled badly here and elsewhere last season.

races.race_name: St Marys Hospice Handicap Chase

runners.name: Line Of Descent
runners.spotlight: 2m1f chase win off today's mark in 2023 is his contribution to the Greatrex yard's 40% strike-rate with chasers here; mostly solid efforts this year since returning from absence, and the slight rise back up in trip is a likely plus having found it all happening too quickly at Plumpton latest (cheekpieces added, retained).
runners.comment: 2m1f course winner off today's mark (and off higher elsewhere); solid efforts this year.

runners.name: Of Corse I Can
runners.spotlight: Back-to-back 3m2f wins at Fontwell and Newton Abbot last October (good and good to soft) suggest he'll enjoy this sharp track but the drop back from a stayers' trip doesn't rate an obvious plus.
runners.comment: Track and going fine, but Fontwell and Newton Abbot wins last October came over 3m2f.

runners.name: Jack's A Legend
runners.spotlight: Only seen twice since making all in a 2m4f open point under Imogen Mathias one year ago, and would have run the winner closer than around 3l at Newton Abbot latest (2m5f, good to soft) but for whacking two out; 1lb rise is very fair.
runners.comment: Upped only 1lb for Newton Abbot third latest that was better than the bare form; chance.

runners.name: Judicial Review
runners.spotlight: Course record of 3-8 includes the odd flop, but also two C&D successes, the latest off 1lb lower last August; hasn't offered anything elsewhere since, and much depends on this place inspiring him again.
runners.comment: No form anywhere else since his most recent C&D success off 1lb lower last August.

runners.name: Italian Spirit
runners.spotlight: Uneven chasing profile looks better once pared down to just efforts at or around 2m4f; chance on the form of his last-time Ffos Las second off 1lb lower, though jumping late on wasn't all it could have been.
runners.comment: Enters reckoning on form of Ffos Las second latest (2m3f); needs to jump better late on.

runners.name: Imperial Rule
runners.spotlight: Triple scorer over just shy of this trip during 2024 (good and good to soft), and now back below his last winning mark; needs forgiving a pair of weakening efforts around Sedgefield most recently, however.
runners.comment: Back below latest winning mark; weakening efforts in 2 Sedgefield runs need forgiving.

runners.name: Blazing Court
runners.spotlight: 2m4f Worcester chase winner off 11lb higher in August 2024, but over a year since showing much over either type of obstacle.
runners.comment: 11lb below winning mark, but just over a year since he last showed much of interest

runners.name: Cardamon Hill
runners.spotlight: Three listless performances since returning from 13 months out; 12lb below his last winning mark now, but no good thing to capitalise.
runners.comment: Poor 2026 so far; 12lb below his last winning mark now, but no good thing to capitalise.

runners.name: Kingofthegame
runners.spotlight: 0-10 and lasted only three fences in a debuted visor (discarded) latest; saw out 3m well enough on the latter of two Irish point starts, but yet to hit the line strongly over 2m3f or beyond under rules.
runners.comment: Early faller latest; ability lurks, but yet to hit the line strongly over 2m3f or beyond.

runners.name: Clonakilty
runners.spotlight: Well treated on 2024 winning form for Olly Murphy; last-time 11l fourth at Sedgefield (2m5f, good) rates a best for his current yard, but he can't afford to repeat that day's constant jumping right.
runners.comment: 15lb below his last winning mark of 2024; jumped constantly to the right last time out.

runners.name: Zumbi
runners.spotlight: Fourth (2023) and eighth (2024) in this race previously, and winless for exactly four years now; place prospects only, therefore, a return to form at Newcastle latest notwithstanding.
runners.comment: Back-to-form Newcastle second latest; winless in four years; twice well held in this race.

runners.name: Walkinthecotswolds
runners.spotlight: Now 9lb below the mark of her sole hurdling success (2m3f, heavy), but aptitude for chasing remains unproven, falling mid-race on one attempt and racing too freely on the other.
runners.comment: 9lb below the mark of her sole hurdling win, but yet to prove herself over fences.

races.race_name: Whitsbury Manor Stud EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands A/B/C/D) (Qual') (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Call Me Tomorrow
runners.spotlight: Shaped well when second of eight at Newmarket's Craven meeting (5f, good) but she failed to back it up at the Guineas meeting (raced too freely); if she returns to the debut form it will take a useful newcomer to beat her but she will need to settle better.
runners.comment: Debut effort sets the standard but she failed to build on it at the Guineas meeting

runners.name: Belardo Ace
runners.spotlight: 3,800gns foal, 25,000gns yearling; dam, placed over 5f (RPR 63), is a half-sister to smart sprint winners Tis Marvellous (5f-6f Group 2) and Mythmaker (6f); represents a powerful stable
runners.comment: 25,000gns yearling; dam a half-sister to some smart sprinters; top yard; check betting

runners.name: Seasail
runners.spotlight: 7,500gns yearling; fourth foal; half-brother to 6f winner Margorie (RPR 66) out of a 5.7f 2yo winner; stable not renowned for winning newcomers.
runners.comment: 7,500gns half-brother to a minor 6f winner; stable not known for winning 2yo newcomers

runners.name: Sonado
runners.spotlight: 40,000gns yearling; second foal of a maiden half-sister to three useful winners; stable off the mark with 2yos and this one looks a likely type on paper.
runners.comment: 40,000gns yearling; dam a maiden from a useful family; market instructive

runners.name: My Maria
runners.spotlight: 50,000gns breeze-up 2yo by Persian Force; half-sister to winners Ippotheos (6f AW 2yo; RPR 84) and Jr Climbs (5.7f 2yo; 81); dam 6f winner (including 2yo; 93); stable's 2yos have made a bright start to the season and she is more appealing than many.
runners.comment: 50,000gns breeze-up 2yo; half-sister to two sprint winners; yard's 2yos going well

runners.name: He's Readytorumble
runners.spotlight: Seventh foal; brother to Listed-placed 5f winner Getreadytorumble (RPR 106), half-brother to winners Desperate Hero (Group-placed 5f/6f including 2yo/UAE; 113) and Whistledown (6f/7f including 2yo; 76); dam placed 5f/6f (including 2yo/AW; 76); lots to like on paper and he is an appealing newcomer.
runners.comment: Comes from a good sprinting family; no surprise to see him in the thick of the action

runners.name: Shimmering Sun
runners.spotlight: £100,000 yearling; first foal of a maiden half-sister to 6f Group 3 winner Shouldvebeenaring; stable not prolific with 2yos but she makes enough paper appeal to warrant a look in the betting.
runners.comment: £100,000 yearling; dam a maiden half-sister to a 6f Group 3 winner; betting should guide

runners.name: Goldtrader
runners.spotlight: First foal of a 6f/7f winner (including 2yo; RPR 80), herself a half-sister to two winners; stable have had a winning newcomer this year; betting can guide.
runners.comment: First foal of 6f/7f winner (inc 2yo; RPR 80); yard have winning newcomers; check betting

runners.name: Viking Barbie
runners.spotlight: 5-1 third favourite for her Yarmouth debut last month (6f, good to firm), a race where she dropped away in the final furlong; the second has done well since and she could take a step forward now dropped to 5f.
runners.comment: Showed speed before weakening over 6f on debut; can do better back at 5f

runners.name: Courseyoudo
runners.spotlight: Only 9-1 for his York debut ten days ago (5f, good) but he dropped right away in the closing stages; presumably felt capable of better.
runners.comment: Only 9-1 for York debut but finished well beaten; presumably felt capable of much better

races.race_name: Taste The World Handicap

runners.name: Kodi Fire
runners.spotlight: Landed a three-timer last summer, including one over C&D; should strip fitter following his recent fifth of 11 at Windsor (1m, good to firm) but may need to come down the weights.
runners.comment: Landed a three-timer last summer, including one over C&D.

runners.name: Great Dream
runners.spotlight: Won an AW maiden at Newcastle in September before coming up short in a Listed contest at Redcar; finished off the year with a third in a York handicap (7f, good to soft) in October; has since left the Crisfords and has been gelded; must come into consideration on his debut for an in-form yard.
runners.comment: Won an AW maiden at Newcastle in September and then finished third in a York handicap.

runners.name: Dovey Moon
runners.spotlight: Won three times last year; should be sharper following his seasonal return when seventh of 12 at Wetherby (1m, good to firm) last month but is 6lb higher than his last winning mark and may need to come down a few pounds.
runners.comment: Won three times last year; should be sharper following his seasonal return.

runners.name: Happy Banner
runners.spotlight: Sole success came in a Kempton AW novice in December 2024; has been beaten in eight handicaps since, latest when a place behind Nakaaha when third of six at Bath (1m, firm) last month; no margin for error off this mark but should find a race soon.
runners.comment: Sole success came at Kempton in December 2024; has been beaten in eight handicaps since.

runners.name: Silca Bay
runners.spotlight: All three wins have came at Kempton AW (7f/1m); caught out wide and was found to have an irregular heartbeat after finishing last back there last time; may be best to wait until he returns to the Sunbury venue.
runners.comment: All three wins have came at Kempton AW; may be best to wait until he returns there.

runners.name: Champagne Powder
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark in a Chelmsford novice (1m, AW) in December; has excuses for his last two defeats as he pulled too hard at Newcastle in February and missed the break at Kempton last time; tongue-tie returns to combine with blinkers and not out of this if things pan out for him.
runners.comment: Got off the mark in a Chelmsford novice in December; has excuses for his last two defeats.
runners.owner: S Jones, P Burrell & P F I Cole Ltd

runners.name: Cristo
runners.spotlight: Both wins have been at Chelmsford AW (1m2f) last spring; progression has stalled this year but handicap mark has reflected that and he finished third of eight at Lingfield (1m2f, AW) two weeks ago; his turn is near but not sure drop back to 1m will be in his favour.
runners.comment: Both wins have been at Chelmsford AW last spring; progression has stalled this year.

runners.name: Glistening
runners.spotlight: Off the mark on her stable debut at Kempton (1m, AW) in February; got caught out wide when ninth of 11 on her handicap debut at Southwell subsequently and probably ran up to her best when beating only one rival in a Listed contest at Kempton (1m) in March in first-time cheekpieces (retained); still unexposed and back at her level here.
runners.comment: Won at Kempton in February; outclassed in Listed company in March.

runners.name: Nakaaha
runners.spotlight: Scored at Ffos Las and Newbury last summer; ran her best race since when runner-up at Bath (1m, firm) last month; holds place claims.
runners.comment: Scored at Ffos Las and Newbury last summer; runner-up at Bath last month.

runners.name: Aspull
runners.spotlight: Made a winning debut at this track (7f, good to firm) last July and wasn't beaten far subsequently in a couple of similar races, and on his handicap/AW debut; has been gelded since and still unexposed and may have more to offer.
runners.comment: Made a winning debut at this track last July and wasn't beaten far subsequently.

runners.name: Tanmawwy
runners.spotlight: All six wins on turf over 6f/7f; steps up in trip on this return from a 556-day layoff; best watched.
runners.comment: All six wins on turf over 6f/7f; steps up in trip on this return from a 556-day layoff.

races.race_name: Whitsbury Manor Stud Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Golden Brown
runners.spotlight: Finished second in the Redcar Two-Year-Old Trophy (6f, good to soft; Listed) last October, form that would give him strong claims in this field; beaten as 4-9 favourite at Lingfield on his reappearance (6f, AW) but the winner there could be useful; should be involved once again.
runners.comment: Second in Listed Two-Year-Old Trophy last October; beaten at 4-9 on return; contender

runners.name: Upper Hendersyde
runners.spotlight: 65,000gns foal, 220,000gns yearling; first foal of a maiden from a good sprinting family; enough paper appeal to warrant a market check.
runners.comment: 220,000gns yearling; dam a maiden from a good sprinting family; betting to guide

runners.name: Charlie Boyo
runners.spotlight: Close second here on last May's debut (5f, good to firm); raced too freely when beaten at odds-on at Nottingham last June; gelded since; not yet exposed and he's one to take seriously back from his absence.
runners.comment: Good 2nd here on debut 12 months ago (5f); below par one month later; gelded; unexposed

runners.name: Lucky Luna
runners.spotlight: 40,000gns yearling; eighth foal; closely related to 1m2f winner Capla Lazarus (RPR 85), half-sister to French winners Salt Lake City (5f-1m; 97) and Flying Fleur (1m1f); dam unraced half-sister to Listed winners Wilki (6f 2yo) and Madhya (1m); bred for further but still worth a market check.
runners.comment: 40,000gns yearling; three winning siblings; bred for further but worth a market check

runners.name: Vichenza
runners.spotlight: Third foal; half-sister to 6f/7f winner Zoum Zoum (including 2yo/AW/Listed; RPR 113); dam a 6f winner (including Listed; 104); lots to like on paper and she's a newcomer of some interest.
runners.comment: Bred for the job, half-sister to Listed winner out of Listed winner; interesting newcomer

runners.name: Speransky
runners.spotlight: 52,000euros yearling; second foal of a 6f AW 2yo winner (RPR 76); has a fair standard to aim at on debut and only of interest if attracting market support.
runners.comment: 52,000euros yearling; dam a 6f AW 2yo winner; fair standard to aim at on debut

runners.name: Whitesnake
runners.spotlight: She has run to a modest level in two 6f runs eight months apart; handicaps should provide more suitable opportunities.
runners.comment: Modest form in two 6f runs eight months apart; minor handicaps should be more suitable

runners.name: Imagine That
runners.spotlight: Just modest form in two 6f AW runs 222 days apart; a big step forward is essential.
runners.comment: Just modest form in two 6f AW runs 222 days apart; a big step forward is essential

runners.name: Fozzy Osbourne
runners.spotlight: First foal of a 5f winner (RPR 75); not easily recommended on his belated racecourse debut.
runners.comment: First foal of a 5f winner (RPR 75); not easily recommended on his belated racecourse debut

runners.name: Red Moon
runners.spotlight: Down the field in two 7f novices, the latest at Newbury eight days ago; a big step forward is required.
runners.comment: Down the field in two 7f novices, the latest eight days ago; a big step forward is needed

runners.name: Lady V
runners.spotlight: 66-1, slowly away and never in the hunt on her Wolverhampton debut (6f; for Paul Attwater) last October; may leave that well behind in time.
runners.comment: 66-1, green and never in the hunt in one 2yo run for Paul Attwater; may do much better

runners.name: Sheik Yerbouti
runners.spotlight: 150-1 and offered little on his belated debut at Kempton (1m, AW) in February.
runners.comment: 150-1 and offered little on his belated debut at Kempton (1m, AW) in February

races.race_name: British Stallion Studs EBF Cathedral Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: Cool Hoof Luke
runners.spotlight: Won the Gimcrack as a 2yo (Group 2; form worked out well); better than ever on AW earlier in the year, making most in a 7f Listed event at Wolverhampton in March; 15-2 fourth favourite for a York Group 2 ten days ago but found nothing when asked for his effort, eventually coming home in his own time; has the ability to go well despite a penalty but he does have a point to prove all of a sudden.
runners.comment: 7f Listed win in March; ran no sort of race in York Group 2 ten days ago; risky after that

runners.name: Cinque Verde
runners.spotlight: The winner of ten of her 39 starts, notably when scampering clear of ten rivals in a Class 3 fillies' handicap at Newmarket three weeks ago; this represents a much stiffer assignment.
runners.comment: Good strike-rate and won well at Newmarket three weeks ago; this is much tougher

runners.name: Prince Of India
runners.spotlight: Made good progress over 6f last season; better than ever with his second at Leicester four weeks ago (6f, good to firm); still has more to give but this looks deep for a Listed contest.
runners.comment: Progressive over 6f last year; better than ever when 2nd at Leicester last month; chance

runners.name: Almeraq
runners.spotlight: Promising in 6f novice events, winning at Yarmouth on second occasion (soft ground), but it was his runaway Ayr win last July that stamped him as a potential top notcher; still in contention for a York Listed event when clipping heels and taking an horrific fall in September; he remains a sprinter of considerable interest but having a positive experience back on the racecourse may be the number one priority today.
runners.comment: Looked high class at Ayr last July; absent since taking terrible fall at York in September

runners.name: Hollywood Treasure
runners.spotlight: Won her first two starts, notably a 5f Listed event at Newbury; improved again when second of nine in a 6f Listed race at Goodwood three weeks ago (fillies' only) on her reappearance; not fully exposed and 3yos, including three fillies, have won six of the last ten runnings.
runners.comment: Unexposed 3yo; good second at Goodwood (6f, Listed) on her reappearance; contender

runners.name: Soldier's Tree
runners.spotlight: Improved with each of his three runs for M J Tynan in Ireland, most recently finishing second of ten in a Cork Listed race seven weeks ago (6f, soft); unexposed as a sprinter and it will be interesting to see if there is market confidence behind him as he starts out for James Owen.
runners.comment: Unexposed after three runs, second in Listed company last month; interesting on yard debut

runners.name: Spy Chief
runners.spotlight: Highly tried after trouncing Almeraq in a Yarmouth novice (6f, good to firm; receiving 7lb) last June, finishing second in the Jersey Stakes nine days later and not beaten far in the July Cup the following month; finished his 3yo campaign quietly but possible he'll kick on again this year.
runners.comment: Highly tried after wide-margin defeat of Almeraq last June and results were mixed; chance

runners.name: Zoum Zoum
runners.spotlight: Just missed out in this race on his reappearance 12 months ago (good to firm); ended 2025 with two good second-place finishes in Listed company at Doncaster and Southwell; each-way shout once again.
runners.comment: Close second in this race last year; ended 2025 with two good Listed runs; each-way shout

runners.name: Flash Harry
runners.spotlight: Won two 6f events last summer, notably a Goodwood handicap (good to to firm; RPR 106) in August; not at the same level in the Ayr Gold Cup when last seen but there should be more to come this year.
runners.comment: Two 6f wins last year; absent since finishing down the field in Ayr Gold Cup; low mileage

runners.name: Oxted
runners.spotlight: Top-class sprinter in his youth with the 2020 July Cup and the 2021 King's Stand Stakes featuring amongst his five wins; retired due to injury in 2024 but returned to training this year; while it would be a fine story were he to come out on top, it is asking an awful lot of him to take this after 1,778 days on the sidelines.
runners.comment: Dual Group 1 winner in prime; retired in 2024 but now back in action after 1,778 days off
runners.owner: S Teal, S Piper & J Collins

races.race_name: Carty Builders 'Proud To Restore The Rubbing House' Handicap

runners.name: Starlight Sami
runners.spotlight: Five-race maiden but placed in four of them, including when a close third at Lingfield on last month's seasonal debut (7f, AW); that run can be upgraded (forced wide) and he should have no issue with the return to turf; more appealing than many.
runners.comment: 0-5 but has shown plenty of promise; reappearance 3rd can be marked up; capable of better

runners.name: Mojito
runners.spotlight: Left her 2yo efforts behind her when running away with a Class 6 handicap at Doncaster on her reappearance last month (second won since); up 6lb but could well have more in the tank.
runners.comment: Ran away with a 7f handicap at Doncaster last month; up 6lb but far from exposed

runners.name: Archer's Grace
runners.spotlight: Improved with each of her three runs in fillies' novice events, getting off the mark at Lingfield last month (7f); not obviously thrown in on that effort but her pedigree and profile do point to further progress being highly likely.
runners.comment: Improving run by run and pedigree/profile suggest further progress is highly likely
runners.owner: Fred Archer Racing - Wheel Of Fortune II

runners.name: Robert Anstruther
runners.spotlight: Didn't run badly on his seasonal return last month (first start after a gelding operation) but he is only 1lb lower and needs to raise his game a notch.
runners.comment: Ran okay on seasonal return last month but he will need more to come out on top here

runners.name: Swiped
runners.spotlight: Beaten odds-on favourite on his debut last summer (6f, good to firm); well beaten twice since but he could leave those efforts behind him now handicapping in blinkers.
runners.comment: Not lived up to market billing in maiden/novice company; goes handicapping in headgear

runners.name: Footstepinthewoods
runners.spotlight: No immediate improvement for the switch to handicaps at Windsor 19 days ago (6f, good to firm); dropped 3lb for his troubles but others still appeal more.
runners.comment: No improvement for handicapping (6f) 19 days ago; down 3lb but improvement essential

runners.name: Lovers Leap
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the fourth attempt when easily seeing off four rivals in a fillies' maiden at Beverley 11 days ago (7.4f, good to firm; 2-13f); this is more demanding but she should have more to come.
runners.comment: Easy winner of small-field 7.4f fillies' maiden at Beverley 11 days ago; may do better

runners.name: Decem Starr
runners.spotlight: Five-race maiden; not beaten far on his handicap debut three weeks ago (1m, good to firm; cheekpieces first time) but he will need to raise his game to come out on top here.
runners.comment: Five-race maiden; not beaten far on recent handicap debut but needs more to take this

runners.name: Doodling
runners.spotlight: Similar form in three runs in maiden/novice company; should have more to offer now handicapping but opening mark doesn't look obviously generous.
runners.comment: Could improve now handicapping but she will need to if she is to defy this opening mark

runners.name: Grimese's Special
runners.spotlight: Won a C&D seller on heavy ground last September; two heavy defeats in AW handicaps have followed and she is easy enough to swerve back from a break.
runners.comment: C&D win came in a heavy-ground seller; tailed off in two AW handicaps since

runners.name: White Ladder
runners.spotlight: Improved once tackling nurseries/wearing cheekpieces for Archie Watson last season, finishing second in 6f events at Doncaster and Southwell; sold for 15,000gns last October; up in trip on stable debut but goes without the headgear.
runners.comment: Improved for nurseries/cheekpieces last summer; up in trip/no headgear on stable debut

runners.name: Speculative
runners.spotlight: Recorded RPR in the 60s in two 7f fillies' events at Newbury last summer; pulled hard back from a break at Kempton 19 days ago; she will need to settle better now handicapping but does still have some potential.
runners.comment: Two runs last summer were encouraging; pulled hard on seasonal return; unexposed

runners.name: Jackabi
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in his first two nurseries (five and four ran respectively); too free when last of four at Newcastle when last seen in December; into a much deeper race now back on turf.
runners.comment: Ran okay in AW nurseries in late 2025 but this is much more competitive; absent 158 days

runners.name: Iconic Dawn
runners.spotlight: Displayed promise in a couple of 6f AW events last September; 28-1 for her handicap debut at Lingfield in October and finished last of the 14 runners; still has time to do better.
runners.comment: Low-key handicap debut last October but could prove a different proposition this year

races.race_name: Symonds Event Catering 'Confined' Handicap (For Horses Which Have Won No More Than One Race)

runners.name: Raspoutine
runners.spotlight: Duly stepped up on his 2yo efforts when winning a Class 6 handicap in good style at Lingfield last month (1m, AW); beaten a nose at Southwell under a penalty nine days later (also 1m; winner gone in again since) and he a further 6lb higher today; stepping up to 1m2f looks sure to prompt further progress though.
runners.comment: Two improved runs at 1m last month; up in weights but he has more to come at 1m2f

runners.name: Too Hot To Tango
runners.spotlight: Consistent filly; ran her best race to date when a close third at Goodwood three weeks ago (1m2f, good); this looks deeper and she will need the new tongue tie to have a positive effect.
runners.comment: Consistent maiden; good third at Goodwood (1m2f, good) three weeks ago; now tongue tied

runners.name: Fahidi
runners.spotlight: Promise at up to 1m in three maiden/novice runs on good or faster ground; cheekpieces enlisted prior to this handicap debut; new trip should suit and he is one to take seriously.
runners.comment: Up in trip and tried in headgear on this handicap debut; open to improvement

runners.name: Mayaada
runners.spotlight: 7f maiden win at Epsom last summer (only four ran); well beaten on her reappearance but she fared better when second of six back at Epsom last month (8.5f, good); stamina for this trip has to be taken on trust and less-exposed rivals may have her measure.
runners.comment: Fair effort at Epsom (8.5f) latest but the new, longer trip isn't sure to suit; vulnerable

runners.name: Semper Femina
runners.spotlight: Shaped well when second at Kempton on debut (1m3f) but that form hasn't really worked out and she has been well beaten at big odds twice since; may tap back into that initial promise now handicapping but others hold stronger claims here.
runners.comment: Not built on debut promise in 2 subsequent runs; moves into handicaps with a bit to prove

runners.name: Bnaider
runners.spotlight: Promise as a 2yo and again upped to 1m2f for his handicap debut at Sandown four weeks ago; carried his head awkwardly (possibly still green) but was well on top at the finish in Haydock event (1m2f, good to soft; dropped to Class 5) yesterday and looks likely to improve further; respected under 6lb penalty, assuming he turns out quickly.
runners.comment: Well on top at the finish in Haydock event yesterday and looks likely to improve further

runners.name: Melody De Vega
runners.spotlight: She has shown promise at up to 8.6f on AW; the longer trip and switch to turf aren't enough to earn her the verdict here though.
runners.comment: Promise at up to 8.6f on AW; new trip/surface aren't enough to tempt
runners.owner: Michael P Tudor & J P M O'Connor

runners.name: Tough Date
runners.spotlight: Down the field in two Class 5 handicaps this month (1m, good to firm and 1m2f, good); dropped 3lb and now tried in cheekpieces but others look much stronger.
runners.comment: Down the field in two handicap runs this month; new cheekpieces not enough to tempt

runners.name: What's The Plan
runners.spotlight: 7f AW winner in September; no significant impact in two handicaps but stepping up in trip could help this well-related 3yo; inexperienced apprentice booked.
runners.comment: Bred to be useful and stepping up in trip could help; inexperienced apprentice booked

runners.name: Terranoble
runners.spotlight: Improved with each of his three runs on the AW, winning an 8.6f novice at Wolverhampton in January; 16-1 when a remote last of six at Epsom on last month's handicap debut; step up in trip needs to have a positive effect.
runners.comment: 8.6f AW winner; well beaten on last month's handicap debut; up in trip with point to prove

runners.name: Tabasko
runners.spotlight: Similar form in four runs as a 2yo, tapped for speed over 9.4f on her nursery debut at Wolverhampton when last seen in November; capable of better this year but she is returning in a useful contest.
runners.comment: Similar form in four 2yo runs, beaten 2.5l in AW nursery (9.5f) when last seen; opposable

runners.name: Battosai
runners.spotlight: Failed to improve for the switch to handicaps when fifth of eight, beaten 12l, in a 1m2f event at Newmarket last week; down in class with blinkers fitted but he needs a big personal best.
runners.comment: Well held on handicap debut last week; down in class with blinkers fitted but not solid

runners.name: Miss Pretty
runners.spotlight: Saw off 11 rivals when winning a 7f fillies' maiden on her debut last August; down the field in a 1m fillies' novice back at Kempton in October; absent since and although 1m2f should suit, she is not obviously well handicapped; will find easier races than this.
runners.comment: Two runs at Kempton at 2yrs, winning on debut before struggling under penalty; tough mark

runners.name: Celestarak
runners.spotlight: Only beat two rivals home in his three novice/maiden runs as a 2yo (all 1m); gelded since last seen; surprising if this stoutly-bred 3yo couldn't leave those efforts well behind now his stamina is being more fully tested.
runners.comment: Down the field in three 1m runs as a 2yo; bred to do much better over 1m2f and further

races.race_name: Saunton Sands Hotel Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Masterinthewoods
runners.spotlight: He had been knocking on the door prior to his 10.4f win at Haydock last summer; not so good since but he should be sharper with his reappearance behind him and he is only 2lb higher than for the win; unexposed at 1m4f and respected back on turf.
runners.comment: 10.4f win at Haydock last summer; sharper for reappearance and returning to turf a plus

runners.name: Siouxfonic
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner; won a 1m4f Wolverhampton maiden in December on his second Flat start; no improvement for handicapping on his last two starts (cheekpieces added latest); still has low mileage but improvement is required.
runners.comment: 1m4f AW win in December; unplaced in two handicap runs this spring; needs more to win

runners.name: Nap Hand
runners.spotlight: Three Flat wins at up to 1m2f; in the frame twice over hurdles since last seen on the Flat; drops into a 0-80 and should stay 1m4f; not discounted.
runners.comment: Three Flat wins at up to 1m2f; fair form over hurdles since latest Flat run; down in grade

runners.name: Too Farhh Gone
runners.spotlight: Didn't have to improve much on her earlier efforts to win a 1m3f novice at Southwell last month; has low mileage but she will need a good step forward if she is to make a successful handicap debut.
runners.comment: Dug deep to win a Southwell novice last month (1m3f); faces tougher task now handicapping

runners.name: One Million Dreams
runners.spotlight: 28-1 and well beaten on his stable debut at Newbury eight days ago (1m4f, good); not easily recommended on the back of that.
runners.comment: 28-1 and well beaten on recent stable debut; not easily recommended after that

runners.name: Shayhana
runners.spotlight: Chased home a useful sort in a three-runner maiden a Lingfield on her debut in November; went one better when beating an errant rival (who has won since) at Wolverhampton last month, the pair pulling 15l clear; this unexposed and well-related daughter of Frankel moves into handicaps with the potential for even better.
runners.comment: Two solid efforts in AW maidens; well bred and moves into handicaps with lots of potential

runners.name: Trojan Storm
runners.spotlight: Won twice last year, notably a 1m6f handicap at Ascot in July off a 1lb lower mark; well held in five starts since December but this trip more suitable than those he has been tackling and the return of the tongue tie could help; betting should be revealing.
runners.comment: Back to a more suitable trip and the tongue tie returns; worth a market check

runners.name: Yaa Min
runners.spotlight: Two wins from four starts since joining Hayley Burton, both the victories coming over 1m4f on Lingfield's AW track; should prove just as effective back on turf and he is not fully exposed just yet.
runners.comment: On the up for new yard, winning twice at Lingfield (1m4f, AW); not fully exposed just yet

runners.name: Cinammon Coco
runners.spotlight: Flat/hurdle winner for Joseph O'Brien; pulled up in two hurdle runs for his new stable and hard to know what to expect now back on the Flat.
runners.comment: Flat/hurdle winner for Joseph O'Brien; pulled up in two hurdle runs for new yard; risky

runners.name: First Officer
runners.spotlight: Six-time winner but only one of those came in a double-figure field (October 2022); ran well at Newmarket when last seen eight months ago; has gone well fresh in the past; others appeal more all the same.
runners.comment: Ran well off this mark when last seen but he's done most of his winning in small fields

races.race_name: Trev's Final Furlong Handicap

runners.name: Moonhall Lass
runners.spotlight: 0-22 in Ireland but form figures of 311 since joining James Owen and 2-2 since returned to turf back in cheekpieces; an 8lb rise will make life tougher but she's shortlisted having been taken out on account of rain-softened ground last weekend.
runners.comment: 2-2 since returned to turf in cheekpieces; has 8lb rise but is shortlisted

runners.name: Rye
runners.spotlight: Regressive, if consistent, maiden who's blown plenty of chances in less competitive handicaps; just behind Elvetham last time (first-time cheekpieces; off tonight) and holds similar claims to that one back on turf.
runners.comment: Regressive, if consistent, maiden who's blown plenty of chances; each-way claims

runners.name: Alashos
runners.spotlight: Ended a lengthy losing run when winning a 0-55 AW handicap run to suit round Lingfield last time; upped 6lb but was competitive off higher marks on turf last summer and that win may have boosted confidence.
runners.comment: Ended a lengthy losing run when winning a 0-55 AW handicap latest; up 6lb, this is tougher

runners.name: Just Jump
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish gelding who's got into the habit of fluffing the start for this yard, which also saddles Rye; can pull hard too and comes with risks, returned to turf and going without the hood tonight.
runners.comment: Into the habit of fluffing the start for this yard; can pull hard too and is risky

runners.name: Insuspense
runners.spotlight: Consistent maiden who's had plenty of chances in handicaps and often been turned over at short prices; won't mind being back on turf and tonight's big field should suit her come-from-behind style, though, and she looks set to go well again.
runners.comment: Consistent maiden; tonight's big field should suit her come-from-behind style; player

runners.name: Elvetham
runners.spotlight: Got it together since dropped to this trip on the AW this year, winning a couple of 0-55s on Polytrack, latterly off 5lb lower in first-time blinkers; returns to turf 7lb higher than when last seen on it and will need a career-best effort to take this.
runners.comment: Got it together since dropped to this trip on the AW this year; will need a career-best

runners.name: Roman Spring
runners.spotlight: Up in the weights following three wins in the second half of last year; not at his best in a couple of starts back off 3lb higher and might need more help yet from the handicapper.
runners.comment: Not at his best in a couple of starts back; might need more help yet from the handicapper

runners.name: Sub Thirteen
runners.spotlight: Wins have come on good or softer but he ran fairly well on good to firm when second to Moonhall Lass at Salisbury three weeks ago; 9lb better off tonight but the winner had a good bit in hand and this one could have done without the summer weather arriving.
runners.comment: 2nd to Moonhall Lass three weeks ago; 9lb better off tonight; rain would have been ideal

runners.name: Liosa
runners.spotlight: Trainer off the mark in the week with another who was formerly under the charge of Stuart Kittow; this three-time AW winner made it 0-10 on turf when some way off the front three on comeback (won first time out last season); needs a deal more.
runners.comment: Made it 0-10 on turf when some way off the front three on comeback; needs a deal more

runners.name: Distant Rumble
runners.spotlight: C&D winner last spring off a 4lb higher mark who finished a clear second on his return to turf nine days ago; 2lb higher now but having Jack Callan (1-11 for the yard) up to take off 3lb won't hurt; one to consider.
runners.comment: C&D winner last spring off a 4lb higher mark; one to consider at this lower level

runners.name: Danger Alert
runners.spotlight: Still consistent but hasn't won in three years and isn't as good now; just a fair comeback effort over 5f here 12 days ago in second-time cheekpieces (has often worn a visor) but won't mind going back up in trip now he's had a run.
runners.comment: Still consistent but hasn't won in three years and isn't as good now; each-way shout

runners.name: Monsieur Patat
runners.spotlight: Won this last year under Finley Marsh off a 4lb lower mark and is currently 3-6 over C&D having gone in again in August; a pleasing comeback third on the AW (7f) will have set him up for a repeat bid.
runners.comment: Won this last year under Finley Marsh off a 4lb lower mark; fair chance of repeating

runners.name: Clasina
runners.spotlight: Finally off the mark on her debut for this yard when last seen nine months ago; fair effort first time out last season gives hope but a 7lb rise looks plenty and she's done most of her racing over 7f, so looks to have a bit to overcome.
runners.comment: Off the mark on debut for this yard when last seen nine months ago; 7lb rise looks plenty

runners.name: Outer Edge
runners.spotlight: Well held first time out in his three previous seasons; resumes on a winning mark but this race looks too competitive after his seven months off.
runners.comment: Well held first time out in his three previous seasons; this race looks too competitive
runners.owner: Int Plywood (lmporters) Ltd And C Mason

runners.name: Capote's Dream
runners.spotlight: Dry since a C&D win in 2023 and has looked on the downgrade in recent times; goes back to cheekpieces from blinkers, minus the tongue-tie again, after four months off; outsider.
runners.comment: Dry since a C&D win in 2023 and has looked on the downgrade in recent times

runners.name: Riot
runners.spotlight: Now 11lb lower than when successful for David O'Meara last autumn but has lost his way; it's a long time since he won on turf and even longer since he ran in a sprint, so he is hard to fancy back from a break.
runners.comment: Long time since he won on turf and even longer since he ran in a sprint; hard to fancy

races.race_name: Bodhi Solutions - Exceeding Expectations In Construction EBF Restricted Maiden (GBB) (Div I)

runners.name: King Of Montenegro
runners.spotlight: Nothing exciting on his debut and went backwards from it at Ascot a fortnight ago; yard ended a dry run recently but 0-5 with 2yos this spring and he needs a deal more.
runners.comment: Nothing exciting on his debut and went backwards from it at Ascot; needs a deal more

runners.name: Greek Street
runners.spotlight: Well below market billing when a remote last of five on his turf debut early last month; bettered that upped to this trip at a big price on Polytrack next time but will need another forward step.
runners.comment: Bettered debut effort at a big price on Polytrack latest; will need another forward step
runners.owner: C Booth, P Boyle, S Coleby & S Smith

runners.name: Harriet Vane
runners.spotlight: Late-foaled 4,500gns yearling who's a half-sister to three 2yo winners, one of debut; trainer (first juvenile runner of the year) is 0-53 with 2yos in recent seasons, with just five placed.
runners.comment: Trainer (first juvenile runner of the year) is 0-53 with 2yos in recent seasons

runners.name: Beach Ahead
runners.spotlight: Early-foaled 13,000gns yearling who's a brother to useful French sprinter Antonella and a half-brother to five winners, including on 2yo debut; yard struggling for winners of late and just 1-59 with 2yos in recent seasons; tongue-tie straight on.
runners.comment: 13,000gns yearling; yard struggling for winners and just 1-59 with 2yos in recent seasons

runners.name: Woodhay Wayfarer
runners.spotlight: May-foaled 27,000gns yearling who's a half-sister to two winners and holds a couple of sales race entries later on; stable form might be better.
runners.comment: May-foaled 27,000gns yearling; half-sister to two winners; couple of sales race entries

runners.name: It's A Dream
runners.spotlight: April-foaled 7,000gns yearling who's the fourth foal of a French 1m winner; her yard's first 2yo runner of the year (2-44 with them here all told) and the market will guide.
runners.comment: April-foaled 7,000gns yearling; yard's first 2yo runner of the year; the market will guide
runners.owner: P J & Mrs J P Haycock

runners.name: Lord Ragnar
runners.spotlight: 11,000euros yearling whose dam won over middle distances; stable going well of late but still to get rolling with 2yos and he may want more of a test in due course.
runners.comment: 11,000euros yearling whose dam won over middle distances; may want more of a test
runners.owner: Sarah Fern, Patricia James And Partner

runners.name: Printing Money
runners.spotlight: Trainer going great guns now after a quieter spell and is 2-4 with 2yos here; this 50,000gns yearling, who's a half-brother to a 1m winner in Norway out of a 5f 2yo winner for Clive Cox (second time out), holds a few notable entries in sales races later on.
runners.comment: 50,000gns yearling; out of a 5f 2yo winner for Clive Cox; a few notable entries later on
runners.owner: Hold My Beer, N Bradley 6, L Bearman

runners.name: Empire Rising
runners.spotlight: 45,000euros foal, 70,000euros yearling who holds a Super Sprint entry in July; turned two only a few weeks ago and the market will be the best guide.
runners.comment: 70,000euros yearling; turned two only a few weeks ago; the market will be the best guide

runners.name: Agitator
runners.spotlight: Yard still to get on the board for the year and this 30,000gns yearling, who holds a couple of sales race entries, didn't offer a lot on his Newmarket debut last weekend; this race is easier, though, and he's entitled to improve.
runners.comment: Didn't offer a lot on his Newmarket debut last weekend; this race is easier though

runners.name: Emerald Bay
runners.spotlight: Gelded 42,000euros yearling whose yard has enjoyed a decent spring with 2yos; always caught wide from stall 13 on his Wolverhampton debut recently and should fare better with that under his belt.
runners.comment: Always caught wide from stall 13 on his Wolverhampton debut and should fare better

runners.name: Time Saxon Warrior
runners.spotlight: Yard (among the winners of late) has a much better record with 2yos on the AW than turf; 2-1 for a small-field Bath maiden on his debut ten days ago but blew the start and ran green; clearly thought capable of better.
runners.comment: 2-1 for debut but blew the start and ran green; clearly thought capable of better

races.race_name: Bodhi Solutions - Exceeding Expectations In Construction EBF Restricted Maiden (GBB) (Div II)

runners.name: Sweet Devastation
runners.spotlight: April foal by Minzaal; 50,000euros yearling; 33-1 and she got the message late on when beaten just over 2l at Nottingham (6f, good) a fortnight ago; likely improver.
runners.comment: Got the message late on when beaten just over 2l on debut a fortnight ago; likely improver
runners.owner: C Wright, D Kilburn & Mrs C Corbett

runners.name: Patronise
runners.spotlight: April foal by Dark Angel; 45,000euros yearling; fifth foal; half-brother to winners Kolachi (1m 2yo; RPR 94), The Thunderer (1m2f-1m4f including AW; 91) and Sage Advice (1m2f Flat, 84; 2m hurdle); dam Listed-placed 7f 2yo winner (102); respected newcomer from leading yard.
runners.comment: April foal by Dark Angel; 45,000euros yearling; respected newcomer from leading yard

runners.name: Pride's Queen
runners.spotlight: April foal by Territories; 30,000gns yearling; second foal; dam French 1m winner (RPR 74), half-sister to German 1m Group 2 winner Peace At Last, out of maiden half-sister to 1m Group 1 winner Fly To The Stars; market informative ahead of this first go.
runners.comment: April foal by Territories; 30,000gns yearling; market informative ahead of this first go

runners.name: Sparkling Jem
runners.spotlight: April 28,000euros foal by Calyx; 14,000gns yearling; fourth foal; dam 7f AW 2yo and 1m2f turf winner (RPR 87), sister to useful 6f-1m winner Alba Power, half-sister to 6f 2yo Listed winner Ventura Diamond; first 2yo runner of the year from a yard not noted for successful starts with its juveniles.
runners.comment: April 28,000euros foal by Calyx; 14,000gns yearling; yard not noted for winning newcomers

runners.name: Perigord Noir
runners.spotlight: March foal by Invincible Army; 45,000gns yearling; 33-1 when third of nine at Kempton (6f, AW) 19 days ago; that was a solid first stab and he can probably do better.
runners.comment: March foal; 33-1 when solid third (6f, AW) 19 days ago; can probably do better

runners.name: True Charm
runners.spotlight: March foal by Ardad; £15,000 yearling, 25,000gns breeze-up 2yo; seventh foal; brother to Listed-placed 5f winner Ardad's Great (RPR 89), half-brother to 5f winner Rotokura Belle (including 2yo/AW; 70); dam 6f winner (71) out of maiden half-sister to 7f 2yo Group 3 winner Ugo Fire; requires a market check.
runners.comment: March foal by Ardad; £15,000 yearling, 25,000gns breeze-up 2yo; requires a market check

runners.name: Satellite Of Love
runners.spotlight: April foal by Minzaal, £45,000 yearling; fifth foal; half-brother to winners Jersey Rocs (1m/8.6f AW; RPR 73) and Bride Tree (7f AW; 67); dam 6f winner (including 2yo; 94), sister to Group-placed 5f/6f 2yo winner Rosebride, out of 6f/7f 2yo winner; stable always to be respected in these events and market should provide a good clue ahead of this one's debut.
runners.comment: April foal by Minzaal, £45,000 yearling; yard respected and market can provide a good clue
runners.owner: Middleham Park Racing Xvii & M Parker

runners.name: Ventura Power
runners.spotlight: Late March 18,000gns foal, 17,000gns yearling and £28,000 breeze-up 2yo; eleventh foal; Perfect Power half-brother to five winners including Rogue (6f-1m1f including 2yo; RPR 96), Zennor Storm (7f/1m AW 2yo; 85) and The Ferret (1m AW 2yo; 72); dam 1m2f winner (51); respected yard and the betting should reveal more.
runners.comment: Late March 18,000gns foal, 17,000gns yearling and £28,000 breeze-up 2yo; heed the betting

runners.name: Harry's Pop
runners.spotlight: Early April foal; 32,000gns yearling by Harry Angel; third foal; dam 5f winner (RPR 56), closely related to 5f-6f winner Jaganory (including 2yo), half-sister to 6f 2yo/7f winner Mr Kiki, out of unraced sister to 6f Group 1 winner Fayr Jag; speedy pedigree and yard is going well; watch the market.
runners.comment: Early April foal; 32,000gns yearling by Harry Angel; speedy pedigree and yard going well
runners.owner: K Shenton, N Bradley 6 & D Cunha

runners.name: One In A Millz
runners.spotlight: Bungle Inthejungle March foal; 60,000euros yearling; seventh foal out of unraced dam; brother to French/Italian 6f/6.5f winner Genie In A Bottle (including 2yo/AW), half-brother to winners Diamond Dougal (5f-1m including 2yo/AW/Italy; RPR 96) and Brand New Day (French Listed-placed 4.5f/5f 2yo; 89); the most expensive of these at auction and the betting may guide on his introduction.
runners.comment: Bungle Inthejungle March foal; 60,000euros yearling; the biggest price at auction

runners.name: Rex Regum
runners.spotlight: February foal by Space Blues; 55,000gns yearling; third foal; closely related to 6f 2yo July debut winner Distant Shore (RPR 76), half-brother to 6f 2yo/1m winner Fondo Blanco (107); dam Group-placed 7f winner (including AW; 99), out of unraced half-sister to 1m2f Group 1 winner Izzi Top; first 2yo runner of the season for his yard and he holds a foaling date advantage over this field.
runners.comment: February foal by Space Blues; 55,000gns yearling; has foaling date advantage on the field

runners.name: My Normandie
runners.spotlight: 15,500gns late April foal, 20,000euros yearling; by Ardad; 22-1 and no real impact when seventh of nine at Leicester (5f, good to firm) on her debut a fortnight ago; goes up in trip for this second start.
runners.comment: 22-1 and no real impact (5f, good to firm) on her debut a fortnight ago; goes up in trip
runners.owner: Gerard A Normand, Anna & Moa Sundstrom

races.race_name: David And Debbie Married At Last Handicap

runners.name: Norfolk Blue
runners.spotlight: Didn't progress last season and was gelded over the winter; just a fair effort in a less-competitive Yarmouth handicap on his comeback and first-time cheekpieces (wore a hood twice as a 2yo) will need to make a difference.
runners.comment: Didn't progress last season; first-time cheekpieces will need to make a difference

runners.name: Lux Aeterna
runners.spotlight: Found a weak Wolverhampton handicap when winning off 5lb lower on his comeback (9.5f), having been gelded; inclined to pull hard on his turf debut last time and drops in trip tonight for a yard among the winners.
runners.comment: Inclined to pull hard on turf debut; drops in trip tonight for a yard among the winners

runners.name: Legacy Rock
runners.spotlight: Always well positioned when winning a messy C&D handicap off 3lb lower early in the month (Eyes Front fifth); showed the right attitude that day but the effort's been let down by the placed horses since and he'll need more again.
runners.comment: Won a messy C&D handicap off 3lb lower early in the month; needs more if he's to follow up

runners.name: Royal Bodyguard
runners.spotlight: Consistent once handicapping last season before finding 1m2f beyond him latterly; entitled to have come on for his recent comeback, having been gelded, and this is easier but he's vulnerable to improvers now.
