runners.comment: Entitled to come on for his comeback and this is easier but he's vulnerable to improvers

runners.name: Galba
runners.spotlight: Inclined to pull hard on comeback and was said to have hung right last time, both round undulating courses (7f); has shown enough ability to suggest he can be a factor now handicapping at a sensible level though.
runners.comment: Shown enough ability to suggest he can be a factor now handicapping at a sensible level

runners.name: Eyes Front
runners.spotlight: Regressive, exposed maiden who wasn't best suited by the way things unfolded behind Legacy Rock over C&D last time; 5lb better off with the winner, which gives him claims, but he didn't conclusively prove his stamina that day.
runners.comment: Not best suited by the way things unfolded behind Legacy Rock last time; 5lb better off

runners.name: Miss Starlet
runners.spotlight: Yard been quiet so far this year and she finished well held on her AW comeback; dam stayed middle-distances, so perhaps the longer trip will help, but she's not easily recommended.
runners.comment: Yard been quiet so far this year and she finished well held on her AW comeback

runners.name: Thomas Picton
runners.spotlight: Beaten a long way in three runs at about this trip this spring; related to winners and this is more realistic now handicapping in first-time headgear, but would want to see some support before considering him.
runners.comment: This more realistic now handicapping in headgear, but would want to see some support

runners.name: Blue Jammin
runners.spotlight: Found a weak soft-ground Chepstow nursery when scrambling home off 3lb lower on his final run last season; different conditions tonight as he returns from nine months off.
runners.comment: Found a weak soft-ground Chepstow nursery latterly; returns from nine months off

runners.name: Shadowmere
runners.spotlight: Big prices for three runs last backend, two on soft ground (7f-1m1f), pulling hard laterly; hard to know what he might be capable of as yet and the market will be the best guide back from seven months off.
runners.comment: Hard to know what he might be capable of as yet and the market will be the best guide

races.race_name: Bodhi Solutions - Constructing Champions Handicap

runners.name: Sargent Dennis
runners.spotlight: Chased home Marengo Star at Kempton (6f, AW) on penultimate start and it was a good step forward when doing well (carried left) to beat 11 others in C&D novice (good to firm) 12 days ago; that was his first go on turf and there could be better to come on this handicap debut.
runners.comment: Good step forward to win novice (good to firm) recently; respected on this handicap debut

runners.name: Powder Monkey
runners.spotlight: Gelded after finishing fifth of eight on his turf debut over 7f at Newmarket in April; opening mark looks reasonable and this experiment at sprinting is well worth a go too; interesting.
runners.comment: Opening mark looks reasonable and this experiment at sprinting is worth a go; interesting

runners.name: Comic Strip
runners.spotlight: Dual nursery (5f, AW) winner who was outpaced when a little short of room on his turf debut at Bath last month; this step back up to 6f should help.
runners.comment: Outpaced when a little short of room on turf debut latest; return to 6f should help

runners.name: Alkuwarrior
runners.spotlight: Changed stables last summer and it was an inauspicious start for this yard when last of nine in course nursery (good to firm) in August; subsequently gelded but again trailed the field at Bath (also 5f, firm) on his reappearance last month; plenty to prove on his first go at 6f.
runners.comment: Finished last on both starts for this yard and has plenty to prove on his first go at 6f

runners.name: Grey Horizon
runners.spotlight: Enjoyed a fine spell on AW during the winter, winning four consecutive handicaps at 6f across January and February; good effort when second in Class 2 at Lingfield (AW; 50-1) on his next start at that trip but hasn't been at his best over shorter in two turf handicaps this spring; still not totally ruled out back at 6f.
runners.comment: Fine AW winter spell at 6f but not at best over shorter in two turf handicaps this spring

runners.name: Ecclefechan
runners.spotlight: 0-10 but he's gone close on a couple of occasions, including when beaten a neck in Lingfield nursery (6f, AW) in November; favourite but only fourth of eight behind Akabusi at Newmarket (6f, good) last Friday; needs more.
runners.comment: Favourite when fourth to Akabusi at Newmarket (6f, good) last Friday; needs more

runners.name: Kesta
runners.spotlight: Busy spell as a juvenile included wins in Brighton maiden (5.5f) and course nursery (5f, again good to firm); also stays 6f and landed series final at Nottingham (good to soft) in October; more exposed than most of these and finished last on his reappearance, however; drops back down in grade.
runners.comment: More exposed than most of these and finished last on his reappearance

runners.name: Marengo Storm
runners.spotlight: Took a good step forward from encouraging debut when comfortably landing Kempton maiden (6f, AW) 19 days ago; runner-up (Sargent Dennis) scored next time out and this one brings lots of potential to his handicap debut; first go on turf.
runners.comment: Good win latest and brings lots of potential to his h'cap debut; first go on turf; player

runners.name: Generous Rascal
runners.spotlight: Off the mark on his second start when taking Kempton novice (AW) for Oliver Cole in October; gelded ahead of his stable debut when third of 13 in Ripon handicap (also 6f, good to soft) last month on his return from a break; pedigree offers hope he can do better this term and his yard's going very nicely.
runners.comment: Won 6f AW novice for Oliver Cole; encouraging handicap third on stable debut last month

runners.name: Hayynah
runners.spotlight: Went very close in C&D novice (good to firm) in July and narrowly picked up Southwell novice (5f, AW) in February; disappointing last of seven on handicap debut over latter C&D next time, however.
runners.comment: Went close in C&D novice in July; disappointed when last on h'cap debut (5f, AW) latest

runners.name: Firewalker
runners.spotlight: Group-class pedigree; recorded the best of three qualifying runs when odds-on second on his introduction at Southwell (6f, AW) in December; makes handicap debut with his two turf runs this spring needing to be improved upon but that's not out of the question.
runners.comment: Two runs this spring need improving on but that's not out of the question on h'cap debut

runners.name: Up The Agenda
runners.spotlight: 7f nursery winner who subsequently improved dropped back to 6f on AW, winning three handicaps including when he got up late at Kempton in January; unexposed on turf and could be the sort to resume his progress now back from a break.
runners.comment: Improved at 6f on AW; unexposed on turf; could resume his progress now back from a break

runners.name: Fresh Fade
runners.spotlight: Couldn't make the most of Lingfield's golden highway when weakening over 7f on his stable debut (50-1) a fortnight ago; drop back to 6f probably in his favour but others hold more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Weakened over 7f on stable debut (50-1) a fortnight ago; drop back to 6f might help

runners.name: Akabusi
runners.spotlight: He's seen plenty of action and was with his third trainer when bagging Newmarket handicap (6f, good) last Friday; return to this career-high mark demands more.
runners.comment: Won handicap (6f, good) last Friday; return to this career-high mark demands more

runners.name: Romantic Twilight
runners.spotlight: Made all in Yarmouth nursery (6f, good; handicap debut) in October but raced freely before weakening over 7f nine days later; that's easily forgiven and she's not ruled out on this return.
runners.comment: Made all in nursery (6f, good) in October; excuse next time; not ruled out on this return

runners.name: Bintola
runners.spotlight: Hooded all three starts last term; best effort when second at Kempton (6f, AW) in September; opening mark looks a little punchy ahead of her handicap debut/reappearance but pedigree offers plenty of hope she'll do better this season.
runners.comment: Opening mark looks a little punchy but pedigree offers hope she can do better

races.race_name: Chloe's Still A Filly At Forty Handicap

runners.name: Night Bear
runners.spotlight: Four-time turf winner who last scored at Brighton (1m4f, good to firm; has won at this trip) two years ago; went close on two occasions at 1m3f (visored) during latest AW campaign but his latest effort when last of ten at Southwell (1m4f; raced freely) wasn't an ideal springboard for this switch back to grass; blinkers (has won in them before) go back on.
runners.comment: Latest AW last of ten wasn't ideal springboard for switch back to turf; blinkers back on

runners.name: Scenario
runners.spotlight: Got up on the line to pip Naughty Niall at Wolverhampton (AW, 9.5f) in January and it was a second win on turf when beating 13 others at Bath (1m, good) in April; excuses on next two starts and she's respected on her first go at 1m2f.
runners.comment: Second win on turf when scoring at Bath (1m, good) in April; respected on first go at 1m2f

runners.name: Monks Mead
runners.spotlight: Turf winner who was in good nick on AW during the winter and he scraped home to secure Lingfield classified (1m2f) in January; another solid effort when runner-up in similar event last time and he's respected back on grass after two-month break.
runners.comment: Turf winner who was in good nick on AW during the winter; respected back on grass

runners.name: Twilight Guest
runners.spotlight: Fired off a hat-trick at this trip in cheekepices last May/June but he's been well held on last five starts, including when making his comeback at Yarmouth last month; visor (unplaced previously) is tried for a third time.
runners.comment: Fired off a 1m2f hat-trick last May/June but well held last five starts; visor tried again

runners.name: Pride Of Nepal
runners.spotlight: Five turf wins were all secured in a tongue-tie, with additional cheekpeices on last three occasions; has never won on AW and after latest campaign on that surface he now returns to grass with good handicapping case to be made; no headgear looks a negative, though.
runners.comment: Five turf wins in tongue-tie/cheekpeices; good handicapping case but no headgear this time

runners.name: Guinness Lad
runners.spotlight: Slowly into stride but still finished second to subsequent prolific winner in similar event over C&D around this time last year; blew the start again when beaten a head at Leicester (1m2f, also good to firm) three weeks later; still not perfected the art of starting but mark is reduced after AW spell and he's dangerous back on turf if ready after a break.
runners.comment: Second over C&D and at Leicester (1m2f, also good to firm) last May; dangerous if ready

runners.name: Le Pelerin
runners.spotlight: Three third-placed finishes on quick turf (1m) and AW (9.5f) last term but finished the year tamely and wind surgery didn't improve matters in two starts last month; tongue-tie added.
runners.comment: Finished 2025 tamely and this year has started similarly; tongue-tie added

runners.name: Naughty Niall
runners.spotlight: Good run of turf form last summer included when making all at Yarmouth (1m2f, good to soft; acts on quicker) in July; mostly creditable efforts on AW since winning at Wolverhampton (1m4f) in November and he's far from ruled out back on grass despite tricky stall.
runners.comment: Good run of turf form last summer; mostly creditable efforts on AW since; not ruled out

runners.name: Judge Frank
runners.spotlight: Went back-to-back in good-ground handicaps (7f/1m, including here) in September 2024 but no wins since and two runs last month (led before weakening) don't suggest another crack at this trip will turn the ship around.
runners.comment: No wins since September 2024 and doesn't appear to be crying out for another go at 1m2f

runners.name: Majestic Leo
runners.spotlight: Latest effort when eighth of 11 here (11.5f, good to firm) was comfortably his best for this yard but that still leaves him with work to do.
runners.comment: Latest eighth here (11.5f, good to firm) was a best for this yard; still has work to do

runners.name: Liberty Bird
runners.spotlight: Nicely treated on some of last year's form for Charlie Johnston (consistency was an issue) but didn't shine in subsequent short spells with Alice Haynes and Lemos De Souza; never figured when ninth at Lingfield (1m, AW; stays further) on her debut for this yard last month.
runners.comment: Nicely treated on some of last year's form but seems to have lost her way

runners.name: Read All About It
runners.spotlight: She has developed a particularly rocky profile but would hold good claims on any of her first three starts in maidens and a novice last term; three poor efforts in handicaps followed but her penultimate run when third (9.5f, AW; possibly flattered) in first-time cheekpieces was a bright spot; tailed off over C&D last time, however, and the visor is now given a whirl.
runners.comment: Rocky profile these days and tailed off over C&D last time; visor is now given a whirl

runners.name: Port Noir
runners.spotlight: Hardy mare who landed four races on AW (1m to 9.5f) between February and April; also a previous four-time winner on turf but this is just her second crack at 1m2f on her 111th start; still not discounted.
runners.comment: Won four AW races (1m to 9.5f) this year; having second crack at 1m2f on her 111th start

runners.name: Masqool
runners.spotlight: Very respectable 12-76 record for one who plies his trade in this sort of grade; vast majority of those wins came on AW but he can do it on turf and latest third over C&D (good to firm), while beaten a fair way, wasn't a bad effort; has dropped back down to his career-low mark.
runners.comment: Effective on turf and latest C&D third wasn't a bad effort; back on career-low mark

runners.name: Galactic Glow
runners.spotlight: Bath/fast ground specialist who wasn't too far away from landing a fifth win at that track when runner-up (1m2f) last Wednesday on his return from a break; effective elsewhere too and should go well.
runners.comment: Bath/firm ground specialist who was second there (1m2f) last Wednesday; should go well

runners.name: Fighting Queen
runners.spotlight: Made a winning turf/handicap debut at Bath (1m) last May and posted a creditable second upped to 1m2f at that track (also firm) nine days later; didn't match that form subsequently but last year was this 5yo's first season racing so chance she might have a shade more to offer this term.
runners.comment: Winning turf/h'cap debut (1m) last May; 1m2f second next time; might do better this term

races.race_name: Bodhi Solutions - Facilities Management Front Runner Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: Lady Dora Mae
runners.spotlight: Good progress since joining this yard includes Southwell novice win (1m) in January and successful handicap debut at Kempton (7f, also AW) in March; just her second turf outing when solid second at Musselburgh (1m1f, good) four weeks ago and highly likely to be competitive again.
runners.comment: Solid second (1m1f, good) four weeks ago and highly likely to be competitive again

runners.name: Valkyrie Storm
runners.spotlight: Made the breakthrough on handicap debut/reappearance when taking Nottingham event (8.5f) in April and a 3lb nudge didn't stop her going in again over that C&D (also good) a fortnight ago; raised a further 4lb but that latest success was a cosy one and she's on the up.
runners.comment: 2-2 in h'caps this term; raised a further 4lb but last time was tidy and she's on the up

runners.name: Proposal
runners.spotlight: Possibly still a touch raw but she knuckled down well when landing Doncaster maiden (7f, good to firm) on her return last month; pedigree suggests she'll kick on again at this trip and beyond; big player on handicap debut for leading connections.
runners.comment: 7f maiden win on return; pedigree says new trip is a positive; big player on h'cap debut

runners.name: Constant Star
runners.spotlight: Unraced at two; promising debut when runner-up at Lingfield (1m) in March then won going away dropped to 7f at Wolverhampton (also AW; flashed tail for pressure) one month later; another interesting handicap debutante and cheekpieces go on for this first taste of turf.
runners.comment: Unraced at two; won second start (7f, AW) latest; cheekpieces on for h'cap/turf debut

runners.name: Crystal Pier
runners.spotlight: Tapped back into initial promise when opening her account at Wolverhampton (8.5f, AW) on her comeback 12 days ago; received a 2lb nudge for that narrow success and, despite shaping like she'll do better returned to further, warrants consideration in her attempt to double up.
runners.comment: Off the mark (8.5f, AW) on her comeback 12 days ago; 2lb nudge and she's considered

runners.name: Naana's Crystal
runners.spotlight: Moderate debut (turf) for previous yard last year but strongly supported in two AW novices for this stable in February, finishing runner-up at Kempton before scoring at Southwell (also 1m) one week later; third of four on handicap debut at Wolverhampton (9.5f) was a little underwhelming last month and now sports a tongue-tie for her second go on grass.
runners.comment: Won 1m AW novice in February; underwhelming third of four on h'cap debut; tongue-tie on

runners.name: Summer In Paris
runners.spotlight: Matched previous best when tongue tied and fourth in Newmarket maiden (1m, good; form upheld) on her reappearance in April; brings potential to this handicap debut.
runners.comment: Matched previous best on her reappearance in April; brings potential to handicap debut

runners.name: Come On Eibhlin
runners.spotlight: Cracking debut when 250-1 but only beaten 4l in Group 2 Queen Mary (5f) last June hasn't been built on, despite winning 6f course novice (good) next time; edging down the weights, however, and might be finding her level having not been disgraced at Wetherby last month; makes first start at 1m.
runners.comment: Edging down the weights and might be finding her level; makes first start at 1m
runners.owner: Hold My Beer, Gee Gees Racing & N Clark

runners.name: Jakarta
runners.spotlight: Dual winning juvenile (maiden/nursery) who was off for 324 days before showing patchy form in three starts from September last year, just failing in Newmarket handicap (1m, good) but lesser shows either side of that; encouraging this year's campaign is starting earlier but reappears as the sole elder filly against some unexposed 3yos.
runners.comment: Patchy form after delayed start to last term; returns as 4yo against mostly unexposed 3yos

races.race_name: Bodhi Solutions - Commercial Fit-Out Challenge Handicap

runners.name: Keep It Classic
runners.spotlight: Well below market expectations when some way behind Darkest Red at Yarmouth; much better a fortnight ago, when narrowly beaten despite over-racing in a small field; only 1lb higher and is shortlisted, going without the cheekpieces this time.
runners.comment: Much better a fortnight ago, when narrowly beaten; only 1lb higher and is shortlisted

runners.name: Worlington
runners.spotlight: Much better on his handicap debut/comeback and simply bumped into one at a short price at Redcar (gone on to win three more); longer trip should be fine on pedigree and his claims are obvious.
runners.comment: Bumped into one at a short price at Redcar; longer trip should be fine on pedigree; player

runners.name: Pepper Fizz
runners.spotlight: Didn't progress last season and finished well held from the front on her comeback here (8.1f) early in the month; goes right up in trip (stamina on the dam's side) in first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Exposed now; goes right up in trip (stamina on the dam's side) in first-time headgear

runners.name: Darkest Red
runners.spotlight: Suited by the step up to 1m2f back on turf when one of three to come away off a 2lb lower mark at Yarmouth late last month (Keep It Classic behind); bit unlucky not to go closer still there and is on the shortlist.
runners.comment: Bit unlucky not to go closer latest (Keep It Classic behind) and is on the shortlist

runners.name: Adores
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to three winners, two of whom improved once handicapping; she may be about to follow suit on comeback evidence (1m), when splitting two subsequent winners, albeit she has stamina to prove on her turf debut.
runners.comment: Half-sister to three winners; worth a look but has stamina to prove on her turf debut

runners.name: Von Dutch
runners.spotlight: Easy to back ahead of his AW handicap debut early in the year, when a well-held fourth of five; not much better on his first crack at turf a month ago and needs more.
runners.comment: Not much better on his first crack at turf a month ago and needs more

runners.name: N'Ion
runners.spotlight: Massive prices for three runs this spring and beaten a long way each time; starts handicap life on a basement mark in first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Massive prices for three runs this spring and beaten a long way each time; headgear on

runners.name: Panthere Noir
runners.spotlight: Exposed, regressive maiden who's with his third yard already; well held on his first crack at 1m2f for his new trainer last time and switches back to cheekpieces tonight.
runners.comment: Exposed, regressive maiden who's with his third yard already; back to cheekpieces tonight

runners.name: Dream Of Ithaca
runners.spotlight: Just behind Von Dutch at Beverley before out of her depth behind Shirakawa back into novice company last time; would be a surprise winner.
runners.comment: Out of her depth behind Shirakawa last time; would be a surprise winner

runners.name: Darn Hot Dame
runners.spotlight: Solid handicap debut effort in February but that race hasn't worked out; ran to a similar level upped to 1m4f when last seen and needs to improve on her turf debut.
runners.comment: Solid handicap debut effort in February but that race hasn't worked out; needs more

runners.name: Rajbello
runners.spotlight: Blinkers on since handicapping this spring; had little chance from off the pace in a messy affair upped to 1m4f last time and that gave some hope as he switches to turf.
runners.comment: Blinkers on since handicapping this spring; little chance the way it unfolded last time

runners.name: Shirakawa
runners.spotlight: All three runs came on Polytrack at Kempton (1m-1m3f); little chance behind a useful winner last time and starts handicap life at a lowly level; should be capable of better.
runners.comment: Little chance behind a useful winner last time and starts handicap life at a lowly level

runners.name: Saxophonic
runners.spotlight: Step up to 1m4f should have suited on his debut for this yard last time but he ran poorly; that leaves him with something to prove on just his second turf start.
runners.comment: Poor last time; that leaves him with something to prove on just his second turf start

runners.name: Naranka
runners.spotlight: Well held in four starts last summer (6f-1m), latterly on his nursery/AW debut; would want to see some support this time back from such a layoff.
runners.comment: Well held in four starts last summer (6f-1m); would want to see some support

runners.name: Midnight Media
runners.spotlight: Not without hope a couple of times at huge prices this spring; stamina not assured on pedigree as she goes beyond an extended 1m for the first time on her turf/handicap debut, but this is more realistic.
runners.comment: Stamina not assured on pedigree but this is more realistic now handicapping on turf debut

runners.name: Action Reaction
runners.spotlight: Well held all starts on the AW, latterly when 66-1 outsider of six on her handicap debut; goes right up in trip on her handicap/turf debut, which needs to make a big difference.
runners.comment: Goes right up in trip on her handicap/turf debut, which needs to make a big difference

races.race_name: XXXX Doomben Cup (Group 1) (Turf)

runners.name: Pride Of Jenni
runners.spotlight: Outstanding racemare. Led and held sway to win at Gold Coast. Leader. Handles the sting out of the ground.
runners.owner: A Ottobre, Mrs L Ottobre & M Ottobre

runners.name: She's A Hustler
runners.spotlight: Was a valiant but vanquished third in the A.D. Hollindale Stakes at Gold Coast. Fitter for those two runs and ready to show best. Wet tracks certainly don't pose a problem. All the better and good enough to figure.
runners.owner: M & J Racing, T J Auld Et Al

runners.name: Half Yours
runners.spotlight: Trimmed margin late at Gold Coast second-up. Ready to peak third-up. Distance increase suits. Stripping fitter and could spring a surprise.
runners.owner: Kildalton Park Racing, J B Biasin Et Al

runners.name: Birdman
runners.spotlight: Just beaten in the A.D. Hollindale Stakes at Gold Coast. Key player.
runners.owner: A Kheir, N Greenhalgh Et Al

runners.name: Kovalica
runners.spotlight: Trimmed margin late at Gold Coast. Only chink in his armour has been heavy tracks. Facing another uphill task.
runners.owner: N Morgan & D H T Devine

runners.name: Asterix
runners.spotlight: Unhurried early before driving hard late to get the money at Gosford at second run back. Could give some cheek at odds.
runners.owner: D Kelt, M J Greatbatch Et Al

runners.name: Vauban
runners.spotlight: Let-up (56-days). Seasoned big-race specialist. Winner too strong but ran on for the minors at Rosehill in second run from spell. Since trialled at Randwick 4 May.
runners.owner: Australian Bloodstock, AB Vauban Et Al

races.race_name: Maiden Race (Turf)
Gold Medallion (Grade 1) (Turf)

runners.name: Status Symbol
runners.spotlight: Run out of it when 6/1 and 2nd of 6 runners, 1.40 lengths behind TIME IS PRECIOUS over 1100m at TURFFONTEIN STANDSIDE on 28 April 26. Improved again-well drawn.

runners.name: The Bronx Bull
runners.spotlight: Resp noise-fatigued when 33/10 and 4th of 6 runners, 5.60 lengths behind TIME IS PRECIOUS over 1100m at TURFFONTEIN STANDSIDE on 28 April 26. Shown ability, chance.
runners.owner: G G Palmer & Mrs D Palmer

runners.name: Better Never Ends
runners.spotlight: Challenged when 16/1 and 2nd of 9 runners, 1.40 lengths behind GIMME SOME LUCK over 1160m at TURFFONTEIN STANDSIDE on 10 May 26. Can be in the fight.

runners.name: Dear Doc
runners.spotlight: Not striding out when 11/2 and 6th of 7 runners, 10.20 lengths behind SHERIFF BILL BROWN over 1600m at VAAL CLASSIC on 14 May 26. More needed for the win.

runners.name: Time Is Precious
runners.spotlight: Awk st - going away when 7/1 and 1st of 6 runners, 1.40 lengths ahead TIME IS PRECIOUS over 1100m at TURFFONTEIN STANDSIDE on 28 April 26. Won 3 in a row - big say.
runners.owner: A Joannides, R M Sewgoolam Et Al

runners.name: Rich Man's World
runners.spotlight: Made it look easy! when 56/100 and 1st of 7 runners, 7.50 lengths ahead RICH MAN'S WORLD over 1100m at SCOTTSVILLE on 29 April 26. Signs of a top horse.
runners.owner: R L Gabler, K P Truter Et Al

runners.name: Kudikaran
runners.spotlight: Good pace-asserted when 13/10 and 1st of 9 runners, 0.40 lengths ahead KUDIKARAN over 1000m at GREYVILLE on 02 May 26. Decent form-task here.

runners.name: Haute Couture
runners.spotlight: Exercise gallop when 91/100 and 1st of 8 runners, 10.50 lengths ahead HAUTE COUTURE over 1000m at TURFFONTEIN STANDSIDE on 10 May 26. Thumped them on debut.

runners.name: Egremont
runners.spotlight: Flashed up late when 50/1 and 1st of 11 runners, 1.90 lengths ahead EGREMONT over 1200m at VAAL on 21 April 26. Easy debut winner.
runners.owner: Sean Tarry Racing (Pty) Ltd & J J Morris
